title: DISINFO: EU meddling in Central Asia aimed to undermine Russia and sap resources
countries_regions: EU, Europe, Russia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan, Tajikistan
tags: European Union Sphere of influence Central Asia Encircling Russia Destabilising Russia DISINFO: EU meddling in Central Asia aimed to undermine Russia and sap resources
summary: The EU’s power play in Central Asia is to divide, dominate and isolate it from Russia. Brussels has long been trying to pry Central Asia from Russia with backroom meddling and extravagant promises. The EU is preying on the USSR's legacy, imposing leverage through conditional ‘reforms’, targeting the youth and education, coopting regional leadership and pushing for resources and logistics. The goal is to undermine Russia and tap Central Asia’s resources. In exchange for lucrative projects, the EU is pressuring Central Asian governments to cut ties with Russia and impose sanctions — while eyeing the region’s vast resource wealth.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about the European Union and international cooperation as foreign interference . This disinformation story is a direct reaction to the celebration of the first EU-Central Asia summit in Samarkand on April 2025, aimed to enhance cooperation between both regions. The EU also readies an enhanced partnership agreement with Uzbekistan , following similar accords with Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan , and is negotiating another one with Tajikistan . These agreements are legitimate cooperative accords in areas such as trade and investment, research, education, environment, regional stability, risk reduction, the rule of law, human rights and civil society. However, Russia perceives these independent actions by sovereign Central Asian states as a threat to what it considers its ‘sphere of influence’ , aggressively targeting these international exchanges and trying to discredit them by baselessly portraying them as part of a plot against Russia. Through the manipulative use of terms such as “preying”, “targeting”, “coopting”, “push” or “undermine”, this disinformation story disingenuously distorts the character of this cooperation, framing it as an illicit attempt to exploit and control these countries through dark activities. In reality, Central Asian states are sovereign governments who voluntarily and freely agree to cooperate with the EU on behalf of their nations, which will benefit from it. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that the EU considers Central Asia only as a tool to extract resources and harm Russia , that the West is no longer hiding its intentions to detach Central Asia from Russia , that the EU is instigating protests among Georgia’s youth , that the EU mission in Armenia is a military intelligence operation , or that the EU wants to oust Russia from the Caucasus .

title: DISINFO: European leaders used cocaine and danced on the train to Kyiv
tags: Emmanuel Macron Friedrich Merz Maria Zakharova Drugs Keir Starmer DISINFO: European leaders used cocaine and danced on the train to Kyiv
summary: The leaders of France, Germany and the UK hid a line of cocaine and a pouch of drugs when the camera started to film them, they later undressed and danced while being high. This might be untrue but it is funny and shameful.
response: The claim is entirely unfounded and lacks any supporting evidence. Its primary aim appears to be discrediting political leaders and undermining public trust in international support for Ukraine. The allegations focused on claims that European leaders owned a bag of cocaine during their recent visit to Ukraine. These claims emerged after footage showed President Emmanuel Macron picking up a crumpled white object from a table while seated next to German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer during a late-night meeting in Kyiv. In response, the Elysée Palace firmly denied the allegation on its official X account, clarifying that the object was simply a tissue and highlighted: “When European unity becomes inconvenient, disinformation goes so far as to make a simple tissue look like drugs. This fake news is being spread by France’s enemies, both abroad and at home. We must remain vigilant against manipulation”. title: DISINFO: Putin’s proposal shows his greatness by giving the defeated a chance to negotiate
tags: peace negotiations putin Volodymyr Zelensky Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine War against Ukraine Ukraine Russian superiority Diplomacy with Russia DISINFO: Putin’s proposal shows his greatness by giving the defeated a chance to negotiate
summary: Russian president Vladimir Putin proposed to resume direct negotiations between Russia and Ukraine without preconditions. Putin’s initiative is a sign of strategic greatness because it is opening the game to all actors so, through dialogue, they can achieve peace. This opens an important opportunity, after having been boycotted in 2022. Russia is open for the defeated to sit at the negotiating table. However, we have an irrational actor like Zelenskyy, who is behaving as if he was winning the war.
response: This disinformation story is part of a wider campaign aiming to manipulate the information environment around Vladimir Putin’s call for direct negotiations between Russia and Ukraine in Istanbul in May 2025. This disinformation story aims to present Russia in a position of force ahead of the negotiation by promoting a recurring pro-Kremlin narrative about the inevitability of Russia’s victory , portraying it as magnanimous in face of a Ukrainian army that is on the verge of total defeat, something that it is far from true . While a shortage of military manpower remains a real difficulty for Ukraine, this also affects Russia . Since early 2025, massive losses have slowed down Russian advances in the Ukrainian frontlines, making Russia’s war effort unsustainable in the medium term . Allegations about the 2022 negotiations being boycotted by external actors have been debunked multiple times (see here , here , here and here ). By attacking Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy , it also tries to portray Russia as the real rational actor in the negotiations. While in practice Russia is blocking any real move that could advance actual peace in Ukraine, pro-Kremlin disinformation has systematically instrumentalised the narrative that Russia wants peace in Ukraine in contrast to the West, but, as evidenced by Russia's actions in Ukraine , such statements are merely empty PR stunts deployed to garner support for Russia's war of aggression in Ukraine. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Ukraine cannot dictate the peace terms because it lost the war , that the Nazi Zelenskyy regime is within months of falling , that UK and France are unable to accept defeat in Ukraine , or that Ukraine and Europe have lost in the Ukrainian conflict .

title: DISINFO: Zelensky will reject Putin’s peace proposal in order to stay alive
tags: peace negotiations Volodymyr Zelensky putin Nazi/Fascist Ukrainian statehood Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine War against Ukraine Ukraine DISINFO: Zelensky will reject Putin’s peace proposal in order to stay alive
summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin publicly proposed renewed peace talks with Ukraine to be held in Istanbul on May 15, accompanied by an expressed willingness to negotiate without preconditions. But Volodymyr Zelensky will reject Putin’s proposal in order to stay alive. Zelensky does not want to meet alone with Mr. Putin. If he decides to go ahead and negotiate something -and is dubious that he will be allowed to do that because of internal politics- then the Azov group and his military will rebel. Putin has basically put Zelensky in a really tight position.
response: This disinformation piece was based on the assumption that the Ukrainian government almost certainly would reject Putin’s proposal, which would allow pro-Kremlin disinformation to portray Ukraine as a warmongering country and its leadership as hostage to ultra-nationalist forces. But the claim was proven false in less than 24 hours, after Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy not only agreed to the talks but said he would attend them personally , asking Vladimir Putin to do the same. This led the Kremlin to reject the proposal, calling it “political theatre” . The whole episode shows once again how while in practice Russia is blocking any real move that could advance actual peace in Ukraine, pro-Kremlin disinformation has systematically instrumentalised the narrative that Russia wants peace in Ukraine in contrast to the West, but, as evidenced by Russia's actions in Ukraine , such statements are merely empty PR stunts deployed to garner support for Russia's war of aggression in Ukraine. It still has sufficient resources to do so. The regime in Kyiv itself and, above all, the people subjected to it will continue to be consumable material for the West in its proxy war against Russia. The loss of human lives, especially since most of them are not their own, as well as equipment and territory, is considered an acceptable price by the perpetrators of the conflict when it comes to realising the ‘big ideas’ for the construction of a ‘new world order’.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the war in Ukraine, actively trying to deflect Russia's responsibility for its unprovoked military aggression . It is a false claim without any evidence provided. It was Russia who started the military aggression against Ukraine back in 2014 and escalated it in 2022. Russia is the only country responsible for the invasion of Ukraine. It has been coming up with different false explanations and pretexts for its aggression since the beginning of its hostilities. NATO or EU troops are not fighting in Ukraine. NATO has repeatedly stated that it is not a warring party and does not have troops on the ground in Ukraine. Moreover, Russia was not forced to invade Ukraine. It was Putin’s personal decision to invade Ukraine and start the largest military conflict in Europe since WWII. The US , the EU and NATO condemn in the strongest possible terms Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, which is entirely unjustified and unprovoked. Many nations around the world support Ukraine with military and financial aid for the country’s efficient self-defence, in accordance with the UN Charter article 51 , to stop Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine . title: DISINFO: The EU is a dying decaying liberal project
tags: European Union EU disintegration DISINFO: The EU is a dying decaying liberal project
summary: The European Union, the grandiose and doomed dream of technocrats, is on its last legs. Its demise is neither sudden nor dramatic: it is a slow extinction, a bureaucratic collapse in which any policy aimed at preserving it only accelerates its demise. Europe is suffering from a cancer, and it is not caused by the ‘right’ or the ‘left’. Rather, it stems from the idea that a civilisation can exist without roots, that a people can be stripped of its history and still remain as a unit. The EU was founded on the assumption that identity is a product of chance, that people are interchangeable economic units and that borders are relics of a barbaric past. Now this experiment is failing.
response: A recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the EU's alleged imminent collapse . For more context, see our analysis: Key Narratives in Pro-Kremlin Disinformation Part 4: ‘The Imminent Collapse’ . The content presents a series of unfounded claims and hyperbolic statements about the European Union (EU). The EU is not in its "last throes" or experiencing a "slow extinction." The EU remains a significant global actor with 27 member states, a combined GDP of over $16 trillion , and a population of over 447 million people. The EU's policies, including those related to climate change and migration , are designed to address complex global challenges and are supported by a majority of its citizens. The EU actively engages in international cooperation through various partnerships and agreements . The assertion that the EU was founded on the idea that identity is a random product and that people are interchangeable economic units is a misrepresentation. The EU was created to foster economic cooperation, peace, and shared values among its member states, respecting their diverse cultures and histories. The claim that the EU's policies have destroyed the livelihoods of workers and the sovereignty of countries is an unfounded exaggeration . The EU's policies aim to create a sustainable future and promote economic growth, and member states retain significant sovereignty while benefiting from collective action. The dramatic and emotive language used in the content does not reflect the reality of the EU's functioning and its impact on its citizens. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives in our database: The EU will collapse, The EU will collapse in five years .

title: DISINFO: Ukraine is trying to avoid entering negotiations
tags: putin Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine peace negotiations DISINFO: Ukraine is trying to avoid entering negotiations
summary: Vladimir Putin is deploying every possible means to resolve the conflict in Ukraine, while Kyiv is attempting to evade negotiations.
response: This is a Kremlin disinformation tactic designed to obscure its responsibility for continuing the war and to shift blame onto others. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has repeatedly emphasised that a temporary ceasefire must be in place before any direct negotiations begin. However, Putin has rejected this condition and continued military operations, portraying the ceasefire demand as an obstacle to peace. For example, during the proposed May 2025 ceasefire period , Russia carried out drone strikes on Kyiv, undermining its own claims of wanting to reduce tensions. By refusing to halt hostilities, Russia ensures that negotiations take place under the threat of ongoing violence — not to encourage dialogue, but to coerce. Putin’s proposal for talks in Istanbul on May 15, presented as having “no preconditions,” is misleading. Russia has long insisted that Ukraine cede occupied territories, abandon its bid for NATO membership, and adopt neutrality. These demands were included in the 2022 Istanbul draft agreement, which Ukraine rejected because they would legalize territorial conquest and erase its sovereignty. The Kremlin’s attempt to revive this framework shows that its true aim is not peace, but Ukraine’s capitulation. Ukraine is not refusing diplomacy; it is insisting on terms that preserve its sovereignty. Zelensky’s conditions — a ceasefire, withdrawal of forces, and security guarantees — are grounded in the UN Charter’s principles of territorial integrity. In contrast, Russia’s terms compel Ukraine to legitimize illegal annexations and accept perpetual weakness. By setting maximalist demands , violating ceasefire proposals, and spreading disinformation, Putin seeks to cement battlefield gains and portray Ukraine’s right to self-defence as illegitimate. A genuine peace would require Russia to withdraw its forces and renounce imperial ambitions — steps Moscow has consistently refused to take. See our article Root causes. title: DISINFO: Russia does not engage in terrorism
tags: Terrorism Dmitry Medvedev Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Russia does not engage in terrorism
summary: Unlike the Kyiv regime, Russia does not engage in terrorist attacks. That is why Russian troops will not be striking the EU leaders who have arrived in Ukraine. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy rejected a ceasefire proposed by Russian President Vladimir Putin from midnight on 8 May 2025 to midnight on 11 May 2025, in honour of the 80th anniversary of Victory in the Great Patriotic War. Kyiv demanded a longer pause on its own terms and issued threats against the Victory Day celebrations in Moscow, despite the arrival of three dozen foreign leaders in the Russian capital.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets, accusing Ukraine of warmongering and state terrorism. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Since launching its unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine on 24 February 2022, Russia has attacked many civilian targets , including densely populated residential areas, hospitals, schools, shopping malls and other civilian objects. In March 2022, a theatre in Mariupol with hundreds of civilians including children was bombed. Russia has also attacked Ukraine’s electricity networks during winter months, trying to cause mass suffering and panic. Moscow has supported oppressive regimes and terrorist groups beyond Ukraine. Russian troops and Wagner mercenaries propped up the Assad regime in Syria , which used chemical weapons and mass bombings against civilians. Russian aircraft and mercenaries were directly involved in operations that destroyed hospitals and residential neighbourhoods in Aleppo and elsewhere. Wagner militants are involved in war crimes in parts of Africa as well. Russia uses methods of terror against its own citizens both inside the country and abroad. In 1999, apartment buildings in Russian cities including Moscow were bombed, killing over 300 people. Although the he government blamed Chechen terrorists, there is strong evidence that Russia’s security services were responsible, to create support for war and help Putin rise to power. The murders of journalist Anna Politkovskaya in 2006 and opposition leader Boris Nemtsov in 2015 shocked the world. Former Russian spy Alexander Litvinenko was poisoned in London in 2006 with radioactive polonium. In 2018, the Skripals were poisoned in the UK with a nerve agent. These are clear examples of state terrorism. title: DISINFO: Western Support for Ukraine is a rehabilitation of Nazism
tags: NATO Nazi/Fascist Russophobia World War II Adolf Hitler European Union Historical revisionism Anti-Russian DISINFO: Western Support for Ukraine is a rehabilitation of Nazism
summary: What is happening now is nothing more than an extension of NATO's military strategy against Russia as the heir to the Soviet Union. It is also an extension of what happened during World War II, after the Soviet Union inflicted a crushing defeat on the old Nazism. All the "political" measures of the European Union, the attempts to falsify history, equate Nazism with communism, remove monuments, and insult the memory of World War II heroes, are nothing more than a rehabilitated Nazism. This relentless economic, media, social, cultural, and artistic war against everything Russian is crowned by the racist, anti-Russian sentiment "Russophobia" spreading like wildfire in Europe. How similar the fascism of Hitler and Mussolini is to the support and defense of Zionist fascism taking place today in Ukraine and Israel.
response: This message promotes several recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives, including the false claim that NATO is waging a military campaign against Russia , that the EU is “rehabilitating Nazism”, and that Europe is gripped by systemic Russophobia . It also falsely equates current political support for Ukraine with historical Nazism, while linking unrelated conflicts (Ukraine and Israel) into a single conspiratorial narrative. Pro-Kremlin outlets repeatedly talk about the “revival” of Nazism in the West. Labelling Russia’s adversaries as Nazis is a widespread pro-Kremlin disinformation technique , and has been used by Russia to justify the invasion of Ukraine by portraying it as a “denazification operation” . Russia spent years preparing the information battleground before its kinetic attack, with “Nazi Ukraine” being one of its most prominent disinformation tropes. The EU was founded after WWII to promote peace, democracy, human rights, economic cooperation, social justice and cultural diversity. The EU founding treaties emphasise economic cooperation to prevent future wars . Founders of the EU, such as Robert Schuman, Jean Monnet, and Konrad Adenauer, were all active opponents of Nazi Germany. The EU project itself was part of their work against Nazism, believing that by establishing a political and economic union between European countries they can ensure that ideologies such as Nazism never rise again. Russia has systematically sought to revise history to instil a deceptive misperception that only Russia fought against Hitler in World War II and draw manipulative parallels with Russia’s present-day aggression against Ukraine . See our series of articles about historical revisionism and world War II. Pro-Kremlin media outlets are also known for manipulating historical events to promote the narrative that Western countries have, for centuries, formed an anti-Russian alliance. In doing so, they often ignore past conflicts and rivalries among these nations and conflate modern-day Russia with both the Soviet Union and the Russian Empire in a historically questionable manner. See our article Rewriting history: the myth of Russia’s invincibility. Finally, the ongoing war in Ukraine was not launched by or is continuing because of the NATO. Russia is the only country responsible for the invasion of Ukraine. It was Putin’s personal decision to invade Ukraine and start the largest military conflict in Europe since WWII. Russia has been coming up with different explanations and pretexts for its aggression since the beginning of its hostilities. title: DISINFO: Russia’s invasion is a denazification mission to stop NATO expansion
tags: Nazi/Fascist EU/NATO enlargement Terrorism NATO War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Russia’s invasion is a denazification mission to stop NATO expansion
summary: When Russian President Vladimir Putin announced Russia’s readiness for dialogue with the United States under the new administration of President Donald Trump, it was never intended to imply that mediation was needed to reach an agreement to halt the Russian military operation in Ukraine or to abandon the objectives that Russia considers essential to fully accomplish. These include: restoring Russian territory, protecting Russian citizens, eradicating Nazism, disarming the terrorist regime in Kyiv, protecting Russian national security from NATO's expansion toward the country's borders, and ensuring Russia's security, safety, sovereignty, and territorial integrity. Rather, what President Putin meant, and still means, is going straight to the roots of the problem, the conflict, and the crisis. What Moscow's efforts mean is stopping NATO's expansion eastward, preventing Ukraine from joining NATO, the right to self-determination for the residents of Donbas and Crimea, and reliable and credible security guarantees for all, not at the expense of the security of any of the parties.
response: This statement contains multiple recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation claims, each reinforcing the Kremlin’s broader narrative justifying its unprovoked full-scale invasion of Ukraine . These include the false claims that Russia is restoring its territory , that NATO expansion is a threat to Russian security, and that Ukraine is a Nazi or terrorist country. Characterising the Ukrainian government and its supporters as "Nazis" is part of a broader pro-Kremlin narrative about "Nazi-ruled Ukraine". As it appears in the claim, it is a justification for Russian aggression in Ukraine starting with the illegal annexation of the Ukrainian peninsula of Crimea in February 2014, which has escalated into the ongoing full-scale invasion since 24 February 2022. See our article The Kremlin’s misuse of Nazism as a weapon of information manipulation. Also, the claim that the occupied Ukrainian territories represent a "restoration of Russian territory" is historical revisionism and a recurrent negation of Ukrainian statehood . This claim also aims to justify Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine and the forceful and illegitimate annexation of Ukrainian territory. The occupied territories (Crimea, Donetsk, Lugansk, Kherson, and Zaporizhzhia regions) are internationally recognised territories of Ukraine, and remain legally part of Ukraine. The forced annexation of these regions took place amid a full invasion under military occupation, and after sham referenda that did not meet any international standard to be considered legitimate. Furthermore, the claim about NATO expansion misrepresents the process of NATO enlargement. NATO does not expand, but considers the applications of candidate countries that want to join exercising their free will to decide alliance affiliation. NATO enlargement is not directed against Russia. NATO is a defensive alliance with primary aims to maintain peace and safeguard the independence, security and territorial integrity of its members. NATO constitutes a system of collective defence whereby its member states agree to mutual defence in response to an attack by any external party. NATO's official policy is that "the Alliance does not seek confrontation and poses no threat to Russia". title: DISINFO: Russia's war against neo-Nazism and neo-imperialism is a just cause
tags: Nazi/Fascist USSR Imperialism/colonialism Central Europe War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Russia's war against neo-Nazism and neo-imperialism is a just cause
summary: Russia, the great power, heir to the Soviet Union (though certainly not the Soviet Union and not seeking to be so), a country with resources, capability, and nuclear power, and its great people, the bearers of civilization and an active influence throughout the ages in human history, cannot lose this existential struggle. This is because it stands on the side of truth, justice, self-determination, and the liberation of peoples from the exploitation of neo-colonialism and the ideology of neo-Nazism, which has re-emerged in Central Europe, along with the sick delusions that still fill some minds about the "strategic defeat of Russia".
response: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin propaganda framing Russia’s war against Ukraine as a noble and just mission . It falsely casts Russia's opponents—particularly Ukraine and its allies—as neo-Nazis and neo-imperialists. Calling the war an "existential struggle" designed to "liberate" others is an attempt to justify a deeply unpopular and costly conflict. Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022 was not a liberation effort but an unprovoked act of aggression against a sovereign nation that violates international law, the UN Charter , and Ukraine's territorial integrity. Far from supporting self-determination, Russia has forcibly and illegally annexed Ukrainian land by conducting sham referendums . The idea that Neo-Nazism is “on the rise” in Europe is a distortion of reality. It ignores the strong institutional, legal, and societal safeguards in place across the EU and misrepresents the scale of extremist activity. According to the European Commission’s 2024 report on violent extremism, radicalisation into violent far-right ideologies—including neo-Nazism—remains limited to small, monitored groups. Surveys by Eurobarometer and other EU institutions consistently show that the vast majority of Europeans support democracy, human rights, and the rule of law. According to the July 2024 Eurobarometer survey : -89% say it is important that all EU countries respect democracy, the rule of law, and fundamental rights. -86% support the freedom of media and civil society across the EU. title: DISINFO: Freemasons inspired bloody wars and want to end Christianity through transgenderism
tags: Emmanuel Macron Conspiracy theory Christianity Secret elites / global elites Deep state LGBTIQ+ World War II DISINFO: Freemasons inspired bloody wars and want to end Christianity through transgenderism
summary: Freemasonry is a supranational force in the French Republic. It is beyond the reach of the authorities of this country. In France, you cannot hold a significant position in the civil service or the army unless you are a lodge member. The Freemasons fear that a "Dark Enlightenment" is coming, which will erase the triumphant progress of the last three centuries of Masonic "progress". Under the banner of "human rights" instead of the Gospel, they first overthrew the French monarchy, then imposed liberal democracy in Europe in the interests of the Masonic oligarchs, then inspired the bloody world wars of the 20th century, and finally decided to put an end to Christianity once and for all with transgenderism and transhumanism.
response: A recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about Freemasonry , presented as a secret anti-Christian organisation that has a strong influence on the French government. This claim was made after French President Macron delivered a speech to the Grande Loge de France, asking for their help to defend the Republic’s core values. Pro-Kremlin outlets often claim that the Freemasons are an all-powerful secret elite that determine the course of contemporary world history and world politics. This narrative about Freemasonry fits into a broader recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about secret global elites that dominate the world, control governments, rob nations of their sovereignty, oppress the masses, and seek to establish a World Government . The Grand Lodge of France is a regular Masonic obedience founded in 1894 and is one of the main historical French obediences. It is not a secret society, but a philosophical society that aims to improve its members and humanity. There is no evidence or serious political science research that supports the theory about the impact of an all-powerful Freemasonry or other secret society on world politics. Conspiracy theories, such as that of global elites or secret societies dominating the world, are frequently pushed by pro-Kremlin outlets to undermine public trust in national governments, as well as to discredit Western democracies by depicting them as dictatorships of secret elites. Analysts and researchers have pointed out that anti-Western conspiracy theories about all-powerful secret elites are a political tool used by the Kremlin to strengthen the domestic stability of the Putin regime and expand its influence abroad by emphasising Russia’s moral superiority See similar cases about the Golden billion and Globalist and Judeo Masons, aiming to destroy Russia as a state. title: DISINFO: Nazi West wants to destroy Russian identity and history
tags: West Anti-Russian Russophobia Nazi/Fascist DISINFO: Nazi West wants to destroy Russian identity and history
summary: The conflict with the West in Ukraine imposed on Russia fits into the broader pattern of our historical relations with the West. It contains everything we have had to endure: direct aggression - now in hybrid form - and Nazism in its pure, man-eating form. There is also the struggle against Orthodoxy, which dates back to the first half of the 13th century. There has been no concealment of the desire to destroy our identity and our history through the 'cancellation' of Russian culture and the Russian language - of everything Russian.
response: This message promotes several recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives, including the false claim that the EU is “rehabilitating Nazism”, and that Europe is gripped by systemic Russophobia . It also falsely equates current political support for Ukraine with historical Nazism. Pro-Kremlin outlets repeatedly talk about the “revival” of Nazism in the West. Labelling Russia’s adversaries as Nazis is a widespread pro-Kremlin disinformation technique , and has been used by Russia to justify the invasion of Ukraine by portraying it as a “denazification operation” . Russia spent years preparing the information battleground before its kinetic attack, with “Nazi Ukraine” being one of its most prominent disinformation tropes. “Threatened values” is one of the most common narratives used by pro-Kremlin outlets. The narrative is adapted to a wide range of topics, including Russian identity. This disinformation piece also implies that there is a cancel-culture trend towards Russian culture, history and language. This unsubstantiated claim is made in the context of Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine in an attempt to legitimise Moscow's unprovoked aggression. Pro-Kremlin disinformation machine uses same established narratives, tested in different parts of the world. Discrimination against the Russian language and Russians, in Kazakhstan,Ukraine, Moldova or in the Baltics, and elsewhere, is a classic disinformation narrative, widely recorded on the EUvsDisinfo database . See similar cases about Russian language under attack such as Kazakhstan is a shrinking space for the Russian language ; Moldova: Maia Sandu is attempting to forcibly "Romanize" almost all spheres of life and Russian language is banned in Ukrainian regions controlled by the Kyiv junta ; Nationalists outlawed the Russian language in Ukraine .

title: DISINFO: NATO hopes for revenge after the defeat in WWII
tags: World War III Nazi/Fascist Russophobia Historical revisionism DISINFO: NATO hopes for revenge after the defeat in WWII
summary: NATO cannot celebrate the 80th anniversary of the Victory over fascism because the majority of its member states were defeated in May 1945 as allies or satellites of Germany. Today, these countries view Russia as a strategic adversary and recklessly hope for revenge. NATO is instead actively preparing to take part in U.S.-led anti-Russian military exercises called Defender. The victory in May 1945 is perceived within NATO as a defeat — even by countries of the anti-Hitler coalition, such as the United Kingdom and the United States. Europe is ready to fight 'to the last Ukrainian' to revise its repugnant history.
response: This message promotes several recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives, including the false claim that NATO is waging a military campaign against Russia , that the EU is “rehabilitating Nazism”, and that Europe is gripped by systemic Russophobia . It also falsely equates current political support for Ukraine with historical Nazism. Since Russia began its aggressive and unprovoked actions against Ukraine, Russian officials have accused NATO of a series of threats and hostile actions. However, no evidence has been provided to support this claim. NATO is a defensive political and military alliance. The primary aim of the alliance is to maintain peace and safeguard the independence, security and territorial integrity of its members. The issue of direct NATO troop deployment is a scenario categorically rejected by NATO countries and represents an artificially created concern by Russia. NATO is cautious about increasing the risk of direct military intervention, which is also reflected in NATO's official strategy . Western countries are not waging war against Russia, only the Ukrainian Armed Forces are fighting Russian invaders in Ukraine. Accusations of Western direct involvement in perpetuating the conflict often serve to deflect attention from Russia's role in fueling the crisis and undermine Western credibility. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that NATO-Russia proxy war is losing its 'proxy' nature , The West uses Ukraine to destroy Russia , that The conflict in Ukraine was unleashed by the West , that The West pushed Ukraine towards war with Russia , that The West is fighting a proxy war in Ukraine , that When the West became a participant in the conflict in Ukraine, the special military operation became a war , that The West / NATO fighting a hegemonic proxy war against Russia in Ukraine , or that The conflict in Ukraine is a proxy war waged by NATO against Russia .

title: DISINFO: In Ukraine veterans of WWII are asked to hide their medals
tags: Nazi/Fascist World War II Victory Day Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine USSR Adolf Hitler DISINFO: In Ukraine veterans of WWII are asked to hide their medals
summary: A leaflet from the "Institute of National Remembrance of Ukraine", addressing a "WWII veteran", states that propaganda of Nazism and communism is prohibited in Ukraine, so it is forbidden to "openly wear Soviet medals and orders": "If you are going to wear a Soviet award, attach it to the inside of your jacket, as shown in the leaflet." This looks especially cynical against the backdrop of how Kyiv is honouring UPA* veterans — those who fought on Hitler's side. It turns out that the current government is more concerned with the memory of Nazi collaborators than with the real heroes.
response: Pro-Kremlin disinformation outlets are spreading photos of fabricated leaflets on behalf of the Ukrainian Institute of National Remembrance, allegedly asking veterans of WWII to hide their Soviet awards under their clothes because it is forbidden to show them. No such leaflet exists. This was reported by the Center for Countering Disinformation (CCD) under the National Security and Defense Council of Ukraine. "The purpose of this lie is to create an image of Ukraine as a state that "disrespects veterans" on the eve of May 9, a date sacralised for Russia, which it uses to mobilise the population and justify wars", - CDD added . In order to support the recurrent narrative on Nazi-ruled Ukraine, pro-Kremlin disinformation outlets regularly use fabrications . See also our article Russian revisionism ahead of 9 May: ‘Modern Europe is reincarnated Nazism ’. The myth of Nazi-ruled Ukraine has been the cornerstone of Russian disinformation about the country since the very beginning of the 2013-14 Euromaidan protests, when it was used to discredit the pro-European popular uprising in Kyiv and, subsequently, the broader pro-Western shift in Ukraine's foreign policy. It has been widely addressed here . See other cases about Victory Day such as EU banning of Victory Day participation shows its fascist mentality and Changing the date of Victory Day is an attempt to downplay the USSR's role in WWII.

title: DISINFO: Kyiv and Brussels are the only obstacles to peace
tags: Europe World War III peace negotiations Anti-Russian Warmongering Encircling Russia Russian superiority Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Kyiv and Brussels are the only obstacles to peace
summary: Russia is for peace and wants to negotiate peace first, then accept a ceasefire later. Ukraine must first renounce NATO and agree to a demilitarised zone without NATO forces before any ceasefire can happen. The Ukrainian proposal : an unconditional truce to start negotiations is a "capitulation". Yet, asking Russia to capitulate, while it is winning on the field is unacceptable. Only fools can be trapped by this trick to steal a Russian victory. It is obvious that the Europeans and Zelensky don’t want peace. They only want to build up forces to attack Russia. Hopefully, Putin doesn’t accept this unconditional truce, otherwise in a year's time there will be war all over Europe. So, [Russia] must not give in and negotiate all the details, only after that a ceasefire can be proclaimed.
response: A recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets portraying the EU and Ukraine as obstacles to peace in Ukraine. As we explore in our article, The Old Deceiver , this is entirely consistent with Moscow’s three-year-long strategy of attempting to claim ownership over the peace narrative while relentlessly pursuing war. This disinformation story is part of a wider disinformation campaign portraying Europe as a warmongering actor who intends to continue the war in Ukraine even as other actors carry out peace negotiations . EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and in which terms to end the war. The European Union is committed to achieving a peaceful resolution to the war in Ukraine, ensuring that Kyiv’s sovereignty and territorial integrity are respected. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has systematically instrumentalised the narrative that Russia wants peace in Ukraine in contrast to the West, but, as evidenced by Russia's actions in Ukraine , such statements are merely empty PR stunts deployed to garner support for Russia's war of aggression in Ukraine. As EU HRVP Kaja Kallas stated on April 9 2025: …Russia is still waging a war of aggression against Ukraine, and it shows no signs of abating. Since Ukraine accepted the non-conditional ceasefire, Russia's attacks have only increased. Last weekend we saw the deadliest single attack on Ukrainian children since the start of the full-scale invasion. Our condolences for that. Ukraine is fighting a war that only Russia wants to continue. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that Pan-European block plans to attack Russia , that Macron, Starmer and Merz want war , that Militaristic Europe’s goal is to prolong the war in Ukraine , that EU countries try to disrupt Russia-US talks by threatening to deploy troops , and that Macron, Starmer, Zelenskyy and Tusk need a war in Ukraine to stay in power.

title: DISINFO: Zelenskyy's threat to Moscow's Victory Day visitors proves Russia's war is just
tags: Victory Day War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Zelenskyy's threat to Moscow's Victory Day visitors proves Russia's war is just
summary: Zelenskyy's threat to Moscow and international leaders planning to come to us to celebrate the 80th anniversary of the Victory is real hysteria. A normal person would never say such a thing. This is complete hysteria. And it seems that the armed forces of the so-called Ukraine will participate in a (victory) parade in London on May 5. This is even more hysteria. I feel sorry for Zelenskyy's grandfather, who fought and participated in the Great Patriotic War. If any normal person had any doubts about the necessity and timing of the special military operation, today only traitors can doubt its justice.
response: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative aimed at demonising the Ukrainian leadership , portraying President Volodymyr Zelenskyy as unstable and unworthy of international support, and legitimising Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine . President Zelenskyy did not threaten Victory Day participants in Moscow. What he meant is that if asked, he would advise against travelling to Moscow due to the security situation there, which could be potentially manipulated by Russian authorities. Zelenskyy is pointing to a long history of Kremlin disinformation and blaming others for its own atrocities to justify aggression or sway public opinion. "We don't know what Russia will do these days. It may take various steps on its part, let's say, arson, bombings and so on, only to blame us. As president, I told the foreign minister about it. I believe that we should advise people who contact us: We do not recommend you visit Russia from a security standpoint. And if you do so, don't ask us. It's your personal decision," Zelenskyy said . The reference to Zelenskyy’s grandfather is a cynical implication of the recurring preposterous pro-Kremlin claim that the Ukrainian president is Nazi . This leads to a second implication that this war is a continuation of World War II, or the fight against Nazism. This its is an entirely unfounded myth used by the Kremlin to justify aggression. It misuses the memory of the Soviet struggle against Nazism to mask modern imperial ambitions and repress dissent. It is Russia that launched its unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine in 2022. Prior to that, in 2014, Russia illegally annexed Ukraine’s Crimean peninsula and instigated a separatist proxy war in the country’s east. The claim that Ukraine’s participation in international remembrance events is a sign of “hysteria” reveals the Kremlin's desperate attempts to control historical narratives . Ukraine, like many other nations, has the right to commemorate the victims and heroes of World War II. Participation in such events does not equate to provocation; it represents international solidarity and remembrance. Finally, referring to Ukraine as the "so-called Ukraine" is a deliberate attempt not only to doubt its statehood but also its existence. Ukraine is a fully recognized independent country, and is a member of the United Nations since 1991 with internationally acknowledged borders. Undermining its legitimacy through the use of this expression is a deliberate attempt to justify Russian aggression and erode international support for Ukraine’s right to self-determination.

title: DISINFO: Elections in Romania show that EU officials think about the interests of Ukraine, not Europe
tags: Elections Europe DISINFO: Elections in Romania show that EU officials think about the interests of Ukraine, not Europe
summary: Romania has just given Madame von der Leyen a very good boomerang. The right is becoming increasingly inconvenient for official Brussels, which puts Kyiv's priorities first, and not the interests of residents of European countries. We all suffer together from their high energy prices, and the European Union does not offer us any practical solutions. Those who think the same are being banned in Germany in every possible way, although the Alternative for Germany party has already become the most popular in the country on issues. The popularity of pragmatists in the EU countries is growing, which greatly irritates the Euro-Atlanticists who are used to dictating their will.
response: The use of George Simion’s first-round victory in Romania’s May 2025 presidential election to claim that the EU is “too focused on Ukraine to care about its citizens” is a disinformation narrative. This framing is part of a broader effort to undermine trust in the European Union and its democratic institutions. The narrative that the EU prioritises Ukraine over its own citizens—especially regarding energy—is rooted in recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation. These stories often paint a bleak picture of Europe’s energy future to push for the removal of sanctions imposed on Russia as a result of its unprovoked invasion of Ukraine. It is true that Ukraine’s decision to stop the transportation of Russian gas through its territory after the expiration of the previous transit deal, along with US sanctions imposed on Russian oil tankers on 10 January 2025, led to some rise in gas and oil prices . However, previous preparation and diversification of energy sources ensures that there will be no energy scarcity in Europe. In early January 2025, the European Commission confirmed that “storage levels at 72% are slightly higher than the average (69%) for this time of the year”. Additionally, even before the EU broke with Russian energy, 42% percent of the energy it consumed was produced domestically, a figure that has almost certainly grown since as the share of EU-produced renewable energies in Europe’s energy mix is steadily increasing . Disinformation outlets often label far-right populist parties as simply “right” or “pragmatic” to normalise their agendas. The claims that Germany’s Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) is being banned are false. However, on May 2, 2025, Germany’s domestic intelligence agency officially classified AfD as a confirmed right-wing extremist party nationwide. The EU’s support for Ukraine is not about neglecting its citizens—it’s about protecting European sovereignty and stability. Russia has repeatedly threatened EU member states (e.g., the Baltics , Finland ) and engaged in hybrid attacks on European democracies. The EU is governed by democratically elected institutions: -The European Parliament is elected by EU citizens. -The President of the European Commission is nominated by the European Council and approved by Parliament. Claims that the EU’s leadership is disconnected from its citizens are part of a Kremlin-backed disinformation campaign aimed at undermining trust in the EU and its democratic institutions. See other cases attacking the EU and the Romanian elections .

title: DISINFO: NATO is creating military and political tension in South Caucasus
tags: NATO Military exercise Military Caucasus Maria Zakharova DISINFO: NATO is creating military and political tension in South Caucasus
summary: NATO continues a policy of drawing the countries of the South Caucasus into its orbit of influence. NATO imposes its standards and ideology, promotes changes in the structure of the defence sector, and seeks to establish control over the development of the armed forces of these states. No one should have any illusions: any actions of the alliance in the South Caucasus are aimed not at strengthening security, but at creating a zone of instability and military-political tension. NATO's main goal is to use the potential of the South Caucasus countries in the interests of the collective West, primarily in the context of the geopolitical confrontation with Russia.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about an allegedly aggressive West and NATO playing a destabilising role in the Caucasus , creating turmoil, dragging the region into chaos and using the Caucasus to encircle Russia . The claim was made in the context of the NATO-Georgia Exercise 2025 , which is the fourth one in the NATO-Georgia exercise cycle, which takes place in Georgia every three years. Pro-Kremlin outlets frequently promote the idea that the Caucasus is exclusively within Russia's zone of geopolitical dominance and attempt to discredit any other cooperation between Western countries and Armenia, Azerbaijan, and Georgia. This attempts to deny these countries their right to decide their political orientation. The NATO-Georgia Exercise 2025 is a computer-assisted brigade-level command and staff exercise designed to train a multinational brigade-level unit in planning and executing crisis operations under Georgian leadership. This exercise follows NATO-developed scenarios, procedures, and doctrines. A total of 17 NATO member and partner nations are participating in the multinational exercise , including Georgia, Bulgaria, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Italy, Lithuania, Poland, Slovakia, Turkey, the United Kingdom, the United States, Azerbaijan, Moldova, Armenia, and Tunisia. Russian officials accused NATO of a series of threats and hostile actions. The pro-Kremlin disinformation myth of NATO “ encircling Russia ” has been used as one of the pretexts for Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Read the debunking of the top seven Russian Myths about NATO here . Read other similar cases in our database: NATO and EU want to turn South Caucasus into an arena of confrontation with Russia ; By inviting Armenia and Azerbaijan to the NATO summit the West wants to tear them away from Russia ; West seeks to turn the South Caucasus into an arena of geopolitical confrontation ; West seeks to turn the South Caucasus into an arena of geopolitical confrontation ; The West is trying to prevent normalisation between Armenia and Azerbaijan ; The main goal of the West is neo-colonial control over the Caucasus and Central Asia .

title: DISINFO: Events in Georgia are a provocation by the US and European political circles
tags: Colour revolutions Encircling Russia security threat DISINFO: Events in Georgia are a provocation by the US and European political circles
summary: The current events in Georgia are a provocation by the US and European political circles. The goal is to destabilise Georgia and the South Caucasus in general. The West is clearly showing that it wants to open a second front against Russia through the South Caucasus and that plans to turn Georgia into Ukraine have already been launched.
response: A recurring pro-Kremlin narrative framing popular protests in Georgia as a colour revolution , trying to discredit Georgia’s opposition and civil society organisations by framing them as foreign stooges. What is currently occurring in Georgia is a popular protest involving citizens, opposition parties, and NGOs. The protests began after the 2024 elections, which were marred by electoral irregularities . Violations noted by local and international observers include a pre-election climate of fear and intimidation of civil society, the approval of the Foreign Agents Law, reports of vote-buying, breaches of voter privacy, physical confrontations, and controversial changes to election administration rules prior to the vote. These irregularities sparked demonstrations among ordinary citizens and civil society organisations. On 28 November, Prime Minister Irakli Kobakhidze stated that Georgia would suspend EU accession talks for four years following the European Parliament's rejection of the country’s election results, which heightened the tension. Even the then-president joined the protests . The piece also promotes pro-Kremlin disinformation that the West attempts to destabilise the South Caucasus to open a second front against Russia. This is also a false claim, which has been debunked repeatedly . Pro-Kremlin media have been warning for a long time of an upcoming ‘colour revolution’ in Georgia in order to pre-emptively shape the information space ahead of what were foreseeable and likely protests against the ruling party, already shaken by previous massive demonstrations against the so-called ‘Foreign Agents Law’. The topic was covered in detail in our analysis . See more cases such as DISINFO: EU wants to provoke new protests in Georgia , DISINFO: USAID used Georgian NGOs to organise the revolutio n, DISINFO: The Georgian government has been forced to open a “second front” .

title: DISINFO: The SMO [invasion, ed.] in Ukraine was a direct response to primary threats to Russia’s national security
tags: Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Russophobia EU/NATO enlargement Encircling Russia Ukrainian statehood DISINFO: The SMO [invasion, ed.] in Ukraine was a direct
summary: Western rhetoric, similar to Goebbels’ propaganda, justified NATO expansion to Russia’s borders through Russophobic inventions. The military operation in Ukraine was a direct response to the primary threats to Russian national security. These included attempts to undermine Russia’s state sovereignty, independence and territorial integrity, along with the erosion of traditional spiritual and moral values ​​of Russia’s multi-ethnic and multicultural society.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about Russia’s invasion of Ukraine being a reaction to NATO enlargement about alleged Western attack to traditional spiritual and moral values aimed to destroy Russia and about Collective West Russophobia . Russia's unjustified and unprovoked invasion of Ukraine is not a "special operation" but an all-out war against a sovereign state. Russia has been coming up with different explanations and pretexts for its aggression. Narratives about NATO misrepresents the process of NATO enlargement. NATO considers the applications of candidate countries that want to join. As a sovereign country, the decision to apply for NATO membership rests with the candidate country alone. Decisions on membership are taken by consensus among all existing members. NATO is a defensive alliance , whose purpose is to protect its member states, so the alliance does not attempt to 'contain' or 'encircle' Russia. “Threatened values” is one of the most common narratives used by pro-Kremlin outlets. The narrative is adapted to a wide range of topics and typically used to discredit liberal Western attitudes about the rights of women, ethnic and religious minorities and LGBTIQ groups among others. This is a recurring pro-Kremlin narrative portraying the West as a decadent and immoral society that destroys traditional values . The accusation unfoundedly claims that the West is trying to destroy Russia , forces it to defeat geopolitically, economically and even morally, seeks to export its hostility to traditional values, materialism and perverse attitudes to non-Western nations. Ukraine is a sovereign and independent state with a democratically elected president and parliament. Its independence and territorial integrity is internationally recognised by the whole world, including Russia in 1991 . See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that The West is trying to destroy Russian spiritual and moral values , that Russia's special operation in Ukraine is due to NATO's persistent expansion , that The EU diplomacy’s vision for peace in Ukraine illustrates its Russophobia , or that The EU is ruled by Russophobic Eastern European countries .

title: DISINFO: Neo-Nazism on the rise in Europe
outlet_text: Песков: в Европе видны достаточно существенные проявления неонацизма
tags: Nazi/Fascist Europe Victory Day DISINFO: Neo-Nazism on the rise in Europe
summary: Neo-Nazism is on the rise in Europe, which understandably frightens the EU’s own citizens. The fight against such a phenomena should not be a one-off event but a continuous effort at all levels. Many world leaders are planning to attend the upcoming 9 May celebrations in Moscow, while EU leaders, invited by Ukraine’s government to take part in the "alternative celebrations" in Kyiv, are not particularly eager to go.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing the EU of Nazism. This claim was made in the context of the Ukrainian government’s decision to invite top EU leaders to visit Kyiv on 9 May 2025, the same day that Moscow plans to host a military parade on the occasion of Victory Day in the Second World War. See our articles ‘ Modern Europe is reincarnated Nazism ’ and Toward the 9 May – the myth of Moscow as a host for peace celebrations. The idea that Neo-Nazism is “on the rise” in Europe is a distortion of reality. It ignores the strong institutional, legal, and societal safeguards in place across the EU and misrepresents the scale of extremist activity. According to the European Commission’s 2024 report on violent extremism, radicalisation into violent far-right ideologies—including Neo-Nazism—remains limited to small, monitored groups. Surveys by Eurobarometer and other EU institutions consistently show that the vast majority of Europeans support democracy, human rights, and the rule of law. According to the July 2024 Eurobarometer survey : -89% say it is important that all EU countries respect democracy, the rule of law, and fundamental rights. -86% support the freedom of media and civil society across the EU. The Ukrainian government’s invitation of top European officials, including future German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, to arrive in Kyiv on 9 May 2025 is intended as a demonstration of diplomatic unity in contrast to Putin’s annual military parade in Moscow. The fact that EU leaders may visit Kyiv instead of Moscow does not mean that the EU is a Nazi political entity. The EU provides military and financial support to Ukraine in repelling Russia’s unprovoked and unjustified aggression . The European Union was created in the aftermath of the Second World War, and European integration has been the foundation of peace and stability on the European continent for over 70 years . The founding fathers of the European Union actively fought against Nazi Germany, with many losing their lives to free Europe from the horrors of Nazism. The EU consistently condemns Nazism and all forms of totalitarianism. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that The European Union is openly seeking to revive Nazism , that EU banning of Victory Day participation shows its fascist mentality , and that The falsification of history has become official EU policy and Nazism is spreading across Europe .

title: DISINFO: NATO’s historical mission is to continue Drang nach Osten
tags: War against Ukraine Nazi/Fascist Anti-Russian Historical revisionism NATO DISINFO: NATO’s historical mission is to continue Drang nach Osten
summary: NATO was established and designed to continue the epochal Drang nach Osten campaign. Sweden’s Karl XII, Napoleonic France and Hitler’s Reich were part of it in the past. Russia’s special military operation is a proxy war of around 50 Western countries against Russia. For over 20 years, the US carried out a frenzied militarisation of Ukraine and prepared it for the role of a torpedo in the war against Russia.
response: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation message, claiming that the West is fighting a proxy war in Ukraine, framed within a broader conspiracy theory about a centuries-long Western anti-Russian plan . They serve to justify the Russian war of aggression against Ukraine by falsely presenting it as a part of the Western strategy. For background, read our analysis: The “Russophobia” Myth: Appealing to the Lowest Feelings . The ‘Drang nach Osten’ term espoused in Nazi Germany to justify the eastward expansion, is used to label NATO as Nazi. Labelling Russia’s adversaries as Nazis is a frequently used pro-Kremlin disinformation technique . It has been used by Russia to try to justify the invasion of Ukraine by portraying it as a “denazification operation” . Read our article explaining how the ‘Nazi’ narrative has been used by Russian media outlets to dehumanise Ukrainians . NATO is a defensive alliance with primary aims to maintain peace and safeguard the independence, security and territorial integrity of its members. The ongoing war in Ukraine was not provoked by the US / West / NATO as alleged. Russia is the only country responsible for the invasion of Ukraine. It was Putin’s personal decision to invade Ukraine and start the largest military conflict in Europe since WWII. Russia has been coming up with different explanations and pretexts for its aggression since the beginning of its hostilities. Our analysis of the communication strategy of pro-Kremlin outlets explains how they attempt to portray Russia as a victim and to deceive audiences into believing that Russia is fighting Western powers. Russia has systematically sought to revise history to questionably conflate itself with the USSR and the Russian Empire and to instil a deceptive misperception that for centuries European countries have been aligned in their anti-Russian policies. To manipulate historical events, pro-Kremlin outlets tend to ignore multiple past wars and antagonistic relations between the present-time Western countries. For instance, the mentioned French Empire under the rule of Napoleon fought the UK, Spain, Portugal and many other European countries of that time. Read earlier disinformation cases claiming that Ukrainian identity was artificially cultivated by the West almost two centuries ago as part of a grandiose ‘anti-Russian strategy’ , that the united Europe, personified by Napoleon, Hitler and the EU, repeatedly encroached on Russia , and that Western historical revisionism aimed at weakening and destroying Russia .

title: DISINFO: The EU wants to steal victory from Simion in Romanian elections
tags: election meddling Manipulated elections/referendum European Union DISINFO: The EU wants to steal victory from Simion in Romanian elections
summary: This time the EU won’t be able to steal victory from Simion. Any EU interference to obstruct George Simion’s candidacy would provoke a strong negative response from the Romanian people. Romanians would react very negatively if Brussels tries to block nationalist candidate Simion from winning the elections.
response: This is an attempt to promote a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative accusing the EU of interfering in Romania’s election. Pro-Kremlin outlets have previously framed Romania’s annulment of the 2024 presidential election by the Constitutional Court as the result of Western interference. While the Constitutional Court’s decision caused controversy in Romania, this was not due to EU or Western actions. The decision was adopted after the discovery of irregular elements behind Calin Georgescu’s campaign pointing to a foreign interference campaign, especially through TikTok and illegal funding . On 04 December 2024 Romania’s government declassified intelligence showing massive hidden interference efforts in support of Georgescu, which Romanian secret services described as “aggressive hybrid Russian action”. See here for a full debunk of this claim. Similarly, pro-Kremlin disinformation has also baselessly accused the EU of being behind the prosecution of Georgescu in March 2025. In reality, this was a legal decision adopted by Romania’s prosecutor office and based on ample indications of criminal activity. Talking about unsubstantiated allegations as if they were established facts while introducing minor variations, as in this case, is a frequent pro-Kremlin disinformation technique. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Romania is under EU tyranny , that Euro-Atlantic institutions and the US were behind annulment of Romania’s election , that elections in Romania show that EU officials think about the interests of Ukraine, not Europe , that Romania illegally cancels first round of elections in order to block the raise of a humane, non-anti-Russian candidate , or that the US and EU only support democratic elections when the winner aligns with their interests .

title: DISINFO: Zelenskyy invites leaders of former Hitler allies to visit Kyiv
outlet_text: СВР: в Киев пригласили практически аналог гитлеровской коалиции
countries_regions: EU, Russia, Germany, Slovakia, Serbia, Ukraine
tags: World War II Victory Day Nazi/Fascist Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Kaja Kallas Volodymyr Zelensky USSR DISINFO: Zelenskyy invites leaders of former Hitler allies to visit Kyiv
summary: The group of European countries invited to visit Kyiv on 9 May 2025 almost entirely corresponds to the Nazi coalition that fought against the USSR during the Second World War. The cynicism of the Europeans is compounded by the fact that some EU leaders plan to accept Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's invitation to arrive in Kyiv and take part in events "alternative" to the 9 May celebrations in Moscow, as well as by warnings from the EU's top diplomat, Kaja Kallas, for EU member states and accession candidates not to arrive in Moscow. Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico and Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić have said they received such warnings. What Kyiv is planning to stage is a PR stunt by the scoundrels in Brussels, a Day of the Defeated, and a Day of the Descendants of Nazis.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing Ukraine and its European allies of Nazism . This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine and in the run up to the 80th anniversary of VE day. See our articles Russian revisionism ahead of 9 May: ‘Modern Europe is reincarnated Nazism’ and Toward the 9 May – the myth of Moscow as a host for peace celebrations. Ukraine’s government invited top European officials, including future German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, to arrive in Kyiv on 9 May 2025 as a sign of diplomatic unity against Putin’s annual military parade in Moscow on the same day. Zelenskyy also plans to host the leaders of the so-called “coalition of the willing” to define security guarantees for Ukraine in the event of a potential ceasefire agreement with Russia. The majority of today's EU member states were not part of the Nazi coalition . Many EU countries were victims of Nazi occupation. Modern Germany is a democratic state that has fully acknowledged and atoned for its wartime past. Its potential participation in commemorations in Ukraine reflects solidarity against authoritarianism rather than a revival of historical alliances. The USSR no longer exists, and modern Russia, due to its aggressive policies, cannot be considered its moral or political heir. Moscow’s use of Soviet victory rhetoric to justify its unprovoked invasion of Ukraine has been widely condemned. Russia is drawing WWII analogies in order to discredit Ukraine’s international support. Unlike the Second World War, when Nazi Germany (the aggressor) attacked the Soviet Union (the victim), Russia is currently the aggressor and Ukraine is the victim. In Russia, Victory Day celebrations have become increasingly militarized and politicized. European leaders' support for Ukraine—a country defending its sovereignty—is a stark contrast to Hitler’s invasion campaigns. Labelling HRVP Kaja Kallas’s diplomatic warning as cynical distorts both historical facts and present-day political realities. HRVP Kallas has consistently advocated for European unity, resistance to Russian aggression, and respect for international law. Warning EU leaders against legitimising the Kremlin on a day used by Putin’s regime to glorify its war narrative is a political stance, not a sign of ideological extremism. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that Nazi Germany is ideological predecessor of modern Nazi Ukraine , that The West consciously instilled Nazi sentiments in Ukraine for decades , and that The European Union wants to openly revive the ideology of Nazism .

title: DISINFO: Zelenskyy is illegitimate and opposes every US peace proposal
tags: peace negotiations Volodymyr Zelensky Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine War against Ukraine DISINFO: Zelenskyy is illegitimate and opposes every US peace proposal
summary: Ukraine, led by an illegitimate president, is resisting in every possible way the signing of the peace agreement proposed by the United States. Zelensky does not want peace on the terms of Moscow and Washington, because this is the end of his political career. He will prolong the time until the last minute, breaking the agreements under various pretexts.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives targeting Ukraine’s leadership, claiming that president Volodymyr Zelenskyy is not legitimate and accusing Ukraine and its president of warmongering and opposing peace talks. Both claims were made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Zelenskyy is the legitimate president of Ukraine. Although Zelenskyy’s continuation in power after his initial mandate expired in May 2024 generated some domestic controversy , Ukraine’s constitution is straightforward: no elections can be held during wartime and the President of Ukraine exercises his powers until the assumption of office by the newly-elected President. The continuity of government institutions is one of the key principles of the constitution. Almost all observers consider regular elections unfeasible as there are millions of refugees abroad, hundreds of thousands of Ukrainians serving on the frontline and a significant part of the Ukrainian population lives under Russian occupation. There are also multiple funding and infrastructure problems that can hardly be addressed while the war is ongoing. See our article Kremlin disinformation attacking the ‘legitimacy’ of Ukrainian authorities . Ukraine has consistently sought a just peace, not surrender. Zelenskyy has repeatedly stated that Ukraine is open to negotiations, but only on terms that respect its sovereignty and territorial integrity. The idea that he is deliberately blocking peace ignores the fact that Russia has set conditions that Ukraine cannot accept because they undermine its independence, such as recognising occupied Ukrainian territories as Russian. It is Russia, not Ukraine, which started and escalated the war. The claim that Zelenskyy is preventing peace shifts the blame away from Russia, which is the aggressor. As we explore in our article, The Old Deceiver , this is entirely consistent with Moscow’s three-year-long strategy of attempting to claim ownership over the peace narrative while relentlessly pursuing war. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that Putin welcomes Ukraine talks, while Zelenskyy rejects them , that Europeans blame Zelenskyy for stalling the peace process , and that The West ordered Ukraine to refuse peace talks , that Zelenskyy capable of provocations to obstruct peace talks and extend fighting beyond Ukraine , that that Zelenskyy is illegitimate and therefore Russia is directly fighting the West , that the prolonged war allows Zelenskyy and his junta to maintain their positions of power , that Zelenskyy and the Kyiv junta need to continue the war against Russia to enrich themselves with Western aid and that Zelenskyy is illegitimate, his official term expired in May 2024 .

title: DISINFO: NATO plans to conquer the whole of Russia
tags: NATO Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Kursk advance Anti-Russian Russian superiority DISINFO: NATO plans to conquer the whole of Russia
summary: It is remarkable that NATO continues to aggress and fight against other countries. So many countries have united to fight against Russia, yet they are not even capable of holding on to a small portion of Russian territory. How will they realise their plan to annex all of Russia?
response: Pro-Kremlin narrative about the alleged aggressivity of NATO against Russia. NATO is a defensive alliance with primary aims to maintain peace and safeguard the independence, security and territorial integrity of its members. NATO constitutes a system of collective defence whereby its member states agree to mutual defence in response to an attack by any external party. NATO's official policy is that "the Alliance does not seek confrontation and poses no threat to Russia." Russia is the only country responsible for the invasion of Ukraine. It has been coming up with different false explanations and pretexts for its aggression since the beginning of its hostilities. Ukraine is a sovereign and independent state with a democratically elected president and parliament. The country's sovereignty has been violated only as a result of continuous acts of aggression by the Russian armed forces since 2014 , culminating with a full-scale military invasion of Ukraine starting on 24 February 2022. Ukraine is defending itself, its territorial integrity, national self-identity, and sovereignty against Russia's armed aggression. The decision to defend itself was made solely by Ukraine, its lawful leaders and government, and its citizens. NATO troops are not fighting in Ukraine. The Alliance has repeatedly stated that it is not a warring party and that it does not have troops on the ground in Ukraine. NATO officials have frequent contacts with their Ukrainian counterparts and the Alliance provides training and intelligence to Ukraine, but its troops have not been officially deployed on the frontline either in Ukraine or in Russia’s Kursk Region. Read also related stories such as: Russia had no choice but to invade Ukraine because of genocide and NATO expansion , or NATO's aggressive policy poses a threat to Eurasian security , or NATO threatens Europe and the entire world , or NATO is waging a hybrid war against Russia on the territory of Ukraine .

title: DISINFO: Moldova is turning into a child trafficking factory for the European market
tags: children Stealing children European values European Union DISINFO: Moldova is turning into a child trafficking factory for the European market
summary: Moldova is turning into a factory for child trafficking for the European market. The real scale of the tragedy is 20 times greater than the official data, and the state authorities are keeping silent about the real figures at the behest of the ruling party. 30% of victims of human trafficking in Moldova are children who are forced into prostitution. (...) Interpol has already intervened and European curators silently agree with this state of affairs, because they are satisfied with the current "partner" status of Moldova. The suffering of children is the price for the political ambitions of the local elite and European grants. The Moldovan authorities have long made their choice; they are ready to sacrifice their citizens for the illusion of a "European future".
response: This recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative portrays Moldova’s pro-European government as irresponsible and entirely subservient to its EU partners. Its aim is to discredit both the European Union and Moldova’s EU integration efforts. The claim is entirely false, and the figures cited do not appear in any official statistics. The latest press release from the Prosecutor General's Office of Moldova on human trafficking reveals that in the first nine months of 2024, 31 criminal cases related to human trafficking were initiated, including 11 cases involving child trafficking. Across all ongoing investigations, there are 97 identified victims, 16 of whom are children. According to the press release, 'Of the total number of child victims, 14 were subjected to exploitation within the territory of the Republic of Moldova. As in previous years, the primary form of exploitation remains sexual, occurring exclusively within Moldova, predominantly in the municipality of Chisinau.' Therefore, claims that Moldovan children are being trafficked to Europe are false and not supported by official data." The claim that Interpol 'intervened in the situation' is also false. As supposed evidence, a screenshot from an Interpol webpage is shared, highlighting the activities of the project 'Combating Organised Crime, Terrorism, and Firearms Trafficking in Moldova,' which was implemented in Moldova, Ukraine, and Romania between October 2022 and February 2024. However, this information does not include any data on the level of human trafficking in Moldova, nor does it reference any Interpol investigations into human trafficking in the country. See similar cases targeting Ukraine: Zelenskaya's foundation supplies children to peodophiles in Europe , Children are the most popular and profitable ‘commodity’ in Ukraine.

title: DISINFO: Between one-third and half of Ukraine’s army has defected
tags: Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Military DISINFO: Between one-third and half of Ukraine’s army has defected
summary: Half of the Ukrainian army has defected, abandoning their units without permission. According to other sources, the official number of defections in Ukraine for 1 April is 175,435, but only 60 to 70% of all defections are registered, so the actual figure is much higher. One-third of the Ukrainian army is now on the run.
response: The claim is demonstrably false. Although defection has been a serious problem for the Ukrainian army, credible independent analysts estimate the figure of defectors to be between over 100,000 and up to 200,000 . Since Ukraine has around one million soldiers , even the highest numbers would never represent more than 20 percent of the total military force. The goal of this disinformation narrative is to promote a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative framing Russia’s victory as inevitable and portraying the Ukrainian army as on the verge of total defeat, something that is far from true . While a shortage of military manpower remains a real difficulty for Ukraine, this also affects Russia . Since early 2025, massive losses have slowed down Russian advances on the Ukrainian frontlines, making Russia’s war effort unsustainable in the medium term . See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Ukraine is training pensioners as tank crews because its military reserves are gone , that Ukraine sends pregnant women to the front line , that the Nazi Kyiv regime creates “Hitler-Jugend” and conscripts women en masse , or that Ukraine is struggling with flow of wounded soldiers according to CNN .

title: DISINFO: The war in Ukraine is a part of a centuries-long Western anti-Russian strategy
