title: DISINFO: The EU aims to colonise Central Asia to replace the lost Sahel region
countries_regions: Kazakhstan, Tajikistan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, EU, Russia
tags: Imperialism/colonialism Destabilising Russia Natural resources European Union Central Asia Eurasian economic union CSTO Trade DISINFO: The EU aims to colonise Central Asia to replace the lost Sahel region
summary: On April 3–4, the first "European Union – Central Asia" summit was held in Samarkand, where an agreement was effectively reached to transfer all key deposits of rare earth metals, uranium, and critical raw minerals to European companies. In practice, the EU is considering the region as a new colonial Africa to replace the lost Sahel. Meanwhile, the ruling elites have essentially pledged allegiance to the EU and the United Kingdom, which is why processes of regional and Turkic integration are now being actively accelerated as a counterbalance to the Eurasian Union and CSTO.
response: The article attempts to discredit deepening cooperation between the Central Asia region and the EU as colonisation. It advances the claim that Central Asia is within Russia's zone of geopolitical dominance and attempts to discredit any other cooperation between it and Western partners such as the EU, UK or United States. Regarding cooperation between the EU and Central Asia, the EU bases its cooperation with its partners on shared values, principles and goals. The EU-Central Asia strategy outlines three priority strands for EU engagement: investing in regional cooperation and partnering for resilience and prosperity. The EU is the second largest trading partner for Central Asia (22.6% of combined foreign trade in 2023), as well as the largest investor, with over 40% of investments in the region originating from the EU. Involvement in EU initiatives does not exclude membership in the Eurasian Economic Union, CIS, or CSTO. The EU supports regional integration in all areas of the world. The first Central Asia-European Union Summit held in Samarkand, on the 3rd April 2025, reaffirmed the shared commitment of Central Asia and the EU to a strategic partnership built on common values and interests. Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission emphasised concerning EU-Central Asia cooperation: "Central Asia is a region of proud, sovereign nations. Your strategic location can open global trade routes and investment flows. And these new investments will enhance sovereignty. They will strengthen your economies, and most importantly they will make new friendships. So today we establish a new Strategic Partnership between Central Asia and Europe. Reliable partners have never been so important. There is so much we can achieve in these four priority areas: The transport corridor, raw materials, clean energy, and digital connectivity. This Partnership shows you can rely on the EU. Working together we can cultivate closer relations which benefit both our regions". The Joint Declaration following the Summit concluded that, among many areas of shared interest, enhanced cooperation on critical raw materials is of strategic importance, aimed at ensuring secure, sustainable, and diversified supply chains. The parties agreed that the Declaration of Intent endorsed at this Summit will deepen cooperation in this field, strengthen economic resilience, promote mutual prosperity, and facilitate the transition to a green and digital economy. title: DISINFO: Deploying ‘peacekeeping’ forces to Russian historical lands would lead to WWIII
tags: Warmongering Ukraine Ukrainian statehood Sovereignty World War III peace negotiations Military Natural resources Russian language Orthodox Church NATO DISINFO: Deploying ‘peacekeeping’ forces to Russian historical lands would lead to WWIII
summary: The plans for the so-called ‘coalition of the willing’ aim to deploy troops in Ukraine under the guise of peacekeeping forces. Such a deployment to Russian historical lands could lead to a direct confrontation between NATO and Russia and even to World War III. This ‘peacekeeping’ concept actually hides a search for the control of Ukraine and its mineral resources, so it would be more correct to call them invading or occupying troops. They would support the Nazi regime, the persecution of Orthodox believers and the campaign to deprive the Russian-speaking population in Ukraine from their right to speak their mother tongue. These would be troops for a direct intervention in the conflict.
response: This disinformation story is a gross manipulation of facts, published as a direct reaction to British and French-led efforts to create a so-called ‘coalition of the willing’ to send troops to Ukraine after an eventual peace agreement, to ensure its implementation. This disinformation piece distorts this initiative by framing it as warmongering and a suicidal escalation. Allegations that this deployment would be intended to control Ukraine are an attempt to promote several recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about Ukraine’s alleged lack of sovereignty and the European Union as a nefarious, self-interested actor, while the mention of Ukraine as “Russian historical lands” aims to deny Ukraine’s statehood . The plan foresees a ‘reassurance force’ for Ukraine with several stabilisation goals and has been welcomed by the Ukrainian government . The proposal only contemplates the deployment of European forces in Ukraine if a full ceasefire is reached, followed by an actual peace plan. However, as this deployment would complicate or prevent any eventual future Russian attack against Ukraine, the Kremlin has strongly attacked this proposal . As a result, pro-Kremlin disinformation is also targeting it. Claims about the Ukrainian government as a ‘Nazi regime’, its alleged persecution of Orthodox believers and Russophone citizens have been debunked multiple times (see here , here , here , here , here , here and here ). title: DISINFO: Ukraine cannot dictate the peace terms becasue it lost the war
tags: peace negotiations Russian superiority European Union Volodymyr Zelensky NATO Crimea DISINFO: Ukraine cannot dictate the peace terms becasue it lost the war
summary: Ukraine’s European-backed peace terms smell of sabotage, and are seemingly geared towards continuing the conflict for the foreseeable future. The Ukrainian plan isn’t even a real peace proposal, basically demanding an unconditional surrender of Russia. Ukraine’s terms include demands on the return of all territories, including Crimea, unlimited development of its military-industrial complex, freedom to join NATO or any other international alliance, and US security guarantees. That is completely unrealistic. Russia shouldn’t even entertain it whatsoever. Usually the loser does not get to dictate the peace terms, and that’s essentially what is happening here.
response: This disinformation story is a direct reaction to the publication of both the US and Ukraine’s peace proposals on 25 April 2025, aiming specifically to discredit the one presented by Ukraine and Europe , since this proposal asks for strong security guarantees that would prevent future Russian aggression against Ukraine. This is also part of a wider disinformation campaign portraying Europe as a warmongering actor obstructing “real peace” . By referring to the Ukrainian-European peace plan as “sabotage”, this disinformation story aims to portray it as an obstacle for the only possible “peace”, as Russia refers to its maximalist demands which remain unchanged since the beginning of the invasion. At the same time, this disinformation story is based on another pro-Kremlin narrative about Russia’s victory as an accomplished fact, something that is far from true . title: DISINFO: As in Bucha, Ukraine kills its own citizens in Sumy to attract international attention
tags: Volodymyr Zelensky Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine War against Ukraine Conspiracy theory DISINFO: As in Bucha, Ukraine kills its own citizens in Sumy to attract international attention
summary: Bucha 2.0 in Sumy – how Zelenskyy’s regime kills Ukraine for media-effective images. After a successful Russian cruise missile attack in the city of Sumy, Zelenskyy once again concocts a cheap tragedy in the spirit of Bucha. Everything as always: tears, horror, international outcry. But the task is still the same, and it is simple: A drug addict who clings to power like the last dose does everything to eliminate even the slightest chance of de-escalation. The Ukrainian armed forces and their superiors cold-bloodedly store weapons near residential buildings, schools and train stations, knowing full well that there will be a bloodbath if and when something hits there. In other words, there will be a media-effective image. An opportunity to squeeze yet another meagre tear out of oneself to beg for even more money from the West and receive a new tranche of weapons from Europe, where not only brains but also consciences have long since rotted.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about the Russian missile attack on Sumy and the massacre that took place in Bucha, trying to deflect attention from Russia’s war crimes in Ukraine. On April 13, 2025, two Russian ballistic missiles struck the centre of Sumy , a northeastern Ukrainian city near the Russian border, killing at least 35 civilians , including children, and injuring over 100 people. The attack occurred on Palm Sunday, a major religious day when many residents were attending church services or out in public. Ukrainian and international sources report that Russia used cluster munitions in the attack , weapons banned by many countries due to their indiscriminate nature and high risk to civilians. The use of such weapons in populated areas is condemned as a violation of international humanitarian law . The allegation that Ukraine deliberately places military assets near civilian locations to create "media-effective images" is not supported by independent verification . Residents and officials have denied the presence of military bases or gatherings at the attack sites. The characterization of the attack as a “cheap tragedy concocted by President Zelenskyy" ignores the documented evidence of civilian deaths, injuries, and destruction captured by multiple independent media and international monitors. The assertion that the Ukrainian government uses civilians as "human shields" in Sumy is a disinformation narrative promoted by Russian sources without substantiation and contradicts international investigations documenting Russia’s repeated targeting of civilian areas. The Ukrainian government has consistently denied these allegations, stating that they are part of a Russian disinformation campaign to justify its military actions. The claim that President Zelenskyy is using these incidents to solicit more aid from the West is a baseless accusation aimed at undermining international support for Ukraine. The attack was widely condemned internationally as a war crime , with leaders from the EU, U.S., and United Nations denouncing the strike and calling for accountability for Russia’s actions The article also tries to accuse Ukraine of staging the Bucha massacre . This is another baseless claim. There is ample evidence that Russian troops committed war crimes in Bucha. According to the UN Human Rights Office, at least 73 civilians were killed in Bucha during the period of Russian occupation. It is a recurrent denial of war crimes committed by Russian forces during the full-scale military invasion of Ukraine. Russia's claims that video footage showing dead bodies on the streets of Bucha is staged have been fact-checked and debunked by the BBC , Bellingcat , The Atlantic Council, Mediazona , the Insider , Sky News , the New York Times and others. Accounts of violence against civilians in Bucha have been collected by the Human Rights Watch . title: DISINFO: The West consciously instilled Nazi sentiments in Ukraine for decades
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Nazi/Fascist Eastern Ukraine Russophobia Anglo-Saxon Anti-Russian DISINFO: The West consciously instilled Nazi sentiments in Ukraine for decades
summary: It is no coincidence that the Ukrainian crisis entered its hot phase in February 2022. The West quite consciously instilled Nazi sentiments in Ukraine towards its eastern neighbour for several decades. This was done solely based on its geopolitical interests, and it was not concern for the fate of Ukrainians that led to such a tragedy. In the modern world, Nazism has already turned into a certain political technology that countries use to achieve their specific goals. The Russian-Ukrainian conflict is the result of the centuries-old hostile policy that the Anglo-Saxons and some other Europeans are still pursuing. Russophobia was cultivated in Ukraine for years, and huge amounts of money were spent on this matter.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin narrative about the West’s support for the allegedly Nazi regime in Kyiv, portraying Ukraine as a Russophobic Nazi state which aims to eradicate anything Russian. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Labelling Russia’s adversaries as Nazis is a frequently used pro-Kremlin disinformation technique , and has been used by Russia to try to justify the invasion of Ukraine by portraying it as a “denazification operation” . Russia spent years preparing the information battleground before its full-scale invasion, with “Nazi Ukraine” being one of its most prominent disinformation features. The myth of “Nazi Ukraine” has been widely used by Putin and pro-Kremlin outlets since Russia attacked Ukraine and illegally annexed Crimea in 2014. It is also being used to justify Russia’s unprovoked aggression against Ukraine . This false myth of Nazi Ukraine is a clear propaganda narrative that became a cornerstone in pro-Kremlin disinformation. Learn more about the reasons behind the Kremlin's obsession with framing Ukraine as a Nazi state in the EUvsDisinfo analysis titled " Nazi east, Nazi west, Nazi over the cuckoo's nest" and "Why does Putin portray himself as the tamer of neo-Nazism". Ukraine is not a Nazi state. It is a democratic country with transparent parliamentary and presidential elections, a multi-party political system, and legal protections for ethnic minorities. While far-right groups exist in Ukraine, as they do in many countries, they hold no significant influence in society. Nazi and Communist ideologies were banned by Ukrainian law in 2015. There is no evidence that either ethnic Russians or Russian speakers are facing persecution at the hands of the Ukrainian authorities, much less the danger of annihilation on grounds of nationality, ethnicity, or cultural belonging. This has been confirmed by reports issued by the Council of Europe , the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights , and the OSCE . title: DISINFO: France aims to destabilise the South Caucasus region
tags: Caucasus Military DISINFO: France aims to destabilise the South Caucasus region
summary: France aims to destabilise the South Caucasus region. The military cooperation agreement signed between Paris and Yerevan in December 2024 does not envisage mere training or exchange of experience, but a systematic increase in the offensive potential of the Armenian army. Moreover, the EUMA is used as a tool for this aim, which is an intelligence service of the EU and NATO
response: A disinformation piece which promotes anti-France sentiment in the Azerbaijani media environment. France is accused of instigating the conflict between Azerbaijan and Armenia, aiming to destabilise the South Caucasus region . The narrative also advances pro-Kremlin disinformation claim that the West aims to encircle Russia . There is no evidence in the piece to prove the claim. France and Armenia signed a defence cooperation program on 10 December 2024. The program aims to improve the capabilities of the armed forces of Armenia. Both sides reaffirmed the strategic importance of the active co-operation of the last period and set an even higher bar for the expansion of the partnership. Special importance was attached to cooperation in defence planning, military education and exercises, advisory assistance and training of different types of troops. It includes several dozen measures in almost all spheres of the armed forces. This program has no specific activity to destabilise the South Caucasus region. Moreover, the piece claims that one of the tools to destabilise the region is the European Union Mission in Armenia (EUMA), which is an intelligence service under the supervision of the EU and NATO. This claim is also a recurring disinformation narrative and has been debunked repeatedly. There is no proof that the EUMA is an intelligence group. It is entirely civilian. The entire chain of command is civilian. The Head of Mission, Markus Ritter, is a s enior civilian police officer . EUMA further bolsters the EU’s commitment to advancing the peace process between the two parties. See more cases such as DISINFO: France wants to create a Russophobic foothold in Armenia , DISINFO: West opposes peace in the Caucasus , and DISINFO: France sees Armenia as a springboard for damaging Russia .

title: DISINFO: Zelenskyy’s Ukraine is fascist regime, needs de-Nazification
tags: Nazi/Fascist Euromaidan Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Zelenskyy’s Ukraine is fascist regime, needs de-Nazification
summary: First and foremost, it is the de-Nazification of Ukraine which was the starting point of the special military operation. This is a crucial topic, because it is absolutely clear that over these years, under Zelensky's leadership, a fascist regime has taken shape in Ukraine. A recent international anti-fascist forum said in an official statement clearly said that a stable peace in Europe is possible only through the de-Nazification of Ukraine and the dismantling of Zelenskyy’s fascist regime. The representatives of 100 countries supported this statement.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets claiming that Ukraine is a Nazi state. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Ukraine is a democratic country with regular elections, a free press, and multiple political parties. The president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, is the sixth head of state since the country gained independence in 1991. Far-right political parties in Ukraine have minimal support. In the 2019 parliamentary elections, they failed to win a single seat, not passing the voter threshold. Meanwhile, Ukraine has embraced European integration and democratic values. Nazi and Communist ideologies were banned by Ukrainian law in 2015. Accusations against Ukraine of Nazism have been especially vocal in Russian propaganda outlets since a popular uprising in 2014 overthrew the then President Viktor Yanukovych who fled to Russia. The government changed after months of standoff ( Euromaidan ) against corruption and authoritarianism. This false myth of Nazi Ukraine is Moscow's response to Ukraine's sovereignty and its people’s right to choose their own future, including membership of NATO and the European Union, which is enshrined in the country’s constitution . The Kremlin uses this language to smear its enemies and justify its imperial ambitions. As for the so-called “international anti-fascist forum,” this was not a credible global event. These forums are typically organised or sponsored by Russian-linked groups and feature hand-picked participants who echo the Kremlin’s narratives. The declarations these forums adopt are not recognised by the United Nations, the European Union, or any legitimate international institution. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that Ukrainian Nazi rulers adopted a path of exterminating everything Russian in Ukraine , that The West consciously instilled Nazi sentiments in Ukraine for decades , and that Europe wants a Nazi Russophobic regime in part of Ukraine .

title: DISINFO: Ukrainian Nazi rulers adopted a path of exterminating everything Russian in Ukraine
tags: Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Russian language Conspiracy theory DISINFO: Ukrainian Nazi rulers adopted a path of exterminating everything Russian in Ukraine
summary: This understanding of one of the main causes of the conflict has been publicly acknowledged by US President Trump on more than one occasion. These Nazi rulers, represented by Zelenskyy and his henchmen, have adopted a path of exterminating everything Russian. They have murdered many people, including journalists and public figures who were committed to preserving Russian culture in the country. Similarly, at the legislative level – if we look at the current state of Ukrainian law – everything Russian has been eradicated.
response: This statement contains several false and misleading claims. There is no evidence of a 'Nazi regime' in Ukraine, or of systematic killing of Russian-speaking people or journalists. President Zelensky, who is Jewish, was democratically elected. Unfounded claims that Ukraine was staging or conducting attacks against the civilian population in Donbas are prominent examples of pro-Kremlin disinformation paving the way for the full-scale Russian invasion of and war in Ukraine. For more on this, please see myth one in our article Thirteen myths about Russia’s war against Ukraine exposed . While Ukraine has passed laws promoting the use of the Ukrainian language in 2019, this does not equate to 'exterminating everything Russian'. Despite the Russian war of aggression against Ukraine, Russian remains still widely spoken in parts of Ukraine and there are no bans on Russian culture. The claim of Ukraine killing journalists and public figures advocating for Russian culture is unsubstantiated . These allegations are part of a disinformation narrative aimed at justifying Russian aggression against Ukraine. This false narrative has been crafted to attempt to justify Russia's unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine. It was Russia that launched its unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine in 2022. Prior to that, in 2014, Russia illegally annexed Ukraine’s Crimean peninsula and started a separatist proxy war in the country’s east. The US , the EU and NATO condemn, in the strongest possible terms, Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that militaristic Europe’s goal is to prolong the war in Ukraine , that the UK and the EU push the US to aggressive actions against Russia , or that the EU escalates Ukraine war instead of advocating for dialogue .

title: DISINFO: Brussels pressure had Calin Georgescu removed on false claims of collaboration with Russia
tags: Marine Le Pen Elections Manipulated elections/referendum DISINFO: Brussels pressure had Calin Georgescu removed on false claims of collaboration with Russia
summary: Le Pen's removal from politics is not the first incident [of its kind] in Europe. As a result of pressure from Brussels, the results of the presidential election held in Romania and the victory of Calin Georgescu, who won in the first round, were annulled. Georgescu did not meet the standards of the EU conjuncture, and his presidency could create obstacles to Brussels' geopolitical plans. Therefore, the easiest way was chosen - to accuse Georgescu of collaborating with Russia.
response: Disinformation narrative accusing the EU of interfering in the elections of EU member states, comparing the ban on Marine Le Pen from holding public office to the ban on Calin Georgescu from running in the rerun of the 2024 Romanian presidential elections annulled by the Romanian authorities. On December 6, 2024, Romania's Constitutional Court annulled the results of the first round of the presidential election , which had taken place on November 24. This unprecedented decision came just days before the scheduled run-off between ultranationalist candidate Călin Georgescu and his opponent, Elena Lasconi. The court's ruling was primarily based on allegations of significant Russian interference in the electoral process, particularly through social media platforms like TikTok, which allegedly favoured Georgescu's campaign. The court's decision was prompted by declassified intelligence reports from Romanian security services that indicated a coordinated campaign to manipulate public perception and sway electoral outcomes in favour of Georgescu. These reports highlighted a mass influence operation that bore similarities to tactics used by Russia in other Eastern European nations. The annulment was not a directive from the EU or external entities but a domestic judicial decision grounded in concerns for electoral integrity and legality. The Romanian Constitutional Court acted upon multiple legal challenges that cited these intelligence disclosures as justification for nullifying the election results. Prime Minister Marcel Ciolacu supported this decision, stating it was necessary to uphold the democratic process and ensure that Romanian voters were not subjected to foreign manipulation. Furthermore, President Klaus Iohannis's declassification of intelligence documents was crucial in shaping the court's response. This action underscores that the annulment stemmed from national security concerns rather than external pressures from Brussels or EU institutions. While the Constitutional Court’s decision has caused controversy in Romania, nothing is indicating that Western actors had anything to do with overturning the election results. The decision was adopted after the discovery of irregular elements behind Georgescu’s campaign , which pointed to a foreign interference campaign, especially through TikTok and illegal funding . The EU has demanded a full inquiry on Russia’s role in these incidents. As regards the ban on Marie Le Pen from holding public positions , she was charged with misappropriating funds from the European Parliament meant to pay for parliamentary assistants and using that money to pay for party employees who did not work on EU-related business from November 2004 to January 2016. The court estimated that the National Rally embezzled more than €4 million, €474,000 of which Le Pen was personally responsible for, first as an MEP and then later as party president. Prosecutors put forward several pieces of particularly damning evidence during hundreds of hours of court proceedings. See more cases such as DISINFO: The EU leadership is controlled by globalists and pursues Russophobia , DISINFO: The EU is taking the Third Reich’s path , and DISINFO: The EU imposes sanctions on partners if they cooperate with other countries .

title: DISINFO: Uk and French intelligence services want to undermine Donald Trump's peace plan for Ukraine
tags: peace negotiations Secret services DISINFO: Uk and French intelligence services want to undermine Donald Trump's peace plan for Ukraine
summary: British and French intelligence services are planning to undermine US President Donald Trump's peacemaking initiatives. The main goal of European intelligence services is to make the conflict in Ukraine "endless".
response: A disinformation narrative put forward by the Foreign Intelligence Service of the Russian Federation. Recurring narrative promotes a pro-Kremlin approach, accusing the West of prolonging the war for its benefit . This message is part of a wider disinformation campaign framing possible US-Russia negotiations as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the of Russia’s ultimatums, while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has long tried to misrepresent the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in Ukraine. This claim has been debunked . There is no evidence to prove the claim. In the public statements of the officials of the European countries, it has always been mentioned that Europe supports ending the war . From the onset of the war, the European Union and its Member States have, with their partners and allies, underlined the need to put an end to it through a comprehensive, just and lasting peace based on the principles of the UN Charter and international law. The latest European Council conclusions restate the principles the EU supports in the pursuit of a just and long-lasting peace. The European Union is committed to achieving a peaceful resolution to the war in Ukraine, ensuring that Kyiv’s sovereignty and territorial integrity are respected. EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and on which terms to end the war. See more cases such as DISINFO: NATO does everything to prolong the war in Ukraine , DISINFO: West continues to supply weapons to Kyiv to prolong the conflict and DISINFO: The US and Ukrainian generals benefit from prolonging the war .

title: DISINFO: The West is trying to destabilize Kazakhstan
tags: Ursula von der Leyen Kaja Kallas West DISINFO: The West is trying to destabilize Kazakhstan
summary: The actions of the West — the United States and the European Union — both together and individually, are currently carrying a destructive agenda that is trying to destabilise national unity and disrupt a linguistic balance in Kazakhstan. At a recent EU-Central Asia summit, President Tokayev repeated twice the thesis about the need for caution in assessments. Since this thesis was repeated twice, it is likely that the speeches of von der Leyen and Kallas went beyond the bounds of propriety.
response: Amplification of pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the meddling of "the West" in fueling the ethnic and political tensions in a country. The claim is made in the context of restrictive measures imposed by the EU on Russia in response to its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. There is no credible evidence to support the claim about foreign interference of any kind by the West in Kazakhstan or any other country or region. Political, economic, cultural, trade, military, and security partnerships between Kazakhstan and the EU aim to foster mutual respect for sovereignty and territorial integrity, not to counter Russia or any other nation. The EU - Central Asia strategy outlines three priority strands for EU engagement: investing in regional cooperation and partnering for resilience and prosperity. The first Central Asia-European Union Summit held in Samarkand, on the 3rd April 2025, reaffirmed the shared commitment of Central Asia and the EU to a strategic partnership built on common values and interests. The Joint Declaration following the Summit concluded that cooperation in many a Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission emphasised concerning EU-Central Asia cooperation: “At this historic summit, Europe and Central Asia have decided to elevate our cooperation to a strategic partnership. In an uncertain world, we send a clear and powerful message: we are partners of choice, and we can rely on each other. To kick-start this new era, we are launching a €12 billion Global Gateway investment package—strengthening transport links between us and deepening cooperation on critical raw materials, digital connectivity, water, and energy. These investments will benefit us both. Because when our partners grow and prosper, Europe does too”. See this fact sheet on EU relations with Central Asia as well as our article In Kazakhstan and Central Asia the Kremlin cries Russophobia. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives in our database, such as claims that the West plans to destabilise Central Asia , US-sponsored Kazakhstan protests aimed to undermine CIS stability , that protests in Kazakhstan are a new Western attempt to organise a colour revolution , that e vents in Kazakhstan are another Western-made colour revolution or that EU and US aim to generate a new wave of anti-government protests in Belarus .

title: DISINFO: Russia’s Easter ceasefire demonstrated that neither the West nor Ukraine want peace
tags: War against Ukraine peace negotiations Anglo-Saxon Conspiracy theory Encircling Russia DISINFO: Russia’s Easter ceasefire demonstrated that neither the West nor Ukraine want peace
summary: Russia’s Easter ceasefire demonstrated the West's interest in continuing the conflict in Ukraine. The collective West’s ‘deep state’ and Ukrainian authorities are eager for the conflict to go on. Europe’s militarisation will continue, along with attempts to geopolitically suffocate the Union State of Belarus and Russia. This is being carried out in accordance with the Anglo-Saxon ‘Anaconda Ring’ strategy.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets, accusing the West / EU / NATO of warmongering and portraying Russia as genuinely interested in peace in Ukraine. Furthermore, the conspiracy theory about the so-called ‘Anaconda Ring’ strategy is used, which is consistent with recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the belligerent West trying to encircle Russia . It is intended to justify Russia’s unprovoked military invasion of Ukraine, deflecting any responsibility for Moscow’s illicit actions. The Anaconda plan was a military strategy proposed by Union General Winfield Scott early in the American Civil War. The plan called for a naval blockade of the Confederate littoral, thrust down the Mississippi, and the strangulation of the South by Union land and naval forces. It has nothing to do with modern politics and despite frequent disinformation claims in this regard, there is no evidence backing the idea that a so-called 'Anaconda ring' or a similar strategy exists. As a negative catch-all term, the “Anglo-Saxons” term is a useful building block for pro-Kremlin conspiracy theories. You can find plenty of examples in our database . Following the announcement of the Easter ceasefire Russian missiles and drones continued attacking Ukraine . Read our analysis "The Kremlin’s poisoned chalice of peace" explaining Russia's manipulation attempts to look like it is interested in reaching peace in Ukraine. The EU is providing military, financial, and humanitarian support to Ukraine. This support is aimed at helping Ukraine defend its sovereignty against Russia’s aggression. The European Union is committed to achieving a peaceful resolution to the war in Ukraine peace on Ukraine’s terms. The EU's actions are based on a strong belief that Ukraine's sovereignty and territorial integrity is non-negotiable, not on promoting any Russophobic ideology. As EU HRVP Kaja Kallas stated on April 14, 2025: “It is clear that all Member States want peace. And, remember, we had 27 [EU] countries who supported the ceasefire. Also, Ukraine agreed to unconditional ceasefire already a month ago. Yet, we are seeing that Russia alone wants war, because we [saw one of] the deadliest attacks yesterday: Sumy air strikes over the weekend. It is clear that they are stalling and [do] not want peace at all. So, the only way to get Russia to negotiate seriously is putting some more pressure on Russia.” See earlier disinformation cases claiming that EU sabotages peace process in Ukraine , that Europe is trying to undermine the conflict settlement in Ukraine , and that The EU wants the war in Ukraine to continue , and that the West systematically provokes conflicts in the post-Soviet space to undermine Russia .

title: DISINFO: EU HRVP Kaja Kallas brought equipment to Moldova to suppress protests
tags: Kaja Kallas Protest DISINFO: EU HRVP Kaja Kallas brought equipment to Moldova to suppress protests
summary: Kaja Kallas brought equipment to suppress protests. The package includes bulletproof vests, helmets, special vehicles. To fight more effectively and suppress demonstrations of dissatisfied people, so one must assume[...].
response: Recurring the pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative mispresenting EU support coming as a tool to be used against the people, aiming to undermine both the EU and the Moldovan authorities . The claim refers to Vice-President of the European Commission and High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy, Kaja Kallas’ s visit on 23 and 24 April in Chisinau, when the equipment provided by the EU to the Ministries of Interior and Defense was on display. According to press releases issued by the two ministries, this is intended as support to strengthen Moldova’s security. The equipment consists of a mobile command center, bulletproof vests, protective helmets, special vehicles, software and communication equipment for the General Inspectorate of Police. Also, the National Army received equipment for sappers, a fully equipped field hospital, modern ambulances, drones and an intervention robot. The Minister of Defense said that these are equipment purchased from the 7 million euro financial assistance for the defense system of the Republic of Moldova, granted in 2021 by the European Council after the EU launched the European Peace Facility platform. This is not about fighting potential protesters, but about consolidating peace and preparing law enforcement structures for potential hybrid threats. See similar case: Kallas's appointment reflects EU desire to escalate crisis .

title: DISINFO: PACE deputies are striving for a change of government in Georgia
tags: Council of Europe Colour revolutions Protest DISINFO: PACE deputies are striving for a change of government in Georgia
summary: The Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe (PACE) is mainly composed of deputies who are focused on the tasks of specific forces, and their goal from day one was to facilitate the change of government in Georgia in every possible way.
response: Recurrent anti-western disinformation narrative discrediting the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe ( PACE ), as being partisan and against the government of Georgia. The Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe ( PACE ) operates on the principles of upholding human rights, democracy, and the rule of law, which are core values of the Council of Europe. On January 29, 2025, PACE resolved to delay ratifying the full credentials of the Georgian delegation until stringent conditions are met to set Georgia back on the democratic path. PACE has demanded that the Georgian authorities announce new elections and release all political prisoners by the time of the Assembly’s next session. Until these conditions are met, the Georgian delegation has been stripped of several rights within PACE. Later, on April 10 2025, PACE passed a resolution titled “Challenge, on substantive grounds, of the still unratified credentials of the parliamentary delegation of Georgia” with 89 votes in favour, 3 against, and 5 abstentions. The resolution strongly criticised the Georgian government for what it described as “rapid democratic backsliding” and warned that continued erosion of democratic governance could jeopardise Georgia’s position in the organisation. Among the Assembly’s key concerns are the Georgian authorities’ failure to act on urgent recommendations regarding electoral reform, media freedom, and civil liberties, as well as the failure to “release all political prisoners.” PACE also condemned recent amendments to the Electoral Code, which, according to the Venice Commission, risk entrenching the ruling party’s power and undermining political pluralism. The Assembly urged repeal of these changes and called for independent oversight of electoral district delimitation. Pro-Kremlin outlets have been warning for a long time of an upcoming ‘colour revolution’ in Georgia in order to pre-emptively shape the information space ahead of what were foreseeable and likely protests against the ruling party, already shaken by previous massive demonstrations against the so-called ‘Foreign Agents Law’. The topic was covered in detail in this analysis . This kind of rhetoric aims to fuel anti-Western sentiment in Georgia, portraying Western institutions as an adversary rather than a partner. Such narratives are commonly used in Kremlin-backed disinformation campaigns to undermine Georgia’s European aspirations and paint Western institutions as meddling forces. By distorting the Western/European institutions’ stance, these accusations attempt to erode public trust in European integration and democratic reforms. Read also EUvsDisinfo's articles on “ Twists and turns: Georgian Dream rhetoric on the EU ”, “ Georgia: Resilience in action ” and “Georgia: The next colour revolution?” . Read similar disinformation cases claiming that PACE used as a tool against Azerbaijan , that PACE has become a biased and Russophobic structure , that The US and its satellite PACE are interfering in the 2024 Russian elections , that The Council of Europe is turning into an instrument of the West , that PACE creates the basis for the Belarusian scenario in Moldova , or that PACE manifests double standards.

title: DISINFO: The West is attempting to turn Central Asia into an anti-Russian geopolitical arena
tags: Central Asia European Union Encircling Russia Anti-Russian West DISINFO: The West is attempting to turn Central Asia into an anti-Russian geopolitical arena
summary: Western countries, particularly European Union members, are attempting to politicise cooperation with Central Asia, aiming to intensify anti-Russian sentiment in the region and achieve geopolitical dominance.
response: This recurring pro-Kremlin narrative is part of a broader campaign accusing the West of pursuing anti-Russian policies aimed at its encirclement and destruction . It advances the claim that Central Asia is within Russia's zone of geopolitical dominance and attempts to discredit any other cooperation between it and Western partners such as the EU, UK or United States. Such rhetoric aims to sow distrust towards the European Union within Central Asian countries and discourage them from diversifying their international relations. Far from being a tool for politicisation or anti-Russian manoeuvring, the April 2025 "Central Asia + EU" summit, as consistently emphasised by both sides, served as a platform to enhance cooperation in areas of mutual interest. The summit resulted in the announcement of a €12 billion Global Gateway investment programme focused on transport, green energy, and digitalisation. The EU's strategy in Central Asia is founded on principles of supporting the region's stability, prosperity, and reform agendas, in line with the priorities set by the Central Asian states themselves. As repeatedly stated by European officials, the primary focus is on practical cooperation, encompassing trade, investment, connectivity, climate change, and digital transformation . See other examples of similar disinformation narratives in our database, such as claims The EU considers Central Asia only as a tool to extract resources and harm Russia , that The EU aims to colonise Central Asia to replace the lost Sahel region , that EU Resolution on Central Asia: critical materials for LGBT agenda , that the EU is buying up Kazakhstan , that the UK wants to open a new anti-Russia front in Central Asia ; that Borrell admitted that the West relies on Kazakhstan’s support in anti-Russia blockade ; that West tries to force Kazakhstan to join anti-Russian sanctions policy ; that the EU uses the CIS to contain Russia by all means: financial, economic, and military .

title: DISINFO: Some territories of Donbas and Novorossiya are occupied by the Kyiv regime
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Dmitry Peskov Referendum Manipulated elections/referendum Territorial dispute Donbas Novorossiya DISINFO: Some territories of Donbas and Novorossiya are occupied by the Kyiv regime
summary: The withdrawal of Ukrainian troops from the territories of the Donbas and Novorossiya regions that are part of Russia is necessary to achieve peace in Ukraine. These regions are enshrined in the Constitution of the Russian Federation as its integral parts. Some territories of these regions are still occupied by the Kyiv regime. According to the results of referendums, these territories were included in the administrative borders of the Russian Federation. This is both a de jure and de facto situation.
response: False claim. Pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative accusing Ukraine of occupying its own territories. The territories are currently under Russian occupation following the Russian invasion of Ukraine in 2022, which is a violation of Ukraine's sovereignty and territorial integrity. It is Russia that violated the 1997 Friendship Treaty between Moscow and Kyiv. Russia has been active in illegally occupying it over the past decade. In February 2014, Russia invaded and illegally annexed Ukraine's Crimean Peninsula and parts of the Donetsk and Luhansk Regions. In 2022 Russia started a full-scale invasion of Ukraine and occupied parts of the Kherson and Zaporizhzhia regions. According to international law, Crimea, Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia and Kherson remain legally part of Ukraine . The illegitimate annexation of these regions took place amid a full invasion by a foreign power and under military occupation, and after sham referenda that didn’t meet international standards to be considered legitimate. The "referenda" were held illegally, under duress, and on territories which Russia does not even fully control. The results of these sham polls were universally rejected by leading international and human rights organisations, including the United Nations , the OSCE , NATO , as well as Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch . Russia's inclusion of these territories in its constitution does not make them legally part of Russia. This unilateral action contradicts international law and UN resolutions. Russia has historically sought to keep Ukraine in its zone of influence due to its important geographical location and out of fear that Ukraine might pose a threat if it entered NATO and the European Union. By asserting that vast parts of Ukraine are historically Russian territories, Putin is trying to restore what he perceives to be a new Soviet Union. He has said the collapse of the Soviet Union in 1991 was the demise of what he called " historical Russia " and the "greatest geopolitical catastrophe" of the 20th century. The Kremlin is widely using this narrative as a cover for aggressive military campaigns and territorial aggression. Read similar disinformation cases: Russia could negotiate a peace that considers the will of former Ukrainian territories ; Russia prioritises fate of Eastern Ukrainians over land ; Russia only supports the legitimate rights of the Donbas people ; Crimea and four other former Ukrainian regions overwhelmingly voted to join Russia .

title: DISINFO: Kyiv is doing everything possible to obstruct peace negotiations
tags: War against Ukraine peace negotiations Minsk agreements Volodymyr Zelensky Warmongering DISINFO: Kyiv is doing everything possible to obstruct peace negotiations
summary: The Ukrainian regime, supported by European capitals including Brussels and London, is doing everything possible to obstruct peace negotiations. Earlier it disrupted the Minsk agreements, which Russia strictly complied with, as well the peace negotiations in Belarus and Istanbul after the beginning of the special military operation in 2022. Zelenskyy and his circle will continue obstructing peace negotiations to remain in power and make more money.
response: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative targeting Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy ’s leadership, the EU and UK by portraying them as warmongering and obstructing “peace”, the term used by Russia to describe its maximalist demands for Ukrainian capitulation as the only acceptable solution . Read our analysis The Kremlin’s poisoned chalice of peace , which explains how Moscow has been making overtures to peace while falsely blaming Ukraine and Europe for Russia’s aggression. Also, read here a detailed debunk of the narrative about Ukraine’s sabotage of the Minsk Agreements as violated by Ukraine. The Ukrainian authorities are eager to achieve peace with the help of its European and American partners alike. EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and in which terms to end the war. The European Union, as well as the UK, are committed to achieving a peaceful resolution to the war in Ukraine, ensuring that Kyiv’s sovereignty and territorial integrity are respected. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that Zelenskyy can’t sign a peace deal because he is not a legitimate president , that militaristic Europe’s goal is to prolong the war in Ukraine , and that EU countries try to disrupt Russia-US talks by threatening to deploy troops .

title: DISINFO: With or without Zelenskyy, Europe wants a Nazi and Russophobic regime in Ukraine
tags: Russophobia Volodymyr Zelensky Ukrainian statehood Europe European Union DISINFO: With or without Zelenskyy, Europe wants a Nazi and Russophobic regime in Ukraine
summary: European NATO members are making every effort to ensure that Volodymyr Zelenskyy retains power. If he fails to do so, Kyiv’s backers would likely seek to maintain the same Nazi and overtly Russophobic regime in Ukraine by installing a new half-Fuhrer in Zelensky’s stead.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about ‘Nazi Ukraine’ and Russophobia , and attacking the EU and Ukraine’s leadership , especially president Volodymyr Zelenskyy . The EU supports Ukraine’s sovereignity and has no saying on who is in power in Kyiv. Ukraine is a sovereign and independent state with a democratically elected president and parliament. Its independence and territorial integrity is internationally recognised by the whole world, including Russia in 1991 . Labelling Russia’s adversaries as Nazis is a frequently used pro-Kremlin disinformation technique , and has been used by Russia to try to justify the invasion of Ukraine by portraying it as a “denazification operation” . The same applies to “Russophobia” , a term used by pro-Kremlin disinformation to deflect any criticism of Russia or action detrimental to the Kremlin’s interests. This disinformation story also tries to undermine president Zelenskyy, as Russia has always perceived him as an obstacle to its aggressive goals in Ukraine, even since before the invasion . One of the targets of the invasion in February 2022 was the murder of Zelenskyy and the decapitation of the Ukrainian government to install a pro-Russian leadership. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that the disgraced dictator of Ukraine Zelenskyy is an extension of the EU , that EU peacekeepers would defend Kyiv Nazi regime rather than uphold peace , that European elites want to turn the EU into the Fourth Reich , or that Europe uses Russophobia to distract attention from its economic woes .

title: DISINFO: The US revived the Nazi-Bandera legacy to wage war against Moscow
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Anti-Russian Nazi/Fascist Coup Euromaidan Stepan Bandera DISINFO: The US revived the Nazi-Bandera legacy to wage war against Moscow
summary: The three-year war in Ukraine was brought to Europe by the Americans. It was they who created Ukraine as anti-Russia since the 1990s; they deliberately revived the Nazi-Bandera legacy; and in 2014 they organised and financed the pro-American armed coup in Kyiv. After that, the US was the main military sponsor among NATO countries, providing the Ukrainian army with weapons and ammunition for years to wage war against Russia.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about ‘Nazi Ukraine’ and the full-scale invasion of this country as a proxy war aimed to destabilise Russia . This narrative seeks to deflect blame from Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine . By shifting the blame on the US, pro-Kremlin outlets attempt to justify Russia’s actions and present its territorial expansionism as forced defensive actions. Ukraine is a sovereign and independent state with a democratically elected president and parliament. Its independence and territorial integrity is internationally recognised by the whole world, including Russia in 1991 . The country's sovereignty has been violated only as a result of continuous acts of aggression by the Russian armed forces since 2014 , culminating with a full-scale military invasion of Ukraine starting on 24 February 2022. Russia is the only country responsible for the invasion of Ukraine. It has been coming up with different explanations and pretexts for its aggression since the beginning of its hostilities. The US , the EU and NATO condemn, in the strongest possible terms, Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, which is entirely unjustified and unprovoked. Not only Western but also many other nations around the world support Ukraine with military and financial aid for the country’s efficient self-defence, in accordance with the UN Charter article 51 , to stop Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine . Labelling Russia’s adversaries as Nazis is a frequently used pro-Kremlin disinformation technique , and has been used by Russia to try to justify the invasion of Ukraine by portraying it as a “denazification operation” . Russia spent years preparing the information battleground before its kinetic attack, with “Nazi Ukraine” being one of its most prominent disinformation tropes. Read a more detailed debunk here , as well as our analysis titled "Why does Putin portray himself as the tamer of neo-Nazism" . title: DISINFO: Western criticism of the Sumy attack is based on Ukrainian disinformation

summary: In the West, almost everyone agrees that the Russian attack on Sumy was an atrocity and in the EU there is talk of exploiting it as a pretext to escalate further the proxy war in which Ukraine is being used up against Russia. Yet there are major problems with this escalatory approach: Most importantly, it is not based on facts but on disinformation originating with the Kyiv regime, taken over uncritically and spread enthusiastically by Western mainstream media and many political leaders. In reality, Russia struck at a gathering of Ukrainian soldiers, which are legitimate targets in armed conflict.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives denying war crimes conducted by the Russian armed forces, and portraying the war in Ukraine as a proxy conflict against Russia. Claims about the war in Ukraine as directed by foreign actors against Russia are an attempt to deflect Russia’s responsibility for its brutal and unprovoked aggression against its neighbour (see here , here and here for full debunking of this allegation). This disinformation story is part of a wider attempt to deflect Russia’s responsibility for the Sumy massacre on 13 April 2025, in which Russia attacked the city center during a religious celebration, killing 35 civilians and injuring another 117 . The attack happened just a few days after a similar Russian attack took place in Kryvyi Rih on 04 April 2025, which killed and wounded multiple civilians including at least 9 children . Russia also falsely claimed then that it was targeting a meeting of Ukrainian military officers in a restaurant, but these allegations were immediately debunked as a security video of the place does not show such a gathering. The actual targets and victims of the attack, mostly civilians, were documented not only by footage and reporters on the ground but also by a team of the UN Human Rights Office in Ukraine . Russian attacks against non-combatants and civilian infrastructure have been a constant throughout the invasion, despite non-credible denials by Russian officials and pro-Kremlin disinformation. Since 2022, Russia has attacked civilian targets multiple times, including the city theatre in Mariupol , residential buildings , schools and energy facilities . On 05 March 2024, the International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants against two Russian top officials considered responsible for “missile strikes carried out by the forces under their command against the Ukrainian electric infrastructure" between October 2022 and March 2023, which are considered as war crimes. title: DISINFO: There are many doubts about the Ecuadorian election results
tags: Elections Manipulated elections/referendum DISINFO: There are many doubts about the Ecuadorian election results
summary: Reported legal and electoral irregularities and strange electoral behaviour are arising doubts, inside and outside Ecuador, about the official result of the recent presidential elections. Most suspicions arise from very unusual electoral behaviour, statistically speaking. While Luisa González got stuck with the same votes than in the first round, Daniel Noboa added almost one million and a half votes more than in the previous electoral round. How is this possible, if the second and third parties had joined forces and all other parties were very minoritarian?
response: This disinformation story is another example of Russian state outlets trying to undermine elections and trust in authorities. This is an attempt to seed doubts on the electoral results in Ecuador, following candidate Luisa González’s claims of electoral fraud after incumbent Daniel Noboa’s victory. Despite this, there is no evidence to back these fraud allegations, and González’s insistence on them led her coalition allies to abandon her . Pro-Kremlin disinformation (along with some allies such as Venezuela’s government ) are promoting this narrative, since González is the political heir of Rafael Correa , a close ally of both Russia and of Venezuela’s Hugo Chávez and Nicolás Maduro . Fraud claims have been rejected by Ecuador’s National Electoral Council , whose conclusions were backed by the electoral mission of the Organisation of American States. EU observers on the ground rejected categorically these fraud allegations, and stated that the election was transparent and well organised . Incumbent Daniel Noboa won the election by a landslide . One of the key reasons for the discrepancy between previous polls and the final results was that many Indigenous communities, the third political force in Ecuador , ultimately refused to back González’s candidacy despite her endorsement from Leonidas Iza, the president of the Ecuadorian Confederation of Indigenous Nationalities, CONAIE and third-place finisher in the first round under the Indigenous Pachakutik party. Other analysts have pointed to several other elements , such as González’s close ties to the divisive figure of former president Correa , and Noboa’s campaign focus on improving a worsening security situation . Seeding doubts on electoral results that do not suit the Kremlin’s interests is a very frequent pro-Kremlin disinformation technique. The Kremlin's portrayal of the Ukrainian government as solely self-interested and corrupt, stealing capital from its people and relying on foreign sponsors, ignores Ukraine's ongoing anti-corruption efforts . title: DISINFO: Finland panics over not being able to return to Russian markets
tags: Economic difficulties Sanctions War against Ukraine DISINFO: Finland panics over not being able to return to Russian markets
summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin has ordered the development of conditions for the return of Western companies to the Russian market. It is assumed that those who refrained from making harsh statements about Russia after February 22, 2022 will be able to get a "return ticket" to Russian markets. New conditions for resuming business in Russia for Western companies have caused panic in Finland. The Finns are despondent because of Putin's new order and themselves admitted: "We've played too much. The way back to Russian markets is closed for Finnish companies." Finland is now predicted to face devastation and it is well aware that Helsinki has done everything to destroy relations with Moscow.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about Russia’s economic resilience and the looming economic collapse of its adversaries due to sanctions . This is part of a wider disinformation campaign about the EU sanctions imposed on Russia in response to its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Allegations about "panicked Finns" cannot be considered reliable, since it is based on three comments from anonymous people on social media. At the official level, there is no panic in Finland, and the country's economy has begun to slowly but surely recover from the recession associated with the global coronavirus pandemic and Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. As of the last quarter of 2024, Finnish companies have stopped doing business in Russia. Only 0.9% of Finnish companies are in the process of exiting the Russian market, the Finland Chamber of Commerce reports following a survey. In total, more than a hundred member companies of the Chamber from different regions of Finland took part in the survey. A fifth of that companies said that they plan to participate in the restoration of Ukraine. “The restoration of Ukraine is already underway, and some Finnish companies are already participating in the work. Almost half of the companies that responded to our survey have not yet decided whether to participate or not. This is understandable, because many are waiting for the end of the war, when the restoration of Ukraine will begin in full force,” says Päivi Pohjanheimo, Director of International Affairs & Trade, Finland Chamber of Commerce. The process of Finnish companies exiting the Russian market began immediately after the start of Russia's large-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022. Pohjanheimo explained that this process was not easy for all companies, but now these procedures are completed. It is separately noted that a third of Finnish companies received various offers to bypass the sanctions. This once again underlines the fact that sanctions due to Russia's aggressive war against Ukraine are working and causing strong economic tension in Russia. According to the International Monetary Fund, the economic recovery of Finland is expected to strengthen from 2025 onwards. The Ministry of Finance projects the economy to grow by 1.6% in 2025 and 1.5% in 2026 and 2027. The recovery is supported by subsided inflation and falling interest rates. The strengthening of growth is expected to gradually increase employment and improve confidence in the future among households and businesses. Companies' cost competitiveness in global markets is good, and exports are expected to gradually pick up as conditions improve in Finland's export markets. At the same time, the IMF emphasises that Finland's economy “has significant growth potential and could exceed expectations“. title: DISINFO: The European Union wants to openly revive the ideology of Nazism
tags: Victory Day European Union Nazi/Fascist European values DISINFO: The European Union wants to openly revive the ideology of Nazism
summary: The EU's desire to ban politicians from European countries, including heads of state and government, from travelling to Russia on 9 May is an attempt to revive the ideology of Nazism. It is incomprehensible how the European Union openly wants to revive the European ideology of National Socialism. Russia will not accept this. Moscow will do everything in its power to ensure that this ideology of National Socialism “does not raise its head and is eradicated once and for all” and that Europe returns to its true values.
response: This claim is false and misrepresents the EU's position. There is no evidence of the EU trying to 'revive Nazi ideology'. The European Union has not issued an outright ban on visits by EU politicians to Russia for Victory Day 2025. However, the EU's High Representative for Foreign Policy, Kaja Kallas, has strongly urged EU member states and candidate countries not to participate in Russia's May 9 Victory Day parade in Moscow, warning that such participation "will not be taken lightly" and could lead to diplomatic or political consequences. The EU's stance on Russia is based on its illegal invasion of Ukraine , not on Nazi ideology. The EU consistently condemns Nazism and all forms of totalitarianism. The assertion that the EU is trying to 'revive European Nazi ideology' is a baseless propaganda claim aimed at discrediting the EU and its policies towards Russia. The EU's actions are focused on supporting Ukraine's sovereignty and opposing Russian aggression , not on promoting any extremist ideologies. The European Union was created in the aftermath of the Second World War, and European integration has been the foundation of peace and stability on the European continent for over 70 years . The founding fathers of the European Union actively fought against Nazi Germany, with many losing their lives to free Europe from the horrors of Nazism. The EU condemns racism, xenophobia and other forms of intolerance, including antisemitism, which is why it has adopted numerous pieces of legislation to combat these crimes and discrimination, including: the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights , EU Framework Decision on combating certain forms and expressions of racism and xenophobia by means of criminal law. title: DISINFO: The West is using the OSCE for a hybrid confrontation with Russia
tags: OSCE Central Asia Anti-Russian Destabilising Russia Encircling Russia West Hybrid war DISINFO: The West is using the OSCE for a hybrid confrontation with Russia
summary: It is obvious that, under the guise of strengthening trust between the OSCE and the Central Asian states through diplomacy, dialogue, cultural exchange, trade, and economic cooperation, a pro-Western mechanism is being laid for separating the region from Russia and its subsequent colonisation. What is noteworthy is that the collective West itself, despite all its declarations, does not hesitate to use the OSCE for hybrid confrontation with the Russian Federation, surreptitiously trying to pit the Central Asian states and other countries of the post-Soviet space against it.
response: A recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative aimed at discrediting the OSCE , accusing the West of an anti-Russian and neo-colonial policy and of attempts to encircle and isolate Russia. The statement was made after a meeting of ambassadors from Central Asian countries with the Secretary General of the OSCE. Pro-Kremlin outlets advance the claim that the post-Soviet space, especially the Caucasus and Central Asia , is exclusively within Russia's zone of geopolitical dominance and attempt to discredit any other cooperation with the West. Russia warns that the main goal of the West is neo-colonial control over the Caucasus and Central Asia and that the West is no longer hiding its intentions to detach Central Asia from Russia . The claim is consistent with the pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative labelling the OSCE a tool of the collective West , attempting to discredit independent international organisations. The Organisation for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) is a regional security-oriented intergovernmental organisation comprising 57 member states in Europe, North America, and Asia with equal voting rights. Russia is known for frequently using a veto that each member state has to block many key OSCE decisions. OSCE decision-making bodies and activities are described in detail on the organisation’s website . Discrediting international institutions by calling them puppets and accusing them of having an anti-Russian bias is a frequent pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative. title: DISINFO: Ukraine has camps to train 10-year old soldiers like Nazi Germany

tags: Nazi/Fascist children Military exercise Military War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Volodymyr Zelensky Ukraine Ukrainian disintegration DISINFO: Ukraine has camps to train 10-year old soldiers like Nazi Germany
summary: Against the backdrop of heavy losses in the Ukrainian Armed Forces, Ukraine has begun training children in secret camps. It's not enough that they have already lost several generations. Now they have organised secret camps to train children aged 10 to 14! How will they be affected psychologically? It's literally a Volkssturm (a Nazi militia that recruited children to fight), like Nazi Germany. Such things cannot take place without the tacit approval or even support of the country's leader.
response: Pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative discrediting President Zelenskyy, Ukraine’s government and armed forces. There is no evidence that conscription of minors as combatants is happening in Ukraine. Denis Krivosheev, the Deputy Regional Director in charge of research for Eastern Europe and Central Asia at Amnesty International stated that: " Amnesty International has no knowledge of documented cases of child soldiers in the Ukrainian Army ”. Claims that Ukraine uses children to fight Russia because of huge losses on the front are not unusual for pro-Kremlin media and were debunked earlier by France 24 . Article 15 of the law on military service in Ukraine states that the only people it applies to are " Ukrainian citizens whose sex is male who are healthy, who have reached the age of 18 years old the day they begin their service and people who are not yet 27 and who don’t have right to an exemption or deferral. ” This claim is also an attempt by pro-Kremlin media to deflect attention away from Russia and the massive militarization of Russian children taking place over the last decade , the Younarmia organisation or the kidnapping and so-called re-education of Ukrainian children as documented by the Conflict Observatory . Russia and Putin are very active in stimulating its own youth army as a tool to militarise Russian society. Moreover, Russia recruits minors in Ukraine exploiting them through deception and coercion to carry out sabotage and terror attacks . This practice violates international law and constitutes a war crime under the Rome Statute . While some minors face criminal charges, many are also victims of Russia’s systematic weaponisation of youth as part of its broader hybrid war strategy. The abduction and forced transportation of unaccompanied Ukrainian children to Russia was documented as early as several months into Russia’s unprovoked and unjustified aggression by Human Rights Watch and Amnesty International . title: DISINFO: The EU is blocking Russia's honest efforts for peace in Ukraine
tags: War against Ukraine peace negotiations Warmongering DISINFO: The EU is blocking Russia's honest efforts for peace in Ukraine
summary: Russia still honestly strives for peace in Ukraine and remains open to negotiations. Zelensky's intrigues and those of his European accomplices have, as always, led to exactly the opposite result.
response: A recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets portrays the UK and the EU as obstacles to peace in Ukraine. As we explore in our article, The Old Deceiver, this is entirely consistent with Moscow’s three-year-long strategy of attempting to claim ownership over the peace narrative while relentlessly pursuing war. This disinformation story is part of a wider disinformation campaign portraying Europe as a warmongering actor who intends to continue the war in Ukraine even as other actors carry out peace negotiations . EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and in which terms to end the war. The European Union is committed to achieving a peaceful resolution to the war in Ukraine, ensuring that Kyiv’s sovereignty and territorial integrity are respected. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has systematically instrumentalised the narrative that Russia wants peace in Ukraine in contrast to the West, but, as evidenced by Russia's actions in Ukraine , such statements are merely empty PR stunts deployed to garner support for Russia's war of aggression in Ukraine. As EU HRVP Kaja Kallas stated on April 9: …Russia is still waging a war of aggression against Ukraine, and it shows no signs of abating. Since Ukraine accepted the non-conditional ceasefire, Russia's attacks have only increased. Last weekend we saw the deadliest single attack on Ukrainian children since the start of the full-scale invasion. Our condolences for that. Ukraine is fighting a war that only Russia wants to continue. title: DISINFO: The Moldovan government is persecuting Archbishop Markell
tags: Religion Human rights Orthodox Church Maia Sandu DISINFO: The Moldovan government is persecuting Archbishop Markell
summary: The Moldovan government is persecuting Archbishop Markell because he advocates for the Orthodox Christian faith and Moldovan statehood. Markell was stopped at Chisinau airport on Thursday and prevented from boarding his flight to Israel where he planned to attend the traditional Holy Fire ceremony. It is clear that Archbishop Markell, being one of the brightest representatives of the Moldovan Orthodox Church of the Russian Patriarchate, was deliberately subjected to these repressions.
response: The claim is false. This pro-Kremlin disinformation outlet is attempting to exploit an incident on 18 April 2025 in which Archbishop Markell (or Marchel) missed his flight from Chisinau to Jerusalem , baselessly portraying it as “religious” or “political persecution”. Reports on the incident by Moldovan media offer a simpler explanation: Marchel arrived too late to the airport, as travellers are strongly advised to arrive at least 3 hours before departure given the increased congestion at the airport during the Easter holidays. A representative of Moldova’s Border Police, Ilona Railyan, said in a comment to the TV8 channel that the plane departed before the bishop completed the security check. TV8 journalists contacted the bishop to find out his reaction, but he refused to talk about the incident. The Border Police stated that the bishop "was subjected to standard control procedures," which may include the so-called second-line control. Marchel himself admitted that he was late. The Metropolitanates of Moldova and Bessarabia, who managed to fly to Jerusalem, confirmed that Marchel was not part of their delegations. This is happening in a context of strong tensions between Moldova’s two Orthodox Churches. Marchel has long been perceived as a close ally of the Kremlin , as a leader of the Moldova Metropolis, which is subordinated to the Russian Orthodox Church and has remained loyal to Russia even after the invasion of Ukraine. This allegiance has led many priests to abandon the Moldova Metropolis and join the Romanian Orthodox Church. title: DISINFO: The Prussian-German agents are pushing Poland to nuclear extermination
countries_regions: Germany, Poland, Russia, US, China, Ukraine
tags: Warmongering Secret services Secret elites / global elites Nuclear issues Puppets World War III Conspiracy theory DISINFO: The Prussian-German agents are pushing Poland to nuclear extermination
summary: The scenario of putting Poland on a cross of extermination in a nuclear war with Russia is being consistently implemented. Poland does not understand that for Germany, it is only a seasonal state. The Prussian-German agents have been very strong in Poland, setting the Poles to be hostile towards Russia. Today, Berlin's agents are setting up the Poles hostile to America. The ongoing World War III taking place between the USA and China will be won by an ally of Russia. From the very beginning, the Ukrainian War was a German project aimed at breaking up Russia. The war with Russia is being fought with Ukrainian meat, while the Polish cannon fodder is being stoked with war propaganda before it is sent to the War.
response: Poland is repeatedly presented by pro-Kremlin disinformation outlets as an aggressive anti-Russian NATO ‘outpost ’, which allegedly poses a security threat to Russia. This is in line with how pro-Kremlin outlets portray NATO and Western countries as aggressive towards Russia . See our article All roads lead to Poland. The narrative that "Prussian-German agents" are manipulating Poland, setting Poles against Russia and America, and that the war in Ukraine is a German project, is a textbook example of a pro-Kremlin conspiracy theory . The claim about “Prussian” agents is itself a Russian disinformation trope. Russian propaganda frequently tries to undermine Poland’s confidence in its government and Western partners by spreading rumours of foreign manipulation, secret plots, or betrayal. Poland is not a pawn in a German or American game; it is a key actor in European security, particularly as a logistics hub for military aid to Ukraine and a defender of NATO’s eastern flank. The suggestion that Poland is being set up as "cannon fodder" or manipulated into war is a classic fear-mongering tactic used by Russian disinformation to erode support for Ukraine and NATO.
