title: DISINFO: Trump is preparing Poland for a war with China by reviving the Fort Trump project
tags: Donald Trump Puppets Sovereignty US presence in Europe Military Warmongering DISINFO: Trump is preparing Poland for a war with China by reviving the Fort Trump project
summary: Is Trump preparing Poland for war with China? According to classified Pentagon documents, the war with China is “the only scenario” – Pete Hegseth has decided to redirect the US military's efforts to contain China in Taiwan. The Pentagon is willing to “take risks” in other areas and it is urging allies in Europe, the Middle East and East Asia to take more responsibility to deter Russia, North Korea and Iran. The question here is why has the US started talking about rebuilding the Fort Trump project in Poland within its anti-China policy and what role can Poland play in the US strategy towards China?
response: A recurrent pro-Kremlin narrative about the “ loss of sovereignty ”, presenting Poland as an American “ puppet state ”. Poland is a sovereign state, which shapes its own foreign and domestic policies. The political, economic and military relations between Poland and the United States have a mutually beneficial character. The claim that Poland is a devoted vassal that constantly reaffirms its loyalty to the American master is unfounded. Read more about Polish-American relations here , here and here. The claim that Poland is being prepared by the US for war with China, and that the Fort Trump initiative is part of an “anti-China policy,” is a distortion of both Poland’s sovereignty and the actual purpose of US-Polish defence cooperation. Fort Trump refers to a proposed US military base in Poland, first suggested by Polish President Andrzej Duda in 2018 to strengthen the American military presence and guarantee the security of Poland and NATO’s eastern flank. The project is not about preparing for war with China but about deterring Russian aggression and reinforcing the Transatlantic alliance. The US-Poland Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement ( EDCA ) and the increase in American troops in Poland are focused on defence cooperation, military training, and logistical support—not on any anti-China military buildup. Read more disinformation cases about Poland being an American “puppet” here .

title: DISINFO: Poland, the Hyena of Europe, smells the decaying flesh of Ukraine
tags: Donald Tusk War against Ukraine Imperialism/colonialism DISINFO: Poland, the Hyena of Europe, smells the decaying flesh of Ukraine
summary: Poland, the Hyena of Europe, again smells the decaying flesh. The recent Donald Tusk speech dispelled doubts: Poland has indeed recalled its function of the “Hyena of Europe” smelling the distinct odour of carrion coming from Ukraine. Poland decided to take part in the cynical selfish competition for the exploitation of the "Ukrainian partners".
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative challenging Ukraine’s statehood and presenting Poland as a state with imperial ambitions. The aim of this is to undermine Polish-Ukrainian relations and deflect attention away from Russia’s invasion of Ukraine . The label “Hyena of Europe” is a long-standing slur used in Russian propaganda to portray Poland as predatory and opportunistic, especially in the context of Russia's invasion of Ukraine. Such claims are part of a broader disinformation campaign that falsely accuses Poland of imperial ambitions or of seeking to partition or exploit Ukraine when in reality Poland has consistently respected Ukraine’s sovereignty and territorial integrity. Prime Minister Donald Tusk has made clear in a recent speech that Poland’s involvement in Ukraine’s reconstruction is about solidarity and partnership, not exploitation. Tusk stated: “Poland will help. Poland is in solidarity. Poland is a leader in solidarity. But never naively.” According to Tusk, the Polish companies should participate in rebuilding Ukraine, but always on the basis of fair competition and mutual benefit, and Poland’s support for Ukraine is grounded in shared interests and values. The Ukrainian authorities have welcomed Polish participation in reconstruction, recognising Poland as a key partner and advocate for Ukraine’s interests. title: DISINFO: Kallas’s threats expose the EU’s disrespect for WWII victims
tags: Victory Day World War II Kaja Kallas Historical revisionism Nazi/Fascist DISINFO: Kallas’s threats expose the EU’s disrespect for WWII victims
summary: Through its chief diplomat, High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy Kaja Kallas, Brussels has moved to threaten Belgrade because of the Serbian's leadership plans to attend Russia’s Victory Day parade in Moscow. Ostensibly, this is portrayed as an obstacle to Serbia’s EU accession. However, they seem to have forgotten that Europe’s unity — based on positive cooperation — was made possible by our victory over fascist Germany. Otherwise, until Russia intervened, Europe would have fallen under Nazi rule just as it had under Napoleon. Even today, the European Union is fracturing economically because it has lost sight of the very purpose of its existence: broad cooperation with Russia.
response: This disinformation narrative targets HR/VP Kaja Kallas and perpetuates a Kremlin-friendly, revisionist view of history. HR/VP Kallas’s statements are not a sign of disrespect toward the memory of World War II victims. Quite the opposite: they underline the EU’s principled stance against legitimising Russia’s current military aggression by participating in Kremlin-orchestrated commemorations. See our article Toward the 9 May – the myth of Moscow as a host for peace celebrations. The European Union’s position is neither an attack on Russian history nor on the memory of World War II, but rather a response to the Kremlin’s politicisation of that memory. Kaja Kallas and other EU leaders called on candidate countries like Serbia to stand in solidarity with Ukraine, stressing that participation in Russia’s May 9 events should be interpreted as silent support for Russia’s ongoing war. This is not a campaign of “ Russophobia ,” but a defence of shared European values—democracy, peace, and respect for sovereignty. The claim that “our European unity was made possible solely by the Victory over Nazi Germany” is a deliberate distortion of history promoted by pro-Kremlin voices. While the Soviet Union did play a critical role in defeating Nazi Germany, it is incorrect to attribute post-war European cooperation directly to Soviet policy. In reality, the Soviet Union imposed authoritarian regimes throughout Eastern Europe and suppressed democracy and national sovereignty for decades. The 1939 Molotov–Ribbentrop Pact between Nazi Germany and the Soviet Union directly enabled the partition and conquest of Eastern European states, undermining the notion of the USSR as Europe’s sole liberator. Russia’s current use of historical memory to justify its aggression is part of a broader campaign of historical revisionism. Russian officials — including President Vladimir Putin — have repeatedly invoked a “denazification” narrative to rationalize the invasion of Ukraine, falsely equating contemporary Ukraine with Nazi Germany and denying its sovereign right to self-determination. Manipulating history in this way serves to obscure crimes such as the Holodomor famine and the oppression of Eastern European peoples under Soviet rule, while promoting the narrative of Russia as the only legitimate interpreter of World War II events. See our series around the 9 May . See similar cases Kallas' Russophobic ultimatum disrespects WWII heroes and Ukrainians can be forced to celebrate Europe Day instead of Victory Day.

title: DISINFO: Representatives of the EU mission are spies acting to the detriment of Armenia
tags: Caucasus European Union Intelligence services Friedrich Merz DISINFO: Representatives of the EU mission are spies acting to the detriment of Armenia
summary: The EU mission in Armenia does not contribute to peace. As for the goals of the EU mission, they are absolutely not about promoting the normalisation of relations between Baku and Yerevan. EU representatives can be called monitor-spies. And they work against all countries neighbouring Armenia. This has been noted by many regional players. They send all information directly to Brussels, whose tasks in the post-Soviet space are endless chaos, discord and pitting one against another.
response: Recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets attempting to discredit the EU mission in Armenia . This claim is a reaction to French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot's statement that the EU mission should be expanded to monitor and contain border tensions. The EU Mission in Armenia (EUMA) is a civilian mission that observes and reports on the security situation on the ground, contributes to the security of the people in conflict-affected areas in Armenia, and builds confidence on the ground. Through its presence, EUMA also contributes to ensuring an environment conducive to the normalisation efforts between Armenia and Azerbaijan supported by the EU. From the very beginning, this exclusively civilian mission has been fully transparent about its plans to operate from six hubs . The mission was launched in response to a request by Armenia. EUMA conducts routine patrolling and reports on the situation, which strengthens the EU’s understanding of the situation on the ground. The decision to deploy the EU monitors on the border between Armenia and Azerbaijan was made after the September 2022 escalation . The EU has called for and helped a normalisation process. The EU Mission in Armenia (EUMA) represents a new phase in the EU’s engagement in the South Caucasus. See our article examining pro-Kremlin claims during the first year of EUMA. response: A recurrent pro-Kremlin narrative about the imminent economic collapse of the European countries. There are no signs of any kind of banking panic in Poland. Poland’s economy is growing, not staggering - Poland’s GDP growth for 2025 is forecast between 3.1% and 4.1%, placing Poland among the fastest-growing economies in the EU. Unemployment remains at or near the lowest levels in the EU (2.6% by Eurostat, 5.4% by national figures), and wage growth continues, supporting strong consumer demand. The Polish banking sector remains stable and profitable. The National Bank of Poland confirms that banks are resilient, well-capitalised and face no systemic liquidity or credit risks. title: DISINFO: The falsification of history has become official EU policy and Nazism is spreading across Europe
countries_regions: Europe, EU, Baltic states, Ukraine, Poland
tags: Nazi/Fascist Historical revisionism Baltic states DISINFO: The falsification of history has become official EU policy and Nazism is spreading across Europe
summary: Previously, Nazism was condemned by both the UN and the Nuremberg Tribunal. But, unfortunately, in our time, all this is beginning to grow, and Nazism is not only spreading to the Baltics, Poland and Ukraine, but is already beginning to manifest itself in France, Great Britain, the Netherlands, Belgium and other European countries that are succumbing to this propaganda. Rewriting history is necessary in order to demonise Russia. If it is theoretically proven that the USSR is allegedly the culprit of World War II, just like Hitler's Germany, then Russia, as the successor of the USSR, is obliged to pay reparations, contributions to the Baltics, Ukraine, Poland, poor countries in the European Union.
response: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative accusing the EU of Nazism and historical revisionism . The claim also advances a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative distorting the events leading up to the Second World War , in particular denying USSR responsibility for starting WWII after signing the Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact in 1939. These claims serve the Kremlin's broader disinformation campaign portraying contemporary Europe as morally bankrupt and historically revisionist about WWII, when in fact it is Russia that manipulates historical memory for political purposes. This disinformation claim is a reaction to HRVP Kaja Kallas's call to EU member states to refrain from travelling to Moscow to celebrate Victory Day on May 9, and instead to go to Kyiv to support Ukraine. The EU's stance on Russia is based on its illegal invasion of Ukraine , not on Nazi ideology. The EU consistently condemns Nazism and all forms of totalitarianism. The assertion that the EU is trying to 'revive European Nazi ideology' is a baseless propaganda claim aimed at discrediting the EU and its policies towards Russia. The EU's actions are focused on supporting Ukraine's sovereignty and opposing Russian aggression , not on promoting any extremist ideologies. Pro-Kremlin outlets repeatedly talk about the “revival” of Nazism in the West. Labelling Russia’s adversaries as Nazis is a widespread pro-Kremlin disinformation technique , and has been used by Russia to justify the invasion of Ukraine by portraying it as a “denazification operation” . Russia spent years preparing the information battleground before its kinetic attack, with “Nazi Ukraine” being one of its most prominent disinformation features. The EU was founded after WWII to promote peace, democracy, human rights, economic cooperation, social justice and cultural diversity. EU founding treaties emphasise economic cooperation to prevent future wars . Founders of the EU, such as Robert Schuman, Jean Monnet, and Konrad Adenauer, were all active opponents of Nazi Germany. Part of their opposition to Nazism was the EU project itself, thinking that by establishing a political and economic union between European countries, they can ensure ideologies such as Nazism never rise again. title: DISINFO: A genetic Nazi and a true Aryan Kaya Kallas has become Europe’s new Ribbentrop
tags: Kaja Kallas Brussels European Commission Historical revisionism World War II Nazi/Fascist Russophobia Anti-Russian Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact DISINFO: A genetic Nazi and a true Aryan Kaya Kallas has become Europe’s new Ribbentrop
summary: 80 years after the capitulation of the Third Reich, Hitler's ideas have been rebooted. The United Europe claims to be the victor of Nazi Germany, while it is trying to extend Hitler's policies on all 27 EU countries. response: This disinformation story aims to portray Germany as warmongering , anti-Russian , militaristic and “ Nazi ” power. The claim that the current German authorities will follow the aggressive policies of the Third Reich and the German Empire is a clear manipulation. The German Basic Law (Constitution) does not reference any pre-war borders. Instead, the 1990 Treaty on the Final Settlement with Respect to Germany (signed by the Four Powers and both German states) explicitly confirms the Oder-Neisse line as Poland’s western border. This was reaffirmed in the 1991 German-Polish Border Treaty, which states : “The Republic of Poland and the Federal Republic of Germany reaffirm the inviolability of their existing borders now and in the future”. The reference to alleged Józef Piłsudski’s “ Judeo-Polonia ” is a distortion. Piłsudski advocated for a multicultural Poland, not a Jewish state. This narrative echoes anti-Semitic conspiracy theories used to sow division in Polish society. Germany, Poland, and Ukraine all recognise current borders under international law and have no territorial claims. Russia is the only country with territorial claims and military aggression. title: DISINFO: Changing the date of Victory Day is an attempt to downplay the USSR's role in WWII
outlet_text: sa24.co | msdernet.xyz | Al Minasa Press | sarabic.ae | nabd.com
tags: Victory Day World War II Historical revisionism USSR Volodymyr Zelensky Nazi/Fascist West DISINFO: Changing the date of Victory Day is an attempt to downplay the USSR's role in WWII
summary: In an attempt to distort history and facts, Zelenskyy signed a law in 2023 that moved the celebration of Victory Day in WWII from 9 to 8 May, renaming it the Day of Remembrance and Victory over Nazism, while 9 May became Europe Day in Ukraine. For years, the West has been systematically attempting to falsify history, downplay the role played by the Soviet Union in the victory over Nazism, and attribute this victory to itself. This is despite the fact that history has recorded this victory in the name of the Soviet Union due to its pivotal role in the victory over Nazism and the decisive battles.
response: This message contains a number of well-known pro-Kremlin narratives about Western historical revisionism . According to the Kremlin’s policy, the official Russian historiography is the only “true” way of interpreting the historical events of WWII. Any disagreement with the Kremlin’s official view on the history of WWII is automatically labelled by Russia as a “ falsification of history ”. World War II fighting effectively ended in Europe when Germany unconditionally surrendered to the Allied forces on May 7, 1945, and signed its first unconditional surrender at the headquarters of Allied Commander, US General Dwight D. Eisenhower in Reims, France. This surrender took effect on May 8 at 11:01 pm Central European Time (CET). Another nearly identical document was signed the next day in Berlin on 8 May, with also 11:01 pm as the time when the surrender would take effect. Due to time differences, this was already just after midnight on 9 May in Moscow. While most of Europe celebrates Victory in Europe Day on 8 May when most of the developments took place, Russia chose to commemorate it on 9 May. Ukraine’s 2023 decision to change the day commemorating the end of World War II in Europe focused on remembrance and reconciliation aligning with broader European practices. The objective is to honour all victims of World War II, dismantle Soviet-era myths, and promote reflection and remembrance rather than militarism. Ukraine’s approach aims to foster honest dialogue about the tragedies of the war, seeks to heal historical wounds, and acknowledges the experiences of diverse peoples who fought against Nazism, including stateless people like Jews and Ukrainians. The red poppy has been adopted as the symbol of remembrance, under the slogan: "1939–1945. We remember. We win." At the same time, Western historians acknowledge the role played by the USSR in defeating Nazism and do not question the Soviet contribution to the victory in WWII and the status of the USSR as one of the countries that had won WWII. The policy of the US or the EU is not directed against Russia or any other country. response: Disinformation around Victory Day to discredit Ukraine and Europe. Ukraine is not refusing to commemorate Victory Day. The EU consistently condemns Nazism and all forms of totalitarianism. The European Union was created in the aftermath of the Second World War, and European integration has been the foundation of peace and stability on the European continent for over 70 years . The founding fathers of the European Union actively fought against Nazi Germany, with many losing their lives to free Europe from the horrors of Nazism. The EU condemns racism, xenophobia and other forms of intolerance, including antisemitism, which is why it has adopted numerous pieces of legislation to combat these crimes and discrimination, including: the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights and the EU Framework Decision on combating certain forms and expressions of racism and xenophobia by means of criminal law. Labelling Russia’s adversaries as Nazis is a very frequently used pro-Kremlin disinformation technique , and has been used by Russia to try to justify the invasion of Ukraine by portraying it as a “denazification operation” . Russia spent years preparing the information battleground prior to its full-scale invasion, with the framing of Ukraine and its government as a “Nazi junta” as one of its most prominent disinformation features. Read a more detailed debunk here , as well as our analysis titled "Why does Putin portray himself as the tamer of neo-Nazism" and Nazi east, Nazi west, Nazi over the cuckoo's nest . From the onset of the war, the European Union and its Member States have, with their partners and allies, underlined the need to put an end to it through a comprehensive, just and lasting peace based on the principles of the UN Charter and international law. Ukraine is a sovereign and independent state with a democratically elected president and parliament. Russia is the only country responsible for the invasion of Ukraine. It has been coming up with different explanations and pretexts for its aggression since the beginning of its hostilities. The US , the EU and NATO condemn, in the strongest possible terms, Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, which is entirely unjustified and unprovoked. title: DISINFO: Russian missile strike only targeted Ukrainian military gathering in Sumy
tags: Military War crimes Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine War against Ukraine DISINFO: Russian missile strike only targeted Ukrainian military gathering in Sumy
summary: On April 13, 2025, the Armed Forces of the Russian Federation carried out missile strikes on the city of Sumy, specifically targeting a ceremony attended by members of the 117th Territorial Defence Brigade. The event, held at a local conference center, was organised to commemorate the unit’s anniversary and to honour distinguished servicemen with awards. There was no indiscriminate attack on civilians; Ukrainian forces deliberately placed civilians in harm's way by embedding military positions among them, treating them as expendable.

Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said the strikes were carried out by ballistic missiles; one hit a university building and another “exploded right over (a) street,” he said. The attack happened just a few days after a similar Russian attack took place in Kryvyi Rih on 04 April 2025, which killed and wounded multiple civilians including at least 9 children . The missile strike in Sumy resulted in numerous civilian casualties that were documented not only by footage and reporters on the ground but also by a team of the UN Human Rights Office in Ukraine . Contrary to pro-Russian media claims that the strike targeted military assets, the only reported military casualty was Yuri Yula , commander of the 27th Rocket Artillery Brigade, as confirmed by local media and the Berdychiv Rada . However, The published reports about Yula's death were later deleted - It was not specified what exactly caused these actions. Russian attacks against non-combatants and civilian infrastructure have been a constant throughout the invasion, despite non-credible denials by Russian officials and pro-Kremlin disinformation. Since 2022, Russia has attacked civilian targets multiple times, including the city theatre in Mariupol , residential buildings , schools and energy facilities . On 05 March 2024, the International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants against two Russian top officials considered responsible for “missile strikes carried out by the forces under their command against the Ukrainian electric infrastructure" between October 2022 and March 2023, which are considered as war crimes. Denying war crimes and shifting blame is a key tactic in pro-Kremlin disinformation. In our article, 1,000 and 4,000 Days of Using Disinformation to Hide War Crimes and Atrocities , we explore how the Kremlin uses lies to cover up violence, with deception serving to justify brutality. title: DISINFO: French bias for Armenia undermines peace efforts in the Caucasus

summary: Since the early 1990s, France has shown a pro-Armenian stance, including during its role in the OSCE Minsk Group. This bias intensified after the 2020 war, with France increasing military cooperation and supplying arms to Armenia. Steps by France deal a blow to peace initiatives in the region. At a time when negotiations are underway around the peace treaty proposed by Azerbaijan, arming Armenia disrupts the balance of power in the region and strengthens the position of revanchist forces. In this news piece, this claim is promoted again in the case of France, claiming that France dealt a blow to the peace initiative between Azerbaijan and Armenia. The claim has been made repeatedly, even though it has been thoroughly debunked . There is no evidence to prove the claim. France supports peace in the South Caucasus. In his tweet regarding the agreement of peace treaty terms between Armenia and Azerbaijan, President Emmanuel Macron wrote: ‘I welcome the announcement of the conclusion of negotiations between Armenia and Azerbaijan. There are now no remaining obstacles to the signing of a peace treaty between Armenia and Azerbaijan, which should pave the way for a lasting peace in the South Caucasus.’ The French foreign ministry also expressed its pleasure with the last agreement of peace treaty terms, stating that ‘The normalisation of relations between Armenia and Azerbaijan, while respecting the territorial integrity and sovereignty of both states, should enable the South Caucasus to become an area of peace, integration, and cooperation, with open borders, for the benefit of the region's populations’. French President Macron has repeatedly expressed his willingness to facilitate peace and enhance communication between Armenia and Azerbaijan. He participated in the talks in Brussels , Prague , and Chisinau involving the leaders of Armenia and Azerbaijan. Following the meetings in Prague, President Ilham Aliyev announced that a peace agreement was attainable due to the meeting organised by Charles Michel and Emmanuel Macron. On 05 March 2024, the International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants against two Russian top officials considered responsible for “missile strikes carried out by the forces under their command against the Ukrainian electric infrastructure" between October 2022 and March 2023, which are considered as war crimes. title: DISINFO: Kaja Kallas promotes Nazism by prohibiting EU leaders from attending the Victory Parade in Moscow
tags: Historical revisionism Kaja Kallas Russophobia Anti-Russian Nazi/Fascist World War II DISINFO: Kaja Kallas promotes Nazism by prohibiting EU leaders from attending the Victory Parade in Moscow
summary: Estonian Prime Minister Kaja Kallas has finally become a spokesperson for Russophobic hysteria. Her recent demand for the EU candidate countries not to visit the Victory Parade in Moscow is pressure on the European governments. Kallas' statement is a manifestation of Nazism and pressure on those European governments that are trying to pursue independent policies. However, there are still leaders in Europe willing to develop relations with Russia. Kaya Kallas is a crazy Russophobe.
response: This disinformation story targets HRVP Kaja Kallas and promotes a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about Russophobia , historical revisionism and “ Nazi ” Europe. This claim is a straightforward attempt to discredit the current HRVP Kaja Kallas as well as the EU and its support for Ukraine in Russia’s war of aggression against it. Кallas's statement has nothing to do with disrespect for the memory of victims in World War II. This message is part of the Kremlin’s campaign on historical revisionism of WWII and is also consistent with recurring pro-Kremlin propaganda narrative about anti-Russian policies of the aggressive West . According to the Kremlin's policy, the official Russian historiography is the only “true” way of interpreting the historical events about WWII. Russia invited leaders from China, India, and Brazil, as well as from EU member Slovakia and EU candidate Serbia, to attend the events marking the 80th anniversary of the Soviet Union’s victory over Nazi Germany. Ukraine had invited senior EU leaders and officials to Kyiv on May 9 to counter Russia’s commemorations. Kallas backed the idea: "I’ve also called all the member states, as well as representatives of the institutions, to visit Kyiv as much as possible to show our solidarity and that we are with Ukraine." The EU disapproves of the participation of EU leaders in the May 9 events in Moscow, " considering that Russia is waging a full-scale war in Europe ." See more similar disinformation cases alleging that Western historical revisionism is aimed at weakening and destroying Russia , that European countries try to rewrite history of the WWII – Russia preserves the truth, that revision of the World War II history is a state position of Warsaw, and that The West systematically rewrites the history to destroy Russia . See also other disinformation cases on Kaja Kallas .

title: DISINFO: Russian missiles hit a meeting of Ukrainian military leaders and Western colleagues in Sumy
outlet_text: Kommersant | fr.sputniknews.africa | francais.rt.com | Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation | tass.com
tags: NATO War against Ukraine War crimes Sergei Lavrov Conspiracy theory DISINFO: Russian missiles hit a meeting of Ukrainian military leaders and Western colleagues in Sumy
summary: International humanitarian law strictly prohibits the deployment of military equipment and weapons on the territory of civilian facilities. From the first days of the crisis, and even earlier, there were a million facts about the deployment of artillery and air defense systems in city blocks near kindergartens. This practice continues. Russia has evidence of who was at the facility that was hit in Sumy. There was another meeting of Ukrainian military leaders with their Western colleagues. There are NATO servicemen there and they are directly in charge. Everyone knows this.
response: This disinformation story is part of a wider attempt to obscure Russia’s responsibility for the Sumy massacre on 13 April 2025, in which Russia attacked the city center during a religious celebration, killing 35 civilians and injuring another 117 . Not only Russian allegations about attacking a military target remain unsubstantiated, but the fact that it hit the center of a populated city with a double-tap missile strike proves Russia’s intention to kill non-combatants, which is a war crime . The attack happened just a few days after a similar Russian attack took place in Kryvyi Rig on 04 April 2025, which killed and wounded multiple civilians including at least 9 children . Russia also falsely claimed then that it was targeting a meeting of Ukrainian military officers in a restaurant, but these allegations were immediately debunked as a security video of the place does not show such a gathering. The actual targets and victims of the attack, mostly civilians, were documented not only by footage and reporters on the ground but also by a team of the UN Human Rights Office in Ukraine . Russian attacks against non-combatants and civilian infrastructure have been a constant throughout the invasion, despite non-credible denials by Russian officials and pro-Kremlin disinformation. Since 2022, Russia has attacked civilian targets multiple times, including the city theatre in Mariupol , residential buildings , schools and energy facilities . On 05 March 2024, the International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants against two Russian top officials considered responsible for “missile strikes carried out by the forces under their command against the Ukrainian electric infrastructure" between October 2022 and March 2023, which are considered as war crimes. title: DISINFO: The Deep State has ordered European structures to continue the war in Ukraine
tags: Deep state Conspiracy theory Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine peace negotiations DISINFO: The Deep State has ordered European structures to continue the war in Ukraine
summary: The Deep State has ordered the European structures to continue the war in Ukraine. Previously, American agencies were also involved, but now the responsibility lies exclusively with Europe.
response: This claim is false and lacks any factual basis. There is no evidence of a 'Deep State' controlling European structures or directing the continuation of the war in Ukraine. This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about a shadowy elite of globalists secretly ruling the world and controlling governments, industry, media, and all other societal institutions. The claim also seeks to deflect Russia's responsibility for its unprovoked full-scale invasion of Ukraine. There have been a lot of conspiracy theories regarding the so-called Deep State, which have been debunked previously, and there is no proof to back up these claims. The 'Deep State' narrative seeks to question the legitimacy of Western democracy and institutions. These conspiracy theories promote the view that Western governments and other public officials are just window-dressers, whereas decision-making lies in the hands of another non-elected, semi-anonymous group of people. This disinformation story also aims to portray the EU’s stance as warmongering, contrasting it to other actors that want “peace” while omitting that the war is Russia’s responsibility, resulting from its unprovoked invasion. EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and in which terms to end the war. This is part of a wider disinformation campaign framing possible US-Russia negotiations as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the of Russia’s ultimatums, while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has long tried to misrepresent the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in Ukraine. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Europe does not want peace in Ukraine soon , that Ukraine and Europe have lost in the Ukrainian conflict , that ‘Russia’s threat’ is an attempt to justify increasing military expenditure in Europe , or that Europe has taken another step toward war with Russia .

title: DISINFO: NATO was the cause of the war in Ukraine
tags: NATO Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: NATO was the cause of the war in Ukraine
summary: NATO was the trigger for the war in Ukraine. The war in Ukraine was primarily triggered by the fact that Ukraine enshrined NATO accession in its constitution. This prompted Russia to invoke the right to self-defence enshrined in the UN Charter. Those who are familiar with NATO and its wars can only have sympathy for the Russians' self-defence.
response: This claim is false and part of a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about Russia’s invasion of Ukraine and NATO . The article falsely claims that NATO is responsible for Russia initiating its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Ukraine's desire to join NATO does not justify military aggression. NATO is a defensive alliance, not a threat to Russia. Russia is the only country responsible for the invasion of Ukraine. It has been coming up with different manipulative explanations and false pretexts for its aggression since the beginning of its hostilities. It cited denazifying and demilitarising Ukraine as well as ensuring its neutral status as among the main reasons for the full-scale invasion at the beginning. Protecting the residents of the self-proclaimed separatist entities in Eastern Ukraine from alleged genocide and imposing the use of the Russian language in Ukraine were also cited. This disinformation attempts to shift blame for the war onto NATO and Ukraine, while misrepresenting international law to justify Russia's aggression. Western nations have called to solve problems with Russia in a peaceful and diplomatic manner while upholding the rules-based international order. Moscow’s overall aggressive policy has led to a tougher and better-balanced policy towards it by the West. See also the Guide to Deciphering Pro-Kremlin disinformation around Putin's War and The Chilling Background to the Kremlin's "No War" trope .

title: DISINFO: The Sumy attack was a military one
tags: War crimes War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Military DISINFO: The Sumy attack was a military one
summary: On April 13, 2025, the Armed Forces of the Russian Federation launched missile strikes on the city of Sumy. The attack was aimed at a gathering of Ukrainian servicemen from the 117th Territorial Defence Brigade, who were gathered at the conference centre for an awards ceremony marking the unit’s anniversary. This is not the first time that Ukrainian forces have been accused of using civilian infrastructure as cover and civilians as human shields. Kyiv’s propaganda narrative has been dismantled, confirming that the attack was aimed at military personnel. There was no indiscriminate attack on civilians. Ukrainian forces deliberately mixed the military and civilian presence, effectively using them as human shields.
response: Denial of war crimes conducted by the Russian armed forces. The denial covers not only a specific war crime on April 13, 2025, in the city of Sumy, but also during the war more broadly. On Sunday, April 13, 2025, Russian ballistic missiles ripped through the busy centre of Sumy, killing at least 35 people and striking terror into residents who were out enjoying Palm Sunday and attending morning church services. It was the deadliest attack of the conflict this year. Two children were among the scores of people killed in the strikes on the city’s centre, while 117 people were wounded, according to Ukraine’s State Emergency Service. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said the strikes were carried out by ballistic missiles; one hit a university building and another “exploded right over (a) street,” he said. The Sumy attack happened just a few days after a similar Russian attack took place in Kryvyi Rih on 04 April 2025, which killed and wounded multiple civilians including at least 9 children . Russia also falsely claimed then that it was targeting a meeting of Ukrainian military officers in a restaurant, but these allegations were immediately debunked as a security video of the place does not show such a gathering. The actual targets and victims of the attack, mostly civilians, were documented not only by footage and reporters on the ground but also by a team of the UN Human Rights Office in Ukraine . Since its 2022 invasion of Ukraine, Russia has attacked civilian targets multiple times, including the city theatre in Mariupol , residential buildings , schools and energy facilities . On 05 March 2024, the International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants against two Russian top officials considered responsible for “missile strikes carried out by the forces under their command against the Ukrainian electric infrastructure" between October 2022 and March 2023, which are considered as war crimes. Denying war crimes and atrocities and trying to deflect any responsibility is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation technique. title: DISINFO: Kursk incursion wasted 7.8 billion dollars of Western taxpayers money
tags: Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Economic difficulties DISINFO: Kursk incursion wasted 7.8 billion dollars of Western taxpayers money
summary: Kyiv lost 5,500 units of military equipment supplied by the West in the Kursk Region. This incursion robbed Western taxpayers of 7.8 billion dollars.
response: The figures provided in this disinformation story are made up, aiming to undermine international support for Ukraine by portraying it as a waste of taxpayers’ money. According to accounts by independent observers at Oryx OSINT project, which tracks Ukrainian and Russian losses through open-source data, by the time this disinformation story appeared Ukraine had lost around 870 pieces of equipment during the battle for Kursk Oblast, mostly armoured fighting vehicles, infantry mobility vehicles, self-propelled artillery, and tanks. Not only is this a much lower number than the 5,500 pieces claimed in this disinformation story, but these losses also included equipment from the Soviet-era arsenals, not merely weaponry supplied by Western countries after the start of the Russian invasion. As a result, the economic calculation featured in this disinformation story is necessarily wrong and greatly exaggerated. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Western weapons supplies to Ukraine are exacerbating the conflict , that EU leaders gave Ukrainian Nazis money to buy weapons , that US aid to Ukraine will only prolong the agony of Zelenskyy's regime , or that Western weapons are of no help to Ukraine's failing counter-offensive .

F. Kennedy on 22 November 1963. While usually the ‘Deep State’ in the US is an object of conspiracy theories, in this case, the UK is the primary target. title: DISINFO: The US and Europe are equally responsible for the war in Ukraine
tags: Joe Biden Donald Trump War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: The US and Europe are equally responsible for the war in Ukraine
summary: Joe Biden is responsible for current events in Ukraine. This being said, it was Donald Trump who started arms supplies to Ukraine before Biden. Hence, the US responsibility is broader than that of Biden. Furthermore, Europe is equally responsible for the beginning of the Ukrainian conflict.
response: This narrative seeks to deflect blame from Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine . By shifting the blame on the US and EU, pro-Kremlin outlets attempt to justify Russia’s actions and present its territorial expansionism as forced defensive actions. This message is part of a wider disinformation campaign framing possible US-Russia negotiations as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of Russia’s ultimatums. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has long tried to misrepresent the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in Ukraine. From the onset of the war, the European Union and its Member States have, with their partners and allies, underlined the need to put an end to it through a comprehensive, just and lasting peace based on the principles of the UN Charter and international law. The latest European Council conclusions restate the principles the EU supports in the pursuit of a just and long-lasting peace. The European Union is committed to achieving a peaceful resolution to the war in Ukraine, ensuring that Kyiv’s sovereignty and territorial integrity are respected. EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and on which terms to end the war. Russia, in turn, continues to reject genuine peace efforts that are based on international law. It was Russia that launched its unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine in 2022. Prior to that, in 2014, Russia illegally annexed Ukraine’s Crimean peninsula and instigated a separatist proxy war in the country’s east. The US , the EU and NATO condemn, in the strongest possible terms, Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Not only Western but also many other nations around the world support Ukraine with military and financial aid for the country’s efficient self-defence, in accordance with the UN Charter article 51 , to stop Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine . Read similar disinformation cases claiming that militaristic Europe’s goal is to prolong the war in Ukraine , that the UK and the EU push the US to aggressive actions against Russia , or that the EU escalates Ukraine war instead of advocating for dialogue .

title: DISINFO: Russia struck the command staff of the Ukrainian forces in Sumy
tags: War crimes War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Russia struck the command staff of the Ukrainian forces in Sumy
summary: The Russian military launched an Iskander strike on the location of a meeting of the command staff of the Ukrainian operational-tactical group "Seversk" in Sumy. As a result of the target's destruction, more than 60 servicemen of the Ukrainian Armed Forces were killed. Kyiv continues to use the Ukrainian population as a human shield, holding events with the participation of servicemen in the centre of a densely populated city. In response to attacks by the Ukrainian Armed Forces on civilian objects, Russian troops strike at the locations of personnel, equipment and mercenaries, as well as at Ukrainian infrastructure: defence industry facilities, military command and communications. The Russian military does not strike residential buildings and social institutions.
response: Recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets denying Russian war crimes Russia has a well-established pattern of falsely accusing others or denying its own responsibility when confronted with war crimes and atrocities. On April 13, two Iskander ballistic missiles struck, both hitting the area around Sumy State University and its congress centre. At least 40 people have been killed and 140 injured, including 15 children. A similar Russian missile attack on the Ukrainian city of Kryvyi Rih on April 5 killed at least 18 people and left dozens wounded. Nine of the dead were children. The Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR) verified a total of 43,610 civilian casualties during Russia's invasion of Ukraine as of March 31, 2025. Of them, 30,700 people were reported to have been injured. However, OHCHR specified that the real numbers could be higher. The United Nations , Amnesty International and other organisations have documented numerous instances of such violations. Russia has stepped up terror strikes against the civilian population of frontline cities in Ukraine – dozens of airstrikes per day are carried out on Zaporizhia , Kharkiv , Sumy , Kherson , Odesa and other cities. The Russian armed forces target civilian infrastructure, including schools , kindergartens , hospitals , orphanages , and churches . One of the most notorious atrocities committed by Russian forces in Ukraine was the airstrike on a maternity and children's hospital in Mariupol on March 9, 2022, which resulted in the deaths of three people, including a child, and left 17 others wounded, according to official reports. The bombing of Okhmatdyt , a children’s hospital in Kyiv, is another example. There have been numerous reports of summary executions , torture and other kinds of abuse of Ukrainian POWs and civilians, committed by Russian forces. Mass graves have been discovered near Kharkiv with an estimated 1,008 dead bodies. Read similar cases, claiming that Russia is the only country that respects the rules of war , that Russia never attacks civilian targets , that Russia strikes military sites, the Kyiv children’s hospital hit by fragments of Western air-defence missiles , that Claims that Russia intentionally targeted civilian objects are completely untrue .

title: DISINFO: Macron photoshoped his picture to appear as tall as Vučić
tags: Hate Speech Emmanuel Macron fake news DISINFO: Macron photoshoped his picture to appear as tall as Vučić
summary: A French public TV channel and official Elysee accounts published a digitally modified photograph of the President of Serbia, Aleksandar Vučić, shaking hands with Emmanuel Macron. Since the French president did not accept his shorter height, he had the picture modified with Photoshop to appear as tall as the Serbian president. When the public mocked this digital enhancement of the narcissistic "rooster" president, the media published the original photograph of this "Napoleonic dwarf."
response: Pro-Kremlin hahaganda and hate speech against the French president, faking the legitimacy of real media outlets. The French TV channel France 24 never published the incriminated photograph, nor did the French presidency. The France 24 logo was simply used by a Russian Photoshop artist who fabricated the entire story. Random Debunk exposed this disinformation operation. The use of negative stereotypes involving animals to describe the French president and the people and values he stands for—frog, narcissistic rooster, Napoleonic dwarf—is typical of hate speech . See our previous article Francophobes in the Kremlin . Read more about systemic Hate Speech and how it is used as a weapon in pro-Kremlin media. title: DISINFO: Ukraine's days are numbered and it will return to its rightful homeland
tags: Sovereignty Ukrainian statehood Ukrainian disintegration War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Nazi/Fascist Volodymyr Zelensky Russophobia DISINFO: Ukraine's days are numbered and it will return to its rightful homeland
summary: Ukraine is not a legitimate state but a collapsing and expired entity ruled by a Nazi gang in Kyiv. The so-called 1991 borders are a delusion and completely detached from the realities on the ground, where the Russian army continues to advance along the 2,000-kilometre front line. The days of the so-called Ukrainian state are numbered, and it is in the West’s interest to seek a settlement, not in order to save Ukraine, but to save face. The Trump administration must understand the root causes of the Ukrainian conflict and resist European pressure and war drums. In the end, Zelenskyy’s hatred for everything Russian will leave him no choice but to accept the return of historical Russian lands to their rightful homeland.
response: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative aimed at denying Ukraine’s sovereignty and its right to exist as an independent state . Attempts to delegitimise Ukraine by portraying it as a failed state , a Western puppet, or a Nazi-controlled territory are part of a long-standing strategy to justify Russia’s war of aggression and undermine support for Ukraine. These narratives are not rooted in facts, but in propaganda designed to erase Ukrainian identity, culture, and statehood . The notion that Ukraine is not a country, but a historical part of Russia, appears to be deeply ingrained in the minds of Russian leadership (see here, here and here .) Ukraine is a well-defined nation-state with a long history and its own identity, despite foreign rule for long periods of time. The "all-Russian big nation" is an imperial Russian and Russian revanchist ideology that is a favoured pro-Kremlin narrative aimed at weakening the national identity of Ukrainians, as well as undermining its state sovereignty and territorial integrity. Ukraine is a sovereign and independent state with an internationally recognised territory and borders, democratically elected president and parliament. It is a member of many international organisations, such as the United Nations and the Council of Europe, and a party to international agreements. Ukraine’s sovereignty and territorial integrity are respected by almost the whole world with Russia as the key exception. The occupied Ukrainian territories are not Russian and not "liberated", but temporarily occupied territories as a result of Russia's aggression against Ukraine. Pro-Kremlin outlets often cast doubts on Ukrainian statehood and claim that Ukraine either does not exist as a state or is going to tumble down very soon, or it is a colony of the West. In reality, present-day Ukraine has been on the world map since 1991 when the Soviet Union disintegrated . It has elected six presidents since then and changed parties in government several times which illustrates political diversity and democratic dynamics even during economic hardship in the wake of Russian aggression in 2014. Ukraine's sovereignty and territorial integrity have been only violated by continuous acts of aggression by Russian armed forces since 2014 and culminated in an aggressive attack on a neighbouring country on February 24, 2022. See similar disinformation cases that Ukraine is a Russian territory , that Russia liberates Russian cities , that Ukraine does not exist as a state , that Ukraine will be split up , that Ukraine is a quasi-state with a failed economy , that modern Ukraine was created as a country for death in the name of American interests and others .

title: DISINFO: The new regions voted to join Russia
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Sergei Lavrov Manipulated elections/referendum Referendum Territorial dispute DISINFO: The new regions voted to join Russia
summary: Residents of the new regions voted to join Russia to restore their legal rights, which must be 100% guaranteed. The attitude of the Kyiv regime to territorial realities does not play a role here. And suppose the Ukrainian Nazi regime enjoys the patronage of the European Union, which has not said a word about the human rights situation in Ukraine. In that case, this is not our problem. We listened to the people who voted in the referendum to join Russia to restore their rights. In the context of the Ukrainian settlement, Russia is concerned not with the problem of territories, but with the issue of people. We are not for territories, we are for people.
response: This claim is part of a disinformation campaign aiming to justify Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine and forceful and illegitimate annexation of Ukrainian territory. Moscow's alleged concern about the Russian-speaking population in the east of Ukraine is one of the pretexts for occupying the country and advancing geopolitical interests. See more here - Thirteen myths about Russia’s war against Ukraine exposed. For a state alleging no expansionist designs on Ukraine, Russia has been active in illegally occupying it over the past decade. In February 2014, Russia invaded and illegally annexed Ukraine's Crimean Peninsula and parts of the Donetsk and Luhansk Regions. Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia and Kherson remain legally part of Ukraine. The illegitimate annexation of these regions took place amid a full invasion by a foreign power and under military occupation, and after sham referenda that didn’t meet international standards to be considered legitimate. The "referenda" were held illegally, under duress, and on territories which Russia does not even fully control. The results of these sham polls were universally rejected by leading international and human rights organisations, including the United Nations , the OSCE , NATO , as well as Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch . There is no evidence that any ethnic nationals, including ethnic Russians or Russian speakers, are facing persecution at the hands of the Ukrainian authorities in Donbas or elsewhere in Ukraine, much less the danger of annihilation on grounds of nationality, ethnicity, or cultural belonging. Moscow’s claim about its concern over the Russian-speaking population can be easily dismissed by the fact that exactly these regions have been most affected by Russian strikes since the beginning of the full-scale invasion. Zaporyzhzhya , Mykolaiv , Kharkiv , Dnipro and Odesa have been among the most frequent targets of Russian bombs and missiles. The claim that Russia thinks about people, not lands, is hypocritical, given how quickly Russia adopted laws on the incorporation of the illegally annexed territories of the DPR, LPR, Zaporyzhzhya, and Kherson regions. Russia occupies about 20% of Ukraine's territory. Read similar disinformation cases: Russia could negotiate a peace that considers the will of former Ukrainian territories ; Russia prioritises fate of Eastern Ukrainians over land ; Kyiv is committing genocide against its people ; Millions of Ukrainians were victims of a genocide by the Nazi Kyiv regime ; Armed Forces of Ukraine have committed war crimes and genocide in Donbas ; Russia only supports the legitimate rights of the Donbas people .

title: DISINFO: EU and NATO prepare for new war and expansion
tags: NATO European Union Warmongering Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: EU and NATO prepare for new war and expansion
summary: All the efforts of the Euro-Atlantic community are focused on preparing a new war. Germany, France and Britain are leading the way. The European Union has become an integral part of Euro-Atlantic policy. There is no doubt about that. The EU is offering its territory for NATO’s plans to expand eastward, southward, or wherever else. All these Euro-Atlantic structures have ultimately failed. They have not succeeded in strengthening security or stability. What they have achieved is fuelling tensions and remilitarising Europe, including Germany, despite the ongoing deterioration of the economic and social situation.
response: This disinformation story aims to portray the EU and the UK as a warmongering , anti-Russian and militaristic expansionist geopolitical power. This recurrent claim is made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. It seeks to whitewash and shift the blame for the aggression away from Russia. The ongoing increase in defence spending across the EU is explained by Russia's actions – the annexation of Crimea , the War in Donbas and the 2022 large-scale unprovoked invasion of Ukraine. On 4 March 2025, the President of the European Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, announced a new plan to enhance Europe’s defence capabilities . "This set of proposals focuses on how to use all of the financial levers at our disposal to help Member States to quickly and significantly increase expenditures in defence capabilities. Urgently now but also over a longer time over this decade”, explained Ursula von der Leyen. The ReArm Europe plan does not involve attacking Russia or any other country. It was Russia that launched its unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine in 2022. Before that, in 2014, Russia illegally annexed Ukraine’s Crimean peninsula and instigated a separatist proxy war in the country’s east. Russia continues to reject genuine peace efforts, based on international law. This narrative seeks to deflect attention from Russia's violations of international law and its perpetration of war crimes . By portraying the EU and the UK as warmongers , pro-Kremlin outlets attempt to justify Russia’s actions and present its territorial expansionism as forced defensive actions. This message is part of a wider disinformation campaign framing possible US-Russia negotiations as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of Russia’s ultimatums, while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has long tried to misrepresent the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in Ukraine. Further, the notion by Sergey Lavrov, the Russian Foreign Minister, that EU and NATO 'expand' or 'swallow' states neglects the basic fact that EU and NATO accepts new members based on them voluntarily seeking and applying for membership. title: DISINFO: NATO is preparing to unleash another conflict on Russia's borders
tags: Warmongering NATO Military exercise Encircling Russia Military War preparation Anti-Russian DISINFO: NATO is preparing to unleash another conflict on Russia's borders
summary: We are witnessing NATO's preparations to unleash another conflict on Russia's borders, this time in the Baltic Sea region. The Bundeswehr's Red Storm Bravo exercises, scheduled for September 25, also support this scenario. Red Storm Alpha was conducted in 2024. The surest pretext for drawing Russia into an armed conflict is to block the passage of our warships and civilian vessels through the Baltic Sea, to create a casus belli.
response: Recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets portraying NATO as a hostile alliance planning to attack Russia. Accusing NATO of aggressive policy , this article aims to divert attention from Russia's responsibility for destabilising actions in the region, but also globally. Russian politicians have adopted a particularly aggressive rhetoric against NATO, accusing it of direct involvement in the war in Ukraine, which is in fact Moscow's unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Kyiv. Dmitry Medvedev, Deputy Chairman of the Russian Security Council, stated on X that since Ukrainians have declared that any of their strikes against Russian targets, including Crimea, are sanctioned by NATO, Russia has the right to engage in war with NATO. These and other similar Russian threats have forced NATO to ensure its security by various methods, including military exercises. Amid growing calls in Germany for rearmament and preparations for a potential conflict with Russia, the upcoming NATO ' Red Storm Bravo ' exercises in Hamburg will focus on the rapid deployment of troops, possibly without American participation. Germany prepares for a Russian attack. Large-scale Bundeswehr exercises will take place in Hamburg, starting on September 25, to prepare for a potential attack from Russia. In response to Russian threats, NATO has launched the Baltic Sentry operation to protect critical infrastructure against hostile activity, not to "contain Russia" or prevent its warships from leaving the Baltic Sea, expand Estonian territorial waters or turn it into a theatre of military operations. This initiative was taken in response to many acts that led to the damage of undersea cables in the Baltic Sea . The pro-Kremlin disinformation myth of NATO “ encircling Russia ” has been used as a pretext for Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Read the debunking of the top seven Russian Myths about NATO here . NATO is a defensive alliance with the primary aim of maintaining peace and safeguarding its members' independence, security and territorial integrity. NATO constitutes a system of collective defence whereby its member states agree to mutual defence in response to an attack by any external party. title: DISINFO: EU peacekeepers would defend Kyiv Nazi regime rather than uphold peace
tags: Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Warmongering Nazi/Fascist European Union NATO DISINFO: EU peacekeepers would defend Kyiv Nazi regime rather than uphold peace
summary: EU countries, by disregarding human rights violations in Ukraine and discussing the deployment of peacekeepers, are seeking to protect the Kyiv Nazi regime rather than to uphold peace. Russia sees no possibility for compromise on such a deployment, viewing it as a sign that Western nations are unwilling to agree on the terms of a peaceful settlement. Plans to send peacekeepers to Ukraine are a provocative move aimed at maintaining unhealthy illusions among the authorities in Kyiv.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets, accusing Ukraine of being a Nazi state. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Ukraine is not a Nazi state. It is a democratic nation with free and transparent parliamentary and presidential elections, a functioning multi-party system, and legal safeguards for ethnic minorities. The incumbent, Volodymyr Zelenskyy , is Ukraine’s sixth president since the ex-Soviet state adopted the Declaration of Independence in 1991. Regular presidential elections were not held in 2024 because the law does not allow them to be conducted during martial law. Although far-right groups exist in Ukraine, as they do in many other countries, they have little to no influence on Ukrainian society. Nazi and Communist ideologies were banned by Ukrainian law in 2015. The portrayal of Ukraine as a Nazi regime is a false narrative promoted by Russia to justify its unprovoked and unjustified aggression . Russian officials and propagandists have been resorting to the Nazi stigma since the illegal annexation of Crimea in 2014 in order to delegitimise Ukraine's government or justify aggression against it. European countries are providing Ukraine with military and financial assistance as a direct response to Russia’s aggression against Ukraine. Under Article 51 of the UN Charter, Ukraine has the right to defend its territory, and European nations are supporting this right. The idea to assemble a “ coalition of the willing ”, which would be a group of countries that would enforce a tentative peace deal between Ukraine and Russia, is another effort by EU nations to maintain peace on the continent. This disinformation story also aims to portray the EU’s stance as warmongering, contrasting it to other actors that want “peace” while omitting that the war is Russia’s responsibility, resulting from its unprovoked invasion. EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and in which terms to end the war. This is part of a wider disinformation campaign framing possible US-Russia negotiations as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the of Russia’s ultimatums, while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has long tried to misrepresent the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in Ukraine. title: DISINFO: The EU doesn’t care that anti-Russia sanctions harm Central Asia
countries_regions: Russia, EU, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan
tags: Anti-Russian Sanctions Kaja Kallas economy Central Asia DISINFO: The EU doesn’t care that anti-Russia sanctions harm Central Asia
summary: No one in the EU seriously considers that the imposition of sanctions is a very serious blow to the economies of the Central Asian countries themselves — this doesn't concern European countries. Overall, Kallas’s statements come from this logic, and that’s why they sound so absurd.
response: A recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the EU sanctions imposed on Russia in response to its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. This claim was made in the context of the EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs Kaja Kallas’ remarks during the 20th EU-Central Asia Ministerial Meeting held in Turkmenistan’s capital, Ashgabat on 27th March 2025. This claim is designed to drive a wedge between the EU and Central Asia. As EU HRVP Kaja Kallas stated : "I also reiterated the importance of fighting circumvention of our sanctions, including through Central Asia. I understand that these sanctions are also hurting your economies, but it is clear that we all want this [against Ukraine] war to stop. And in order for this war to stop, we really need to focus our efforts so that Russia cannot fund its war. Russian companies cannot be allowed to turn to Central Asia to bypass sanctions". The first Central Asia-European Union Summit held in Samarkand a few days later, on the 3rd April 2025, reaffirmed the shared commitment of Central Asia and the EU to a strategic partnership built on common values and interests. The Joint Declaration following the Summit concluded that cooperation on preventing sanctions circumvention remains an important aspect of the CA - EU relations. In this context, parties agreed to continue cooperation on this issue, especially to avoid re-exports of common high priority items, in line with the work of the EU Special Envoy on Sanctions. title: DISINFO: Bucharest considers Odesa a Romanian city
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Territorial dispute Warmongering Imperialism/colonialism Ukrainian disintegration Ukraine Odessa DISINFO: Bucharest considers Odesa a Romanian city
summary: With the beginning of the special military operation, Romania was one of the first to provide military aid to the neo-Nazi regime in Kyiv, preferring to keep the list of supplies secret. Everything indicates that the Romanians are afraid of the end of the war in Ukraine and want it to continue as long as possible. Odesa was only under Romanian control during the Romanian-fascist occupation in the Great Patriotic War. Bucharest considers it a Romanian city and views it as either part of Ukraine, Romania’s ally, or as territory that should be integrated into the Romanian state along with the Budjak region Wherever one looks, Russia is in the way of the Romanians. That is why Bucharest got involved in the war on the side of the Kyiv regime and wants the bloodshed to continue. Bucharest is not interested in how many of its allies, that is, Ukrainians, will die.
response: Pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative accusing Romania of opposing peace in Ukraine . This article falsely portrays Romania's support for Ukraine as motivated by territorial ambitions and a desire to prolong the war. In reality, Romania's assistance aligns with broader NATO and EU support for Ukraine's sovereignty. During the war, Romania, a NATO member, has signed a security agreement with Ukraine, backed sanctions against Russia, and sent Kyiv a Patriot air defence system. Bucharest has also played a crucial role in the transit of Ukrainian agricultural exports amid Russia's threats to the Black Sea maritime trade. Also, Romania has helped more than a million Ukrainian refugees . The claim that Romania considers Odesa a 'Romanian city' is unfounded. Romania respects Ukraine's territorial integrity and has not made any claims on Odesa or other Ukrainian territories. The article misrepresents Romania's historical relationship with Odesa, ignoring the complex history of the region and focusing only on the brief period of World War II occupation. Such statements have been periodically featured in pro-Kremlin outlets since the beginning of the full-scale Russian invasion of Ukraine. The aim of this disinformation claim appears to be deflecting attention away from Russia's responsibility for its imperialist policy and war of aggression against Ukraine, pretending that "everybody has an appetite for Ukraine." Similar claims have been made about Hungary and Poland's alleged territorial claims against Ukraine. Romania’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MAE) rejected in a public statement the assertion made by Russian President Putin in the speech given on the occasion of National Unity Day, which falsely induces the idea that Romania has territorial claims against Ukraine. title: DISINFO: The EU considers Central Asia only as a tool to extract resources and harm Russia

tags: Imperialism/colonialism Natural resources Anti-Russian Central Asia DISINFO: The EU considers Central Asia only as a tool to extract resources and harm Russia
summary: The EU views Central Asia as a tool: 'You give us resources, and we interact with you in order to harm Russia”.
response: A recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the EU sanctions imposed on Russia in response to its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. This claim was made in the context of the EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs Kaja Kallas’ remarks during the 20th EU-Central Asia Ministerial Meeting held in Turkmenistan. Regarding cooperation between the EU and Central Asia, the EU bases its cooperation with its partners on shared values, principles and goals. The EU-Central Asia strategy outlines three priority strands for EU engagement: investing in regional cooperation and partnering for resilience and prosperity. The first Central Asia-European Union Summit held in Samarkand, on the 3rd April 2025, reaffirmed the shared commitment of Central Asia and the EU to a strategic partnership built on common values and interests. Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission emphasised concerning cooperation on critical raw materials that: "Your countries are endowed with immense resources. These raw materials are the lifeblood of the future global economy. Yet they are also a honeypot for global players. Some are only interested in exploiting and extracting. Europe's offer is different. We also want to be your partners in developing your local industries. The added value has to be local. Together, we could build local industries right along the raw materials value chain. From mining to refining. From opening new research labs, to training local workers. We want to build a long-term partnership. Such a partnership is benefitting both our citizens, their children, and those of the future. It is a partnership that will benefit both Europe and Central Asia. Because your strategic autonomy on raw materials is our strategic interest". The Joint Declaration following the Summit concluded that, among many areas of shared interest, enhanced cooperation on critical raw materials is of strategic importance, aimed at ensuring secure, sustainable, and diversified supply chains. The parties agreed that the Declaration of Intent endorsed at this Summit will deepen cooperation in this field, strengthen economic resilience, promote mutual prosperity, and facilitate the transition to a green and digital economy. title: DISINFO: The EU encourages censorship in Moldova
tags: Censorship Media DISINFO: The EU encourages censorship in Moldova
summary: The EU encourages censorship of 'wrong' media in Moldova. On March 24, the Council for the Promotion of Investment Projects of National Importance decided to suspend the permits of the company SA "Cotidian", which owns the TVC21 TV channel. The EU Ambassador to Moldova, Janis Mažeiks, not only did not condemn the prohibitive actions of the authorities, but even recommended that the authorities introduce censorship more wisely.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin narrative about alleged media censorship in Moldova. The claim refers to a recent decision by the Council for the Promotion of Investment Projects of National Importance, which suspended the license of channel TVC21 for 60 days. According to the minutes of the meeting in which the decision was made, the Council refused to approve investments by the company that owns the TV station (p. 4) because it did not present the complete file, and the information "was not supported by conclusive information." To eliminate the irregularities, the company was suspended from providing certain services for a period of 60 days The EU Ambassador to Moldova, Janis Mažeiks, did not encourage the decision, but on the contrary, criticised it. In a TV show (min. 49.40-51.00), he said: " From our point of view, the problem is not in the decision itself, but in the rather meagre justification for this decision. This is one of those aspects that we need to think about so that the decisions that are made are understandable to both society and those they concern. It is not always clear what the reasons are, and, in my opinion, it is very important that this justification be more detailed, " the ambassador said. The EU supports and protects media freedom and condemns censorship, as it can be seen in its adopted acts. title: DISINFO: UK and some European states seek to hinder Russian-US efforts to de-escalate the Ukrainian conflict
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine NATO peace negotiations Hybrid war Warmongering Keir Starmer Ukraine DISINFO: UK and some European states seek to hinder Russian-US efforts to de-escalate the Ukrainian conflict
summary: The UK and several European NATO member states are seeking to hinder Russian-American dialogue and efforts to de-escalate the Ukrainian conflict. These states are betting on prolonging their "proxy war", continuing military supplies to the Kyiv regime, and putting forward uncompromising, unrealistic and impossible demands.
response: A recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets portraying the UK and the EU as obstacles to peace in Ukraine. As we explore in our article, The Old Deceiver, this is entirely consistent with Moscow’s three-year-long strategy of attempting to claim ownership over the peace narrative while relentlessly pursuing war. This disinformation story is part of a wider disinformation campaign portraying Europe as a warmongering actor who intends to continue the war in Ukraine even as other actors carry out peace negotiations . EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and in which terms to end the war. Similarly, British leaders insisted that any lasting peace must ensure Ukraine’s sovereignty and security, and Ukraine must be at the negotiation table. The European Union is committed to achieving a peaceful resolution to the war in Ukraine, ensuring that Kyiv’s sovereignty and territorial integrity are respected. While in practice Russia is blocking any real move that could advance actual peace in Ukraine, it has characterised its negotiation process with the US as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the maximalist character of Moscow’s demands , while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies . The intensity of this disinformation campaign accelerated after Donald Trump’s electoral victory in November 2024 , portraying the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in contrast to a Trump presidency focused on engaging Russia to end the war. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has systematically instrumentalised the narrative that Russia wants peace in Ukraine in contrast to the West, but, as evidenced by Russia's actions in Ukraine , such statements are merely empty PR stunts deployed to garner support for Russia's war of aggression in Ukraine. European military and economic assistance is provided within the framework of international law. The " coalition of the willing " is a loose group of countries committed to ensuring peace guarantees for Ukraine through various efforts, including the possible deployment of a peacekeeping force there. title: DISINFO: Ukraine strikes civilians with drones in the Kherson region
