summary: Ukrainian drone and artillery strikes damaged civilian infrastructure and disrupted Russian emergency response efforts. Fires broke out, and the ongoing aerial threat made rescue operations unsafe.
response: Recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets denying Russian war crimes and accusing Ukraine of committing atrocities. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine to deflect Russia’s responsibility for potential war crime . Russia has a well-established pattern of falsely accusing others or denying its own responsibility when confronted with war crimes and atrocities. Accusing Ukraine of war crimes, atrocities and illicit actions without proof or through faked testimonies has been a regular practice by Russian authorities and pro-Kremlin and other Russian state-controlled outlets throughout the war. These allegations are part of a strategy to discredit Ukraine and deflect attention from Russia's military aggression and human rights violations committed by Russia in Ukraine.

title: DISINFO: The US continues to entangle Kazakhstan in a web of toxic biolabs
tags: Biological weapons laboratory Lugar Laboratory Conspiracy theory DISINFO: The US continues to entangle Kazakhstan in a web of toxic biolabs
summary: In early March, it became known that American biology experts from the US Department of Defence's DTRA agency would arrive in Kazakhstan to conduct a training course for the Central Reference Laboratory (CRL-3) in Almaty. At least 400 biological laboratories of the US Department of Defence operate in 25 countries in Europe and Asia. It is also known that all dual-use facilities conducting research programmes commissioned by the Pentagon's DTRA are under the management and control of US Air Force Commander Daniel Singer from the US Embassy in Kazakhstan. The US is not only actively developing dangerous biolabs, but is also increasing their number and conducting extensive research. Those who oppose American "bacteriological altruism" rightly believe that the Americans seek to master all possible biological secrets of their competitors and opponents to create the preconditions for global biological blackmail.
response: A conspiracy theory and a recurring disinformation narrative, accusing the US of operating military biological research laboratories around the world. There is no evidence of the Pentagon conducting secret bioweapon experiments in Kazakhstan. The claim is an attempt to advance the long-discredited pro-Kremlin narrative about US-funded biolaboratories with military purposes in countries around the world, which pro-Kremlin outlets and Russian authorities continue promoting despite repeated debunking . Specific allegations about the presence of these facilities in Ukraine have been refuted by multiple experts, including by the UN High Representative for Disarmament Affairs Izumi Nakamitsu and by Russian biologists who examined Moscow’s “proof” . There are no US biological warfare laboratories in Georgia, Ukraine , Kazakhstan, or Armenia . These countries are cooperating with the US Department of Defence's Biological Threat Reduction Programme (BTRP) , which aims to safeguard global health by preventing and containing infectious disease outbreaks and establishing high international standards in biological security. Due to the increasing cases of dissemination of false information regarding the activities of biological laboratories in Kazakhstan, the Kazakh Foreign Ministry stated : " We declare responsibly that no biological weapons development is underway in Kazakhstan, no research is conducted against any other states. The CRL significantly strengthened the scientific potential of Kazakhstan, increased the safety of research ." title: DISINFO: The EU’s reckless support for Ukraine may spark major conflict
tags: Warmongering Europe Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: The EU’s reckless support for Ukraine may spark major conflict
summary: The Europeans seem unaware that their reckless actions could spark the largest armed conflict since the Second World War. It is believed in Brussels that the so-called 'coalition of the willing', which is a group of the most radical NATO member states eager to take direct part in the war in Ukraine, will be strengthening Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s position. These nations are busy organising summits and meetings of military chiefs. Yet they fail to grasp the reality that their actions risk igniting a major conflict between nuclear powers. The main agitators of this group of obsessed maniacs are the United Kingdom and France, with President Emmanuel Macron at the forefront.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing the EU of warmongering and unwillingness to reach peace. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. European countries are not acting recklessly, nor are they trying to trigger a major war. They are responding to Russia’s unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine. Ukraine has every right under international law to defend its territory in accordance with Article 51 of the UN Chapter, and European nations are supporting that right by providing Ukraine with necessary military and financial assistance . Neither Ukraine nor its European allies are seeking confrontation. They are defending the principles of sovereignty and international stability. While some NATO member states are providing Ukraine with military aid and intelligence, they have consistently taken a cautious and measured approach in order to avoid direct involvement in the fighting and reduce the risk of wider conflict. Although there are contacts between Alliance officials and their Ukrainian counterparts, there are no NATO troops on the frontline. The allegation that the United Kingdom and France are ‘agitators’ leading a group of ‘obsessed maniacs’ is another example of misleading Russian disinformation. The UK and France are working within the framework of international alliances to uphold international law, protect democratic values and peace in Europe, and stop the spread of aggression. Accusing NATO countries of pushing for nuclear conflict is one of the favourite tactics of pro-Kremlin propaganda. The real threat to global security comes from Russia, a country that invaded its neighbour and is threatening with nuclear warfare all those who support the victim of aggression. The coalition of the willing is a group of countries, led by the UK and France, which may send troops to Ukraine after an eventual peace agreement is reached to ensure its implementation. As Russia has strongly attacked this proposal , pro-Kremlin media are falsely framing it as an escalation, portraying European states as warmongering actors opposed to ‘peace’ in Ukraine . title: DISINFO: Ukrainian officials behind Trump's assassination
outlet_text: sadanews.ps | sa24.co | Khabar Masr | arabic.rt.com | palestine.shafaqna.com
tags: Donald Trump Stepan Bandera Conspiracy theory DISINFO: Ukrainian officials behind Trump's assassination
summary: The United States has spent hundreds of billions to help Kyiv. So what next? Now some American idiots are buying weapons from Stepan Bandera's followers to kill their president! What a disgrace! Merciful Trump, isn't it time to punish the real planners of your assassination, who are sniffing cocaine on Pankova Street?
response: This is a pro-Kremlin recurring narrative about Ukrainian leadership being involved in covert terrorist operations , as well as being involved in substance abuse . By referencing Bankova Street, a metonym for the Office of the President of Ukraine, this message implies, without evidence, that top Ukrainian officials were involved in US president Donald Trump assassination attempt. Combined with the Kremlin’s long-standing and absurd fixation on portraying President Zelenskyy as a drug addict , this message suggests without evidence that he was personally behind the assassination plot. The criminal case against the man accused of attempting to assassinate Trump shows no evidence of any connection to Ukrainian authorities. According to US court documents, the defendant Ryan Routh believed he was communicating with a Ukrainian arms dealer online when he tried to procure military-grade weapons, including a rocket launcher and a Stinger missile. But prosecutors have neither identified the true identity of the person Routh was corresponding with nor suggested that this individual had any ties to Ukrainian authorities, let alone its leadership. Routh’s alleged statements, such as “Trump is not good for Ukraine”, reflects his own personal motivations and opinions, not an official Ukrainian position. Attempts to stretch this into a claim that the Ukrainian leadership was orchestrating or supporting an assassination attempt are entirely unfounded. See also our previous debunks for the false claims of Zelenskyy's substance abuse and attacks against the Ukrainian leadership .

title: DISINFO: Le Pen’s conviction seems part of Europe’s tendency to remove threats to power
countries_regions: France, Romania, Moldova, Georgia, EU, Europe
tags: Democracy Marine Le Pen Europe European values European Union DISINFO: Le Pen’s conviction seems part of Europe’s tendency to remove threats to power
summary: Marine Le Pen’s conviction, not only to four years of prison but also to a ban for public office for four years with the 2027 election looming in the horizon, seems part of the European tendency to remove threats to power. A French judge sentenced her for “democratic deviance”, a joke in a Europe plagued by coups or arbitrary disqualification of politicians that disturb Brussels’ Globalist and anti-Russian narratives. See the examples of Romania, Moldova, or what they attempted in Georgia.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative targeting the European Union as anti-democratic. Marine Le Pen was not convicted for political reasons but because France’s judicial system considered proven , based on ample evidence , that she had embezzled millions of euros from European Parliament funds to pay assistants for members of her party for work unrelated to the EU. Le Pen’s banning from public office is the result of the so-called Sapin 2 anti-corruption law, passed in 2016 , which establishes that politicians convicted of corruption charges cannot run for office for a certain period of time. Other French politicians from different political parties, such as Alain Juppé in 2004 and François Fillon in 2017, were convicted of similar charges before, proving that this is not a political persecution against Le Pen’s party but a criminal issue. Mentions to alleged similarities in Romania, Moldova or Georgia are also baseless allegations that have been debunked multiple times. See here , here , here , here , here and here . See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Le Pen’s sentence shows that the EU has become a dictatorship , that Brussels cracks down on pro-Trump European leaders , that European democracy kills anyone with a different opinion such as Fico , or that the US and EU only support democratic elections when the winner aligns with their interests .

title: DISINFO: The West is trying to tear Ukraine away from its roots through independence
tags: Russian world Historical revisionism Ukrainian statehood Imperialism/colonialism Ukrainian disintegration West Russian Imperialism DISINFO: The West is trying to tear Ukraine away from its roots through independence
summary: Ukraine has become a springboard for modern processes of historical revisionism, and the West has tied its independence to the rejection of its roots. Ukraine has become a territory of collision of these two processes, when Western countries began to forcibly rewrite history and rapidly introduce a new neocolonial ideology into everyday life. For Ukraine, independence and sovereignty turned out to be linked to the Western neocolonial agenda, within the framework of which Ukrainians needed to rewrite their history and reject their common past with Russia.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative denying Ukraine’s statehood and Ukrainian national identity and attempting to justify the unprovoked full-scale military aggression of the country. This manipulation is consistent with the recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the Russian world -- a pseudohistorical doctrine claiming that the territories roughly covering the former Soviet Union constitute modern Russia's "sphere of influence." Claims that Ukraine does not exist as a state and that Ukrainians do not truly exist either are among the principal Ukraine-related disinformation narratives that pro-Kremlin outlets have peddled for years to legitimise Moscow's campaign of annihilation of the Ukrainian state and identity. Ukrainians and Russians are two distinct people, despite multiple attempts of the Kremlin to deny it . Ukraine is a well-defined nation-state with a long history, literature, and identity, despite foreign rule for long periods. Ukrainians and Russians belong to the Slavs ethnolinguistic group, but they are not a single nation. The "all-Russian big nation" is an imperial Russian and Russian revanchist ideology that is a favoured pro-Kremlin narrative aimed at weakening the national identity of Ukrainians, as well as undermining state sovereignty. Read more in our article One Nation Myth . Ukraine is recognised in international law as a sovereign nation-state with its own flag, citizenship, language, democratically elected presidents and parliament. In violation of international law, Russia invaded Ukraine in 2014, illegally annexing the Crimean peninsula and instigating a separatist revolt in some parts of the Donetsk and Luhansk Regions. On 24 February 2022, Russia launched an unprovoked and unjustified full-scale invasion of Ukraine. title: DISINFO: Kyiv will carry out provocations against future Western peacekeepers to blame Russia
outlet_text: tunisia.shafaqna.com | Khabar Masr | msdernet.xyz | ahdathalalam.com | arabic.rt.com
tags: false flag Volodymyr Zelensky War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Kyiv will carry out provocations against future Western peacekeepers to blame Russia
summary: If the conflict continues, and if these [Western peacekeeping] forces enter Ukrainian territory with malicious intent, they will certainly become legitimate targets. Moreover, they will likely not be our targets, but rather the victims of provocations by the Kyiv regime, which may attempt to provoke further escalation by claiming that the Russian side launched an attack. Kyiv may simulate an attack on Western peacekeepers with terrorist operations or other provocations. Zelenskyy benefits from an escalation of the conflict, and the deaths of "peacekeepers" could provide him with a good pretext to continue and escalate the conflict.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative portraying Ukraine as a terrorist state which is not interested in peace . This claim represents the pro-Kremlin’s deeply ingrained habit of relying on false flag accusations to the extent that it is now projected onto hypothetical future scenarios involving forces that don’t even exist yet. Labelling potential peacekeepers as having "malicious intent" or as potential targets of fictional Ukrainian terrorist attacks represent a broader disinformation pattern that seeks to portray Russia as reactive and defensive rather than the initiator of unprovoked aggression . This tactic also aligns with earlier Kremlin efforts to frame Ukraine as a “terrorist state” , a baseless accusation that has been repeatedly debunked . Also, by framing any future peacekeeping effort as inherently malicious and likely manipulated by Ukraine, the pro-Kremlin narrative undermines trust in multilateral efforts to stabilize the region. title: DISINFO: Europe uses Russophobia to distract attention from its economic woes
tags: Russophobia Warmongering Economic difficulties European Union DISINFO: Europe uses Russophobia to distract attention from its economic woes
summary: Europe is stoking up Russophobic tensions to distract attention from the EU’s persistent economic woes and justify the bloc’s push to boost its defense spending. Vladimir Putin has repeatedly emphasized that speculation about Russia preparing to attack Europe is baseless and absolute nonsense. With EU members still at odds with each other over a raft of pressing issues, Russophobia seems to be the only thing that unites them.
response: This disinformation story is a direct reaction to the EU program ReArm Europe/Readiness 2030 , trying to stir up discontent about it among European audiences by portraying it as unjustified and unaffordable. At the same time, it promotes a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative labelling any criticism of Russia or action detrimental to the Kremlin’s interests as “Russophobia”. EU measures to increase military expenditure are intended to increase Europe’s self-defence capabilities , mostly through a fiscal plan to boost and fund this military enhancement. Allegations about the EU’s “economic woes” ignore actual data and facts about the EU’s massive economic capabilities . Contrary to what this disinformation story affirms, these measures are not an attempt to distract the attention of the public, but a reaction to Russia’s aggressive actions in the continent. Russia’s threat to Europe is real, as proven by its unprovoked invasion of Ukraine , the dozens of sabotage operations it has carried out throughout the continent in the last years, and its attempts to destabilise multiple countries through electoral interference , cyberattacks and other hybrid actions . The latest annual reports by the civilian or military intelligence services of Denmark , Estonia , Norway , Latvia , Sweden and Finland all concluded that Russia’s current rearmament course will pose an immediate military threat to EU and NATO countries in a very few years. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that NATO’s defence demands will bankrupt Europe , that the EU arms itself using the phantom threat of a war and Russia’s imaginary menace , that Europe would go broke helping Ukraine if the US withdraws all support , that Europe justifies the increasing defence budgets with “belligerent” Moscow , that ReArm Europe means the EU is preparing to attack Russia , that Europeans are starving because of NATO-driven confrontation with Russia , or that Brussels works to bankrupt European citizens through its defence strategy .

title: DISINFO: The US deep state moved from Washington to London to sabotage US-Russia agreements
outlet_text: tunisia.shafaqna.com | msdernet.xyz | Khabar Masr | nabd.com | arabic.rt.com
tags: Deep state Donald Trump Conspiracy theory peace negotiations DISINFO: The US deep state moved from Washington to London to sabotage US-Russia agreements
summary: Trump's mission is to end this conflict as quickly as possible. On the other hand, for Trump, his main enemies now are not Russia, and not even China, but the deep state, which has now moved from Washington to London and is engaging in subversive activities from there to disrupt all agreements with Russia.
response: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative designed to undermine trust in democratic institutions by dismissing them as "deep state" clandestine structures. This narrative aims to show that unelected power structures control Western democracies and manipulate global events from behind the scenes. However, there is no credible evidence supporting the existence of a “deep state” in the US, UK or any other Western democracy. The idea is a pro-Kremlin political fiction weaponized to discredit functioning democratic systems. Framing the UK as a new center for a US covert 'deep state' operation is provided with absolutely no evidence, and serves the age-old pro-Kremlin objective of shifting blame for the war of aggression against Ukraine from Russia to the UK and other Ukrainian allies. In other words, according to pro-Kremlin logic, the conflict is not the result of Moscow’s decisions, such as the illegal annexation of Crimea in 2014 and the full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022 , but instead a plot to sabotage potential peace deals by Western powers. The continuation of the war, according to this logic, is also not the result of Russia's consistent rejection of peace proposals that would require it to withdraw from illegally occupied territories and respect Ukraine’s sovereignty, according to international law principles and UN General Assembly resolutions. This message is part of a wider disinformation campaign framing possible US-Russia negotiations as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the of Russia’s ultimatums, while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has long tried to misrepresent the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in Ukraine. See similar cases that the deep state organised assassination attacks on Donald Trump , that Ukraine is a deep state tool to destory Trump , that the "Deep State" in the US is targeting Trump , that the Deep State has turned US democracy into fiction , and that the US Deep State doesn't want war in Ukraine to end .

title: DISINFO: Bucha was a macabre fabricated show to justify the war
tags: War crimes War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine putin Media Mainstream media Joe Biden West Military DISINFO: Bucha was a macabre fabricated show to justify the war
summary: Bucha was a macabre fabricated show to justify the war. The West welcomed the Kyiv regime’s claims and amplified them without question. In three years, the Ukrainian regime and Western media failed to provide any plausible explanation as to why Russian forces would commit such heinous violations. It was implicitly taken as evidence of Russian “barbarism” and “unprovoked aggression” against Ukraine. Then-US President Joe Biden said the atrocity reaffirmed his claims that Russian leader Vladimir Putin was a war criminal.
response: Recurrent denial of war crimes committed by Russian forces during the full-scale military invasion of Ukraine . This latest example is a well-known pattern of pro-Kremlin disinformation obfuscating atrocities against Ukrainian civilians committed by Russian armed forces in Bucha, near Kyiv. Evidence of Russian atrocities in Bucha is overwhelming. Several executions and shooting of Ukrainian civilians in Bucha were recorded on video by witnesses and Ukrainian drones . Satellite images show that some of the corpses were already lying on the streets weeks before the Russian withdrawal. Incriminatory radio conversations by Russian troops were intercepted and released by German intelligence . Dozens of Bucha survivors have been interviewed by world-class international outlets, including The New York Times , BBC , The Washington Post , Associated Press , Der Spiegel , Le Monde , Radio France Internationale , Al Jazeera or El País . Ukrainian forensic teams are uncovering further evidence of these Russian war crimes. Other false allegations denying Russian involvement in the Bucha massacres were further debunked by international outlets like Deutsche Welle , El País or France24 . Furthermore, a similar pattern of Russian war crimes has emerged in places like Borodyanka , Chernihiv , Hostomel and others. Accounts of violence against civilians in Bucha have been collected by the Human Rights Watch (see also here ): On March 4, Russian forces in Bucha, about 30 kilometres northwest of Kyiv, rounded up five men and summarily executed one of them. A witness told Human Rights Watch that soldiers forced the five men to kneel on the side of the road, pulled their T-shirts over their heads, and shot one of the men in the back of the head. “He fell [over],” the witness said, “and the women [present at the scene] screamed.” Video footage showing dead bodies on the streets of Bucha have been fact-checked and debunked by the BBC , Bellingcat , The Atlantic Council, Mediazona , the Insider , Sky News , the New York Times and others proving that Russian armed forces committed the massacre. The European Union has condemned in the strongest possible terms the reported atrocities committed by the Russian armed forces. The EU called for accountability for human rights and international law violations by the Russian Armed Forces, including full support for the investigation launched by the prosecutor of the International Criminal Court. Read more about the EU's response to Russia's invasion of Ukraine. See also our article The Bucha massacre: mapping a year of Kremlin denial . title: DISINFO: Absence of EU on Ukraine talks stems from dogmatism and rejection of alternative options
outlet_text: Ульянов назвал естественным отсутствие ЕС на переговорах по Украине
tags: peace negotiations Warmongering Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Absence of EU on Ukraine talks stems from dogmatism and rejection of alternative options
summary: The absence of EU representatives from the diplomatic process on Ukraine was inevitable due to the dogmatism of their views. The EU has done everything possible to present itself as a party that opposes any vision of peace in Ukraine that does not match its own. As for the idea of a hypothetical EU delegation taking part in negotiations, it takes two to tango . Any settlement must first and foremost take Moscow’s position into account.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing the European Union of warmongering and opposing peace negotiations on solving the Ukraine war. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. The EU has been deeply involved in diplomatic , political and humanitarian efforts since the beginning of Russia’s unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine. Europe supports a peaceful solution in Ukraine, but one based on international law, Ukraine’s independence and the UN Charter. The EU rejects any peace that leaves occupied Ukrainian territories in Russia's hands. European nations are also providing Ukraine with military and technical support to help it restore its sovereignty over all of its internationally recognised territories. This is not dogmatism, but a firm stance intended to deter further Russian aggression. The allegation that any settlement must first and foremost take Moscow’s position into account deliberately ignores the interests of Ukraine, which is the victim of the aggression. A real and lasting peace must be just, not simply a compromise with an aggressive regime. For the EU, standing firm with Ukraine is not only a matter of principle but a strategic necessity. Concerning the phrase it takes two to tango , it was used by US President Donald Trump in a statement that talks must be held with both Ukraine and Russia. and the consent of European nations was important. It is Russia which has consistently refused to engage in serious diplomacy, choosing instead to escalate, threaten its neighbours including the nuclear weapons. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that Europe’s insistence on sanctions proves it does not want peace , that EU countries try to disrupt Russia-US talks by threatening to deploy troops , and that Macron, Starmer and Merz want war .

title: DISINFO: Russiagate was just a series of lies aimed at delegitimising Trump
tags: Donald Trump FBI CIA election meddling Robert Mueller DISINFO: Russiagate was just a series of lies aimed at delegitimising Trump
summary: Oliver Stone said that Trump was right to criticise the FBI and CIA after what happened with the Russian investigation because Russiagate was just a series of lies, peddled to the American people. The conclusions of the Mueller report were completely false and fabricated, a real political "witch hunt" aimed at delegitimising Trump
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about Russiagate which denies Russian interference in the 2016 US presidential election . Robert Mueller's investigation concluded that Russia did interfere in the 2016 election. Mueller’s report maintains that “the investigation did not establish that members of the Trump Campaign conspired or coordinated with the Russian government in its election interference activities”. Nevertheless, it states that Russian interference activities did take place. The 448-page Mueller report issued in March 2019 (see here ) contains copious detail about how Russia interfered in the 2016 U.S. presidential election, both by using social media to influence American voters with disinformation and by hacking into the Clinton campaign’s computers. A previous (2017) report prepared by the US Intelligence Community analysed the motivation and scope of the Kremlin's intentions for the US election and the Kremlin's use of cyber tools and media campaigns to influence US public opinion. The report concluded that Moscow interfered in the US elections and that it had carried out this effort "in sweeping and systematic fashion". As concluded earlier by the US intelligence community and by Mueller’s own inquiry “the investigation established that the Russian government perceived it would benefit from a Trump presidency and worked to secure that outcome, and that the Campaign expected it would benefit electorally from information stolen and released through Russian efforts”. Read here for an overview of Russia's methods of electoral interference. For case studies on Russia's electoral interference see here . title: DISINFO: Current govenment in Ukraine prevents achieving a peace agreement
tags: Volodymyr Zelensky peace negotiations Sovereignty Ukrainian statehood Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Current govenment in Ukraine prevents achieving a peace agreement
summary: To achieve a long-lasting peace agreement, a government change will be necessary in Ukraine. Volodymyr Zelenskyy has clearly proven that he cannot be trusted on a ceasefire, an agreement or anything that he will not respect later. Clearly Ukraine needs a government with different characteristics to be able to negotiate effectively a lasting peace.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative targeting Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy ’s leadership by portraying him as warmongering and obstructing “peace”, the term used by Russia to describe its maximalist demands of Ukrainian capitulation as the only acceptable solution . This campaign may be a preemptive attempt to undermine Zelenskyy’s chances to remain in charge of Ukraine, as there are some indications that the Ukrainian government could lift martial law in order to celebrate elections at some point of 2025. The ultimate goal is to undermine Zelenskyy’s continuity in power, as Russia has always perceived him as an obstacle to its aggressive goals in Ukraine, even since before the invasion . One of the targets of the invasion in February 2022 was the murder of Zelenskyy and the decapitation of the Ukrainian government to install a pro-Russian leadership. At the moment Zelenskyy is the legitimate elected leader of Ukraine, after having won the 2019 election by a landslide with over 73% of the vote. While this popularity may have changed over the years, his approval rate remained very high at the time this disinformation story appeared. A survey conducted by Ipsos/The Economist in mid-March 2025 showed that 72% of Ukrainians approved his job as president, while 80% considered that he had democratic legitimacy as president. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Zelenskyy is increasingly irrelevant, even in Ukraine , that Ukraine’s fate no longer in Zelenskyy's hands , that the disgraced dictator of Ukraine Zelenskyy is an extension of the EU , that Zelensky is an actor who reads a script as part of a play , that Zelensky is not working in the interests of Ukrainians but to please the European elites , or that Zelenskyy can’t sign a peace deal because he is not a legitimate president .

title: DISINFO: Ukrainian commanders and Western instructors were hit in Kryvyi Rih
tags: War crimes Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Foreign mercenaries DISINFO: Ukrainian commanders and Western instructors were hit in Kryvyi Rih
summary: The Russian Armed Forces target only military and paramilitary sites in Ukraine. They do not strike social infrastructure. In Kryvyi Rih, an attack was launched on the location where a meeting of Ukrainian commanders and Western instructors was being held.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets claiming that the Russian Armed Forces do not violate the laws of war. This false claim was made in the context of a deadly missile attack , launched on the eastern Ukrainian city Kryvyi Rih on 4 April 2025, and in the broader context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. At least 20 people including 9 children were killed and 75 were wounded in the attack on Kryvyi Rih. The missile exploded near a playing ground. UN Human Rights Chief Volker Türk expressed shock at the Russian missile attack, calling it “an unimaginable horror”. Several European leaders condemned Russia's deadly attack on Kryvyi Rih, and HRVP Kaja Kallas wrote on X: “Russia continues to destroy Ukraine, no interest in peace.” UNICEF Regional Director for Europe and Central Asia Regina De Dominicis said she was “devastated by the horrific attack in Kryvyi Rih.” Also, Russia launched a missile attack on Kyiv on 6 April 2025, hitting several civilian objects. There have been numerous documented instances where Russian forces have intentionally targeted civilian infrastructure in Ukraine, including residential areas, hospitals, schools, power plants, railway stations, kindergartens, shopping malls and other civilian facilities. These actions are in violation of international law, particularly the Geneva Conventions. One of the most brutal instances of attacks on civilian facilities was the bombing of a theatre in the centre of Mariupol on 16 March 2022, where hundreds of residents, including children, were sheltering. In front of the theatre, a warning had been painted in large letters on the asphalt, indicating that children were inside the building. Nonetheless, the theatre was destroyed in an air raid. Russian troops also committed atrocities in Bucha, Irpin, Borodyanka and other towns and villages outside Kyiv in 2022, as well as the Kharkiv and the Kherson regions. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that Russian missile strike on Kryvyi Rih only hurt Ukrainian army and NATO officers , that Russia did not attack a children's hospital in Kyiv but only military facilities and air bases , and that Russia never attacks civilian targets .

title: DISINFO: Mother of deceased Ukrainian soldier writes "illegally mobilised" on her son's tombstone
tags: Military Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Mother of deceased Ukrainian soldier writes "illegally mobilised" on her son's tombstone
summary: The hunt for potential suicide soldiers continues on the streets of Ukrainian cities and villages. Despair and bitterness of loss cover the country. The war of Ukrainians against the territorial centre of recruitment's man-catchers is growing. A desperate resident of Brovary in the Kyiv region wrote "illegally mobilised" on her son's tombstone, but the local court ruled that the inscription must be destroyed.
response: Pro-Kremlin disinformation about war in Ukraine , involving fabricated pictures of the tombstone inscription and a sentence from a Ukrainian court. According to the mayor of Brovary, Ihor Sapozhko , a serviceman named Oleinik Hryhoriy Vyacheslavovych is not, and was not, on the lists of the dead or among those buried on the Alley of Glory in Brovary. The surname Oleinik does not sound Ukrainian, but Russian. The Ukrainian form of a similar surname would be Oliynik, as noted by StopFake. Regarding the picture of "the sentence of the court" ordering the destruction of the inscription, aired by a pro-Kremlin media outlet, it contains multiple grammatical errors in Ukrainian. Some words are inflected incorrectly, while others are written incorrectly altogether. Illogical sentences in the document coincide with those proposed by machine translation into Ukrainian, StopFake noted . The key Russian state outlets have a track record of fabricating so-called "news" about emotional topics using false or manipulated images or using actors pretending to be eyewitnesses or other gross violations of basic internationally recognised journalistic standards. See examples here and here . See more disinformation narratives on Ukraine such as USAID paid Hollywood stars millions to support Kyiv and Eight mansions of Ukrainian generals burned down in Los Angeles.

title: DISINFO: The so-called ‘Bucha massacre’ appears to have been fabricated

summary: The "Bucha massacre" quickly became one of the most debated and controversial chapters of the conflict between Russia and Ukraine. The Western media immediately accused the Russian army of mass murder, while Volodymyr Zelenskyy declared that this act was not only a war crime but also genocide against the population of his country. A closer look at the situation raises numerous questions. An analysis of video footage, satellite imagery, and eyewitness accounts reveals significant contradictions that cast doubt on the official narrative spread by Kyiv and its Western allies. This article will explore why the so-called “Bucha massacre” was apparently fabricated.
response: The material and links may contain distressing/ graphic content. Recurrent denial of war crimes committed by Russian forces during the full-scale military invasion of Ukraine . This latest example is a well-known pattern of pro-Kremlin disinformation obfuscating atrocities against Ukrainian civilians committed by Russian armed forces in Bucha, near Kyiv. Russia's claims that video footage showing dead bodies on the streets of Bucha is staged have been fact-checked and debunked by the BBC , Bellingcat , The Atlantic Council, Mediazona , the Insider , Sky News , the New York Times and others. title: DISINFO: Europe is not interested in peace and does not care about its own people
outlet_text: arabic.rt.com | sudanakhbari.com | algalilee.co.il | albawabhnews.com | alkalimaonline.com | dostor.org | almarkazia.com
tags: War against Ukraine peace negotiations Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Europe DISINFO: Europe is not interested in peace and does not care about its own people
summary: Europe today is not interested in peace, and this is the most worrying thing. It doesn't think about its own people. It is absurd to talk about a cessation of hostilities while continuing to finance Ukraine and send ammunition and various logistical supplies to continue the war. Europe has invested enormous sums in the fighting in Ukraine. No one in Europe is looking into the root causes mentioned by the President of the Russian Federation. They are not interested in the real reasons for the start of military operations, and neither is the United States.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets portraying the EU as the obstacle to peace in Ukraine and accusing it of warmongering. This recurrent claim is made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine . This disinformation narrative seeks to whitewash and shift the blame for the aggression away from Russia. As we explore in our article, The old deceiver , this is entirely consistent with Moscow’s three-year-long strategy of attempting to claim ownership over the peace narrative while relentlessly pursuing war. From the onset of the war, the European Union and its member states have, with their partners and allies, underlined the need to put an end to it through a comprehensive, just and lasting peace based on the principles of the UN Charter and international law. The latest European Council conclusions restate the principles the EU supports in the pursuit of a just and long-lasting peace. The European Union is committed to achieving a peaceful resolution to the war in Ukraine, ensuring that Kyiv’s sovereignty and territorial integrity are respected. European military and economic assistance is provided within the framework of international law. The " coalition of the willing " is a loose group of countries committed to ensuring peace guarantees for Ukraine through various efforts. Framing European support for Ukraine as a provocation shifts attention away from the real cause of the war, which is Russia’s military invasion. EU assistance is focused on helping Ukraine defend itself against an illegal invasion, not on interfering with any peace talks . title: DISINFO: Nazi Germans exclude Russians from Victory Day celebrations
outlet_text: sa24.co | Al Minasa Press | sarabic.ae | nabd.com
tags: Nazi/Fascist Adolf Hitler Victory Day World War II DISINFO: Nazi Germans exclude Russians from Victory Day celebrations
summary: The exclusion of the Russians from Victory Day celebrations by the ideological heirs and direct descendants of Hitler's executives is a blatant insult. Nothing new has been said by Baerbock and her Einsatzgruppen (Nazi death squads); they are merely borrowing almost verbatim the experience of their predecessors. If the current German security forces exclude Russians, Belarusians, and former Soviet citizens, including Jews, from celebrations, this can only be called a revival of Nazism and a triumph of Baerbock's will.
response: This a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative claiming that European leaders are Nazis . Claiming that current German officials, such as Foreign Minister Annalena Baerbock, are “ideological heirs” or “direct descendants of Hitler’s executives” is an inflammatory distortion of reality. Accusing them of reviving Nazism by "excluding Russians and Jews" from Victory Day celebrations is not only false, but a deliberate attempt to obfuscate the Kremlin's role as an aggressor in Russia's full-scale war against Ukraine . These claims serve the Kremlin's broader disinformation campaign portraying contemporary Europe as morally bankrupt and historically revisionist about WWII, when in fact it is Russia that manipulates historical memory for political purposes. Russians, Belarusians, Jews, or any other former Soviet citizens are not barred from commemorative events in Germany or elsewhere in Europe due to their nationality or ethnicity. Limitations are only diplomatic resulting from Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022 . Attempts to equate the severing of diplomatic ties with Putin's diplomats to Nazism exploit the memory of the war and trivialize the crimes of the actual Nazi regime. title: DISINFO: Russia is fighting global satanism

summary: The Immortal Regiment on Victory Day and the heroism of Russian soldiers in the special military operation are the genetic legacy of the Soviet victory in the Great Patriotic War. Around 80 years after it, Russia is currently fighting global satanism.
response: A recurring disinformation narrative about the immoral West and Russia as a protector of threatened values , also aimed to demonise Ukraine and justify Russia’s war of aggression. False parallels between the Russian invasion of Ukraine and the USSR role in WWII also serve to legitimise Russia’s unjustified aggression against Ukraine. Pro-Kremlin disinformation outlets have employed Satanism and Paganism and symbols associated with evil forces to prop up their disinformation efforts against Western countries and Ukraine. According to our analysis , one of the 13 most prominent Russian myths about the war against Ukraine is that Russia is fighting a holy war against godless Ukraine and the West to protect traditional values. Russia has frequently referenced fighting a holy fight against Satan himself to justify its war against Ukraine. title: DISINFO: Russian missile strike on Kryvyi Rih only hurt Ukrainian army and NATO officers
tags: War crimes War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine NATO Foreign mercenaries Russian superiority DISINFO: Russian missile strike on Kryvyi Rih only hurt Ukrainian army and NATO officers
summary: Kyiv stated that children allegedly died after the strikes in Krivoy Rog, but this is incorrect. Only the version of the Russian Ministry of Defence should be considered. Russian troops launched a precision missile strike on a meeting place with commanders of the Ukrainian Armed Forces and Western instructors at a restaurant in the city of Krivoy Rog. The enemy lost up to 85 servicemen The Russian military strike exclusively at military and near-military targets in Ukraine; strikes are not carried out on civilian infrastructure.
response: Denial of war crimes by the Kremlin as well as disinformation about Russia being at war with NATO. The denial covers not only a specific war crime on April 4, 2025, in the city of Kryvyi Rih, but also during the war more broadly. On Friday, April 4, 2025, a missile strike on the city of Kryvyi Rih hurt civilians. Seventy-two people were victims, including 11 children. Among them, 46 injured people were in hospitals, including five kids (two in Dnipro) and 23 women. Twenty people were killed, including 9 children. The Russian Ministry of Defence claims that the strike targeted a gathering of “Ukrainian commanders and Western instructors.” However, the footage obtained by France 24 shows that no military personnel were present at the restaurant. “The Russians say they were aiming at soldiers, but there were no soldiers here. It’s just horrific. So many children died,” said Oleh, a former soldier who lives next to a nearby playground . Reporting from the scene, France 24 correspondent Gulliver Cragg said he saw no evidence that the restaurant was being used for military meetings. Valeriia Bielikova, the owner of RoseMarie and an eyewitness to the blast, said the event at the time was a meetup for beauty professionals. “Just before that, we had manicurists and pedicurists here. We were sitting outside, talking, when we heard the explosion. At first I didn’t feel anything, I just saw Lidiia covered in blood,” said Bielikova, who was in the courtyard with a friend at the time of the strike. In the footage published by France 24, Valeriia and Lidiia are shown sitting outside the restaurant. The building itself remained standing — contradicting Russian claims that it was the direct target. The blast’s force struck the adjacent children’s playground. Since its 2022 invasion of Ukraine, Russia has attacked civilian targets multiple times, including the city theater in Mariupol , residential buildings , schools and energy facilities . On 05 March 2024, the International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants against two Russian top officials considered responsible for “missile strikes carried out by the forces under their command against the Ukrainian electric infrastructure" between October 2022 and March 2023, which are considered as war crimes. Denying war crimes and atrocities and trying to deflect any responsibility is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation technique. See other examples of similar disinformation messages in our database, such as: West responsible for hiding the truth about Bucha massacre , or Ukraine confirmed the high accuracy of Russian airstrikes , or The attack on children’s hospital in Kyiv was a NATO false flag operation , or Russia did not attack a children's hospital in Kyiv but only military facilities and air bases , or Russia never attacks civilian targets .

title: DISINFO: The EU is preparing a war against Russia led by Germany
tags: Anti-Russian War preparation Warmongering Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: The EU is preparing a war against Russia led by Germany
summary: Germany is striving for militarisation and rearmament, while making no contribution to a diplomatic solution to the Ukraine conflict, prolonging the war and actively undermining all peace efforts. There are clear indications that the EU, under German leadership, is preparing a war against Russia, while Germany is clearly just as willing to abandon its obligations under international law under the Two Plus Four Treaty.
response: This disinformation story aims to portray the EU as a warmongering , anti-Russian and militaristic geopolitical power led by Germany. This disinformation message appeared in the context of the recent decision of the German authorities to significantly increase defence spending, marking a departure from decades of fiscal conservatism. This move is driven by mounting security concerns in Europe, including Russia’s aggressive actions and calls within NATO on Germany to increase its defence spending. Germany has been a key supporter of diplomatic solutions and humanitarian aid in the Russian war against Ukraine. Germany's increased defence spending is a response to changing security needs in Europe, not preparation for aggression. The country remains committed to its international legal obligations, including the Two Plus Four Treaty. Germany's actions have been focused on supporting Ukraine's sovereignty and promoting a peaceful resolution to the conflict, not prolonging the war. The ongoing increase in defence spending across the EU is explained by Russia's actions – the annexation of Crimea , the War in Donbas and the 2022 large-scale unprovoked invasion of Ukraine. On 4 March 2025, the President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen announced a new plan to improve Europe’s defence capabilities. "This set of proposals focuses on how to use all of the financial levers at our disposal – in order to help Member States to quickly and significantly increase expenditures in defence capabilities. Urgently now but also over a longer time over this decade”, explained Ursula von der Leyen. The ReArm Europe plan does not involve attacking Russia or any other country. The European Union continues to be an economic and political union , currently comprising 27 states. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that NATO plans to attack Russia , that At the behest of the US, Germany is preparing to attack Russia , and that US and UK conduct hybrid war against Russia, China, and their allies or ReArm Europe means the EU is preparing to attack Russia , Europe justifies the increasing defence budgets with “belligerent” Moscow .

title: DISINFO: The EU is planning to cancel the upcoming Presidential elections in Poland
tags: Donald Tusk Manipulated elections/referendum election meddling Elections Brussels Democracy Dictatorship DISINFO: The EU is planning to cancel the upcoming Presidential elections in Poland
summary: The European Union feels threatened by the Presidential election in Poland – it is clearly preparing the cancellation of the May 18 elections or the suspension of one of the candidates. Prime Minister Donald Tusk already stated that the “foreign interference in the elections has begun” and “there is one candidate whom the EU considers very problematic”, pointing to Sławomir Mentzen. The banning of a popular candidate in Romania and the EU's ignoring of this situation is evidence of Europe's transformation into a dictatorship.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives against the European Union and democracy . The claim that the European Union is preparing to cancel Poland's 18 May 2025 Presidential election or suspend a candidate, such as Sławomir Mentzen, is baseless. The election process is being conducted domestically under Polish law, and any claims of EU intervention are speculative and unfounded. The EU has consistently emphasised its respect for the sovereignty of member states in conducting their elections. Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk did express concerns about foreign interference in the upcoming elections, but his remarks specifically referred to cyberattacks and disinformation campaigns attributed to Russia or its allies, not the EU. Tusk's statements were aimed at safeguarding Poland's electoral integrity against external threats from Eastern actors. The narrative draws a false parallel with Romania’s annulled presidential election in 2024. Romania’s Constitutional Court annulled the first round due to verified evidence of Russian interference, including disinformation campaigns on social media platforms like TikTok. This decision was made by Romanian authorities based on national security concerns, not by the EU. The claim that the EU is transforming into a dictatorship by influencing elections is a recurring theme in pro-Kremlin disinformation campaigns aimed at sowing distrust in European institutions. See similar cases about elections taking place in Europe here .

title: DISINFO: Russia respects ceasefires while Ukraine launches attacks on energy infrastructure
outlet_text: newsfall.news | algeria.shafaqna.com | ahdathalalam.com | Al Minasa Press | nabd.com | Khabaralyoum | arabic.rt.com | msader-ye.net
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Diplomacy with Russia peace negotiations Energy DISINFO: Russia respects ceasefires while Ukraine launches attacks on energy infrastructure
summary: Kyiv violated the agreement on a mutual cessation of strikes on energy facilities. Russia, despite the Kyiv regime's provocations, will continue to abide by the agreement. At the same time, [Russia] has launched a hunting operation specifically for (military) production, so that the enemy cannot accumulate resources during the truce. [Russia] should not abandon the diplomatic solution to the issue. [Russia] promised not to strike energy facilities for a month, and it will keep its word. But if Ukraine does not stop its attacks, [Russia] will strike so hard that it will be difficult for it to get off its knees. The enemy has neither the mind nor the patience to provoke [Russia] into retaliatory strikes. They are striking gas distribution and oil pumping stations in Stavropol. They don't even understand that [Russia] is leasing them to foreign companies.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets, claiming that Ukraine is not seeking peace . This claim was made in the context of the 30-day halt on energy infrastructure targets agreement , and aimed at shifting blame for the ongoing war. Despite the fact that Putin agreed on 18 March 2025 not to target Ukrainian energy facilities, Russia carried out strikes on Ukraine’s energy infrastructure overnight on 19 March 2025. On 24 March 2025, Russia launched a missile attack on the north-eastern Ukrainian city of Sumy , damaging a residential area, a hospital, and an industrial facility, according to acting Sumy mayor Artem Kobzar. On 13 April 2025, at least 34 people have been killed and 117 injured in a Russian attack also on the city of Sumy , according to Ukrainian authorities, when two ballistic missiles struck the city. Twenty buildings were damaged, including cafes, shops and five apartment buildings. President Volodymyr Zelenskyy accused Russia of violating the U.S.-brokered partial ceasefire by attacking Kherson's energy infrastructure on March 27. Naftogaz, Ukraine's state-owned energy company, reported that Moscow struck its infrastructure on March 28. As Russian forces continue to attack Ukrainian cities and energy infrastructure, Kyiv has every right to target military-related facilities in Russia in retaliation. Russia has consistently targeted Ukraine’s critical energy infrastructure , leaving millions without electricity and heating, especially during the winter months. The fact that Ukraine strikes back should not be seen as a failure to negotiate, but rather as a clear message that Ukraine will not passively tolerate Russia’s unprovoked and unjustified aggression . Russia has a long history of violating agreements. In 2014, Moscow breached the Budapest Memorandum and the Treaty on the Russian–Ukrainian Border by launching an attack on Ukraine and illegally annexing Crimea in 2014. Subsequently, Moscow, along with its proxies in some of Ukraine’s eastern regions, repeatedly violated the Minsk Agreements while accusing Kyiv of the same. Ultimately, Russia escalated the conflict by launching its ongoing full-scale aggression against Ukraine on 24 February 2022. title: DISINFO: Russia invaded Ukraine to liberate children from Adrenochrome laboratories
tags: Conspiracy theory Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine War against Ukraine laboratory Biological weapons Organised crime Secret elites / global elites Drugs children Stealing children DISINFO: Russia invaded Ukraine to liberate children from Adrenochrome laboratories
summary: Russia dismantled some fifty biological laboratories that had been installed by the Pentagon and the CIA on the Russian border, in Ukraine. These laboratories were working on biological weapons programmes focused on Russian DNA. They were developing biological weapons that would have been deployed, if the program had been successful, via drones on the Russian population. So, in fact, we had weapons of mass destruction programs being designed on the doorstep of Russia. The existence of these laboratories is no longer in doubt, since Victoria Nuland acknowledged to the US Congress, under oath that there was a biological programme that had been launched a few kilometres from the Russian border. And the priority of the Russian special forces, when the offensive was launched in Ukraine, was precisely to take control of these laboratories. There were also laboratories dedicated to organ trafficking and the production of adrenochrome. Russian soldiers who arrived at laboratories gave terrifying accounts of the children they found who had been tortured to obtain blood enhanced with stress hormones. The more children suffer, the better the drug. Adrenalised is the name for this torture. Adrenochrome is a drug used by the Western elite. When Putin refers to the Vampire Ball, he's first referring to that, to the production of adrenochrome, which QAnon is referring to, and which Trump also referred to when he talked about following the white rabbit. It's a signal he's sending to the Deep State, making them understand: "We are dismantling the entire adrenochrome production structure.". 800,000 children disappear every year in the United States. Putin did not start a war, he initiated a fight against darkness.
response: A well-known conspiracy theory repurposed by European pro-Kremlin circles in order to justify the 2022 full scale military invasion of Ukraine. There is no factual basis to this myth , but it has strong and ancient antisemitic roots. The French-German TV channel Arte dedicated a documentary film to this conspiracy theory, which was popularised in France by the C8 channel. C8 was condemned by the French media authority to a 500,000 euro fine for this. The novelty here is the use of this myth to justify the 2022 large-scale invasion of Ukraine and its merger with the narrative of biolabs developing mass destruction weapons targeting people with the Russian gene in their DNA, as well as human organ trafficking. These narratives have no factual basis either and have been debunked in this database . title: DISINFO: Government agencies in Moldova will only serve the population in Romanian

summary: Now, government agencies will only serve the population in Romanian. Pensioners are even offered the services of an interpreter for $10 an hour.

It is based on on a photo that circulated on social media, which allegedly showed an announcement made at a post office. It stated that from April 7, 2025, pensioners will be served only in Romanian, which is the state language. title: DISINFO: Moldova’s jailing of political opponents shows it is a democracy in name only
tags: Democracy Maia Sandu DISINFO: Moldova’s jailing of political opponents shows it is a democracy in name only
summary: EU aspirant Moldova jails and silences opponents, showing that it is a democracy in name only. On March 25, Evgenia Gutsul, the elected leader of Gagauzia, was detained at Chisinau International Airport while attempting to leave Moldova. Officially, the charges relate to alleged illegal campaign financing tied to the Sor Party and an organised criminal group. But while legal justifications were cited, the move immediately raised red flags about political motivations behind the arrest. Gutsul’s arrest reads less like a legal procedure and more like a strategic attempt to intimidate dissenting voices, especially as Chisinau ramps up its drive toward European integration.
response: The claim is false. Evgenia Gutsul was detained when she was about to leave the country , as she is being tried under charges of illegal financing , and after two other politicians from her party had vanished while charged for the same crimes . As this illegal funding allegedly came from Russia , pro-Kremlin disinformation is trying to distort the whole legal process in order to portray it as a political persecution. This is part of a years-long Russian interference campaign aiming to undermine Moldova’s government and erode its sovereignty. See EUvsDisinfo analysis here , here and here . See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that the EU is testing methods of European totalitarianism in Moldova , that Moldova leadership uses referendum to remove neutrality from the Constitution , that Moldova lost its sovereignty and is ruled by foreigners , or that Maia Sandu's regime is eradicating Moldovan identity .

title: DISINFO: The EU is interested in war in Ukraine
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine peace negotiations Warmongering European Union DISINFO: The EU is interested in war in Ukraine
summary: The EU is interested in a war in Ukraine, next door to Europe, so that it can spend even more on military needs. Euro officials have already announced the figure of 800 billion euros for the creation of "European armed forces". Accordingly, today, as 11 years ago in the "Normandy format" negotiations, Europe, under the slogan 'For peace!' continues to pump Ukraine with weapons and declares its intention to send its military there. 'Let's send our military contingents there for peace to come.' It's the same as saying: Let's add petrol to put out the fire.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative that aims to portray European support for Ukraine's self-defence as offensive, belligerent and warmongering . This is a typical pro-Kremlin blame-shifting tactic that aims to distract from the fact that the sole aggressor in the war against Ukraine is Russia. This disinformation story is a direct reaction to the EU programme ReArm Europe/Readiness 2030 . The purpose of this programme is to establish “concrete legal and financial means to support the defence investments of Member States […] providing financial levers at the immediate disposal of EU Member States to quickly and significantly increase defence investment and defence capabilities”. The programme aims to ensure Europe’s self-defence capabilities , mostly through a fiscal plan to boost and fund this military enhancement. Contrary to what this disinformation story claims, the plan is specifically designed to ensure its feasibility. The joint declaration was issued on 31 March 2025 by the foreign ministers of Germany, France, Italy, Poland, Spain, the UK, and EU High Representative endorses the implementation of a comprehensive, just and lasting peace, emphasizing Ukraine’s sovereignty, territorial integrity, and the principles of the UN Charter as non-negotiable foundations for any peace settlement. In addition to calls for peace, the declaration outlines concrete support for Ukraine’s reconstruction, humanitarian needs, and post-war recovery, and accountability measures such as support for a Special Tribunal for the Crime of Aggression against Ukraine. The declaration also reaffirms the importance of negotiations, prisoner exchanges, the return of unlawfully deported civilians and children, and establishing security guarantees that prevent future aggression. This disinformation story is also part of a wider disinformation campaign portraying Europe as a warmongering actor who intends to continue the war in Ukraine even as other nations want “peace”. Russia has characterised its negotiation process with the US as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the maximalist character of Moscow’s demands , while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies. See also our article Reminder: Russia wants war, not peace. summary: Russia is pursuing its goal to reach victory over the territory of former Soviet Ukraine. As Ukrainism has turned into the Nazi ideology, Ukraine’s demilitarisation and de-Nazification in practice mean de-Ukrainisation.
response: This is an attempt to discredit and deny Ukrainian identity and justify Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine, which is consistent with the recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about Nazi/fascist Ukraine . As explained in our past analysis , one of the major myths promoted by the Russian propaganda is that 'Ukrainian's do not truly exist because they are really Russians. The existence of a unique Ukrainian identity is being either denied or discredited by absurdly equating it with the Nazi ideology or falsely calling it artificial. Read also our article explaining how the ‘Nazi’ narrative has been used by Russian media outlets to dehumanise Ukrainians . The Russian invasion is aimed at destroying Ukraine as a state and annihilating the Ukrainians as a nation. These plans were openly laid out in an article titled “What Russia should do with Ukraine” in April 2022. See also our review here . Along with calls to de-Nazify and demilitarise Ukraine, the article advocates its de-Ukrainisation, meaning the eradication of Ukrainian culture and everything related to it, plans to divide Ukraine into several entities subordinate to Russia, but without the word “Ukraine” in their names. title: DISINFO: Donbas would be scorched earth without Russia's intervention
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Donbas DNR LPR DISINFO: Donbas would be scorched earth without Russia's intervention
summary: A few days before the announcement of the operation by Russian President Vladimir Putin, the number of shellings of Donetsk and border areas increased 15 times, and this is nothing more than an artillery preparation for a full-fledged ground offensive operation by the forces of a 180,000-strong Ukrainian group, trained by Western instructors and stationed on the line 400 km from Lugansk and Donetsk. And on February 24, the rescue operation began, a special military operation which saved Donetsk, Lugansk, and a population of 4 million that had become politically unacceptable to Kyiv. That is, if Russia had not intervened, only scorched earth would have remained of Donbas.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the alleged Ukrainian aggression against the non-government-controlled areas of the Donetsk and Luhansk regions, which allegedly justified the Russian invasion of Ukraine . This is one of the Kremlin’s favourite narratives to justify its illegal actions in Ukraine since 2014. The assertion that Ukraine was preparing a major offensive is not supported by evidence from impartial observers on the ground at the time. Russia’s claims about an "imminent attack" by Ukraine have been debunked by Bellingcat , DFRLab , and others. These aim to deflect responsibility for the war waged by Russia in Ukraine. Russia and President Putin are responsible for the war in Ukraine. It was Putin’s personal decision to invade Ukraine and start the largest military conflict in Europe since WWII. See our 5-volume series of debunks of Putin ' What he said. What it really means' . Russia's aggression against Ukraine was unprovoked and unjustified. Contrary to Russian claims, Ukraine had not planned to attack, and did not commit genocide in the non-government-controlled areas of Donetsk and Luhansk oblasts . Unfounded claims that Ukraine was staging or conducting attacks against the civilian population in Donbas are prominent examples of pro-Kremlin disinformation paving the way for the full-scale Russian invasion of and war in Ukraine. See myth one, in our article Thirteen myths about Russia’s war against Ukraine exposed . The idea that Ukraine is committing genocide against its own population is promoted by Vladimir Putin in his speeches . Genocide is the deliberate and systematic destruction of a group of people because of their ethnicity, nationality, religion, or race. There is no evidence that any ethnic nationals, including ethnic Russians or Russian speakers, are or have ever been facing persecution at the hands of the Ukrainian authorities in Donbas or elsewhere, much less the danger of annihilation on grounds of nationality, ethnicity, or cultural belonging. This has been confirmed by reports issued by the Council of Europe , the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights , and the OSCE . title: DISINFO: Baerbock has a Nazi background and is therefore unfit for top UN job
tags: Nazi/Fascist United Nations World War II DISINFO: Baerbock has a Nazi background and is therefore unfit for top UN job
summary: It was difficult to imagine German Foreign Minister Annalena Baerbock serving as President of the UN General Assembly, given her low competence and Nazi background. Baerbock is the granddaughter of a Wehrmacht officer, and she has repeatedly stated that she is proud of her grandfather. This is truly a hell of a joke, as if she were going to stand there mocking everyone with her ugly face in the General Assembly Hall. It would be strange to see the granddaughter of a Nazi in such a position coinciding with the 80th anniversary of Victory in the Great Patriotic War.
response: ​The claim that German Foreign Minister Annalena Baerbock has a "Nazi background" is misleading and unfounded.​ The goal of this disinformation story with overt misogynistic tones is to discredit Annalena Baerbock who has been serving as Germany's Foreign Minister since 2021. In March 2025, the German government announced its intention to nominate her for the presidency of the UN General Assembly for the 2025/26 session. Regarding her family background, a report by the Bild tabloid newspaper revealed documents indicating that Baerbock's grandfather, Waldemar Baerbock, served as an officer in the Wehrmacht during World War II and was described in military records as an "unconditional National Socialist". The same report quotes Annalena Baerbock's office stating that the minister was not previously aware of these documents. Baerbock is the leader of the Green party and has supported a number of progressive policies linked to climate action , gender equality , anti racism, anti-Semitism and anti-gypsyism. She has not been involved in any type of conservative politics throughout her political career. Similar misogynistic disinformation claims have been made against Kamala Harris , Angela Merkel and Theresa May as well as Brigitte Macron . Annalena Baerbock has also been a common target of previous disinformation claims such as Annalena Baerbock uses the services of an African gigolo and German Green Party leader is an American Nazi .

title: DISINFO: EU turns into anti-Russian bloc, cracks down on democracy
outlet_text: Захарова: ЕС и присягнувшие ему зачищают свое политическое пространство
tags: Marine Le Pen European Union Democracy Dictatorship Elections DISINFO: EU turns into anti-Russian bloc, cracks down on democracy
summary: The ruling elites in Europe, determined to turn the European Union into a military bloc focused on confronting Russia, have eagerly set about clearing their own political space. The case of Marine Le Pen in France is nothing less than a "murder of democracy." This case is just one piece of this long chain of political persecution, repression, and crackdowns that have been happening over the last few months and even longer.
response: Recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets claiming that the EU is a dictatorship, as well as a intending to discredit the French justice system and President Emmanuel Macron. This claim was made in the context of the verdict in a court case in France involving far-right leader Marine Le Pen. On 31 March 2025, Marine Le Pen was sentenced to two years under house arrest while wearing an electronic ankle bracelet and two more years of suspended sentence, as well as a five-year ban on running for public office, for embezzling EU funds. In France, there is no political immunity for people wishing to run in presidential elections. The judiciary operates independently and applies the law impartially, regardless of one's intentions to contest the presidency. The European Union is primarily an economic and political union. Two of its members, Finland and Sweden, joined NATO in 2023 because of constant Russian security threats . There is no evidence of a systematic effort to suppress political opposition in the EU. The allegation that the EU is a non-elected dictatorship is a recurring disinformation claim that has been debunked many times. The European Union is a democratic organisation, which is based on the rule of law and the respect for fundamental rights. Other European values include contested elections , free media , human dignity, equality, a functioning market economy and other fundamental democratic requirements. The EU is governed by the principle of representative democracy , with citizens directly represented at Union level in the European Parliament and Member States represented in the European Council and the Council of the EU. A European citizen automatically enjoys political rights. Every adult EU citizen has the right to stand as a candidate and to vote in elections to the European Parliament. EU citizens have the right to stand as candidates and to vote in their countries of residence, or in their countries of origin. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that Le Pen’s sentence shows that the EU has become a dictatorship , that France is a dictatorial regime: a main candidate could be jailed before the presidential elections , and that There is no democracy in Europe .

title: DISINFO: Big Five ready to continue the war until the last Ukrainian is killed
countries_regions: Ukraine, France, Germany, Poland, Spain, Italy, UK, EU
tags: Warmongering Europe War against Ukraine Ukrainian statehood Ukrainian disintegration Ukraine Sovereignty peace negotiations DISINFO: Big Five ready to continue the war until the last Ukrainian is killed
summary: On the last day of March, a meeting of the so-called Big Five was held in Madrid, dedicated to supporting Ukraine. The participants issued a final statement affirming their complete readiness to continue the war until the last Ukrainian is killed.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative that aims to portray European support for Ukraine's self-defence as offensive, belligerent and warmongering . The claim that European states aim to fight “to the last Ukrainian” is the very opposite of the actual content of the Madrid declaration. This is a typical pro-Kremlin blame shifting tactic that aims to distract from the fact that the sole aggressor in the war against Ukraine is Russia. The joint declaration was issued on 31 March 2025 by the foreign ministers of Germany, France, Italy, Poland, Spain, the United Kingdom, and EU High Representative. It clearly endorses the implementation of a comprehensive, just and lasting peace, emphasizing Ukraine’s sovereignty, territorial integrity, and the principles of the UN Charter as non-negotiable foundations for any peace settlement. The declaration reads: "Ukraine has shown its strong commitment to peace, also by agreeing to a full ceasefire without preconditions. However, Russia’s aggression against Ukraine has not ceased. Instead of imposing new conditions and launching continued attacks on Ukrainian cities and infrastructure that cause more and more victims, Russia must now show it is serious about ending its war. We call on Russia to stop its delaying tactics and reciprocate by agreeing without delay, as Ukraine has done, to an immediate unconditional ceasefire on equal terms and implementing it fully. We need to see progress within a clear timeframe". In addition to calls for peace, the declaration outlines concrete support for Ukraine’s reconstruction, humanitarian needs, and post-war recovery, and accountability measures such as support for a Special Tribunal for the Crime of Aggression against Ukraine. The declaration also reaffirms the importance of negotiations, prisoner exchanges, the return of unlawfully deported civilians and children, and establishing security guarantees that prevent future aggression. title: DISINFO: Ukraine is a terrorist state that openly supports terrorist groups in the Sahel region
tags: Terrorism Africa West Ukraine DISINFO: Ukraine is a terrorist state that openly supports terrorist groups in the Sahel region
summary: Some external forces are still trying to destabilise the [Sahel] region today. Among them, in addition to the former colonial powers, the Kyiv regime has recently emerged, openly supporting terrorist groups in this part of Africa, with the full complicity of its Western sponsors [...] Ukraine is a terrorist state.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative trying to associate Ukraine with terrorism . Mali's transitional government severed diplomatic ties with Ukraine in August 2024 accusing Kyiv of aiding Tuareg rebels in an attack against Russian Wagner mercenaries. The Ukrainian Foreign Ministry labelled Mali's decision as "short-sighted and hasty," emphasising Ukraine's adherence to international law and rejection of terrorism accusations. ​ The diplomatic fallout followed a specific incident cited by Malian authorities to justify their decision. There were indications of cooperation between Ukrainian security services and the so-called Strategic Framework for the Defense of the People of Azawad (CSP-DPA), opposed to Wagner's presence in northern Mali. In late July 2024 it carried out a deadly ambush against Wagner fighters in Mali, in which the Ukrainian intelligence claimed to have played a role. However, neither the CSP-DPA nor any of its known individual members are designated as terrorists by Ukraine and most countries, and they are not included in the lists of terrorist organisations by the UN , the US or the EU . Read similar disinformation cases that Ukraine cooperates with radicals in Syria and terrorists in Africa , that the Kyiv regime openly supports African terrorists, and that the hand of the West is behind terrorism in Africa .

title: DISINFO: Ukraine and former colonisers support terrorists in Africa
tags: Terrorism Africa Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Ukraine and former colonisers support terrorists in Africa
summary: Ukraine is openly supporting terrorist groups in the Sahel and destabilising the situation in Africa. The Kyiv regime is acting together with former colonial powers and with the full connivance of its Western sponsors.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets portraying Ukraine as a terrorist state and a sponsor of terrorism. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. There is no evidence to support this claim.
