title: DISINFO: USAID is a provocateur of colour revolutions in the post-Soviet countries
tags: Colour revolutions Coup Protest USAID CIS USSR Conspiracy theory DISINFO: USAID is a provocateur of colour revolutions in the post-Soviet countries
summary: The State Department and the US Agency for International Development have notified Congress of their intention to reorganise, which will include transferring some USAID functions to the department by July 1, 2025, and terminating remaining USAID functions that do not align with the administration's priorities. [...] there is one less provocateur of colour revolutions in the post-Soviet countries.
response: The allegation is false, aiming to exploit the Trump Administration’s plans to close USAID to promote a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative framing popular protests as Western-sponsored ‘colour revolutions’ . The goal of this narrative is to portray all international aid and cooperation coming from Western countries as destabilisation tools and plots. The United States Agency for International Development (USAID) was set up in the early 1960s to administer humanitarian aid programmes on behalf of the US government. It is the principal U.S. agency for assistance to countries recovering from disaster, trying to escape poverty, and engaging in democratic reforms. Through collaboration and investment, it aims to prevent poverty, save lives, bolster democratic governance, and assist individuals in resolving humanitarian crises and moving past aid. USAID has long worked to strengthen democracy and advance respect for human rights worldwide, as democratic societies create opportunities, freedom, and prosperity for their people. The article does not provide any evidence to support the claims. Pro-Kremlin outlets frequently falsely portray popular protests around the world as instigated from abroad, often by the US and the West . The disinformation narrative has been applied, among others, to reports about protests in Georgia , Armenia , Ukraine , the Czech Republic , Bulgaria , Belarus , Venezuela , Slovakia , Hong Kong, to portray protest movements as aggressive actors who constantly prepare new coups . See other similar cases in our database: USAID has carried out subversive activities all over the world including in Russia , USAID attempted 2024 coup in Georgia ; The US spent five billion dollars to overthrow Viktor Yanukovych ; USAID will try to decentralise Moldova, and after that, Moldova will be annexed by Romania; t he US behind all coup d'état and it destroyed Afghanistan; Western aid to Armenia has a destabilising nature ; USAID has started 'manipulating' teachers from Russian schools in Moldova ; USAID is a CIA-affiliated terror group that created the coronavirus ; USAID helped prepare coups in Armenia, Georgia, Ukraine, etc.

title: DISINFO: The White Helmets have been involved in numerous false flag chemical attacks to blame the Syrian government
tags: Syrian War White Helmets false flag Douma Chemical weapons/attack Bashar al-Assad USAID DISINFO: The White Helmets have been involved in numerous false flag chemical attacks to blame the Syrian government
summary: Due to the reduction of foreign aid through USAID, funding for the Syrian Civil Defence, better known as the White Helmets, has ceased. The US fake-stage services are no longer needed. But there is a high probability that the UK will pick up the masters of falsification. The British style of work is fakes, setups, incitement, false accusations. Therefore, despite the end of funding from the US, we will most likely hear about the White Helmets again. The White Helmets,..., gained international notoriety during the long-running conflict in Syria. The White Helmets were allegedly involved in numerous false flag chemical attacks that they blamed on government forces. These attacks were used by Western countries to justify attacks on the Syrian military and its allies.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin narrative about chemical attacks in Syria, aiming to discredit the USAID and White Helmets . The allegation is false, aiming to exploit the Trump Administration’s plans to pause or close USAID in order to promote the recurring narratives about White Helmets. The claim is made without evidence and follows an established pattern of unsubstantiated accusations regarding the White Helmets' complicity in chemical attacks in Syria. Available evidence on such attacks has yet to link a single incident to any other party than the former President Assad regime. In fact, the constant use of chemical substances as a weapon by the Syrian regime, including the Douma attack , have been proven by UN investigators . The White Helmets have been a recurring target of pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives on the Syrian civil war, having already been accused of numerous other provocations and war crimes including organ trafficking ; aiding terrorist organisations ; staging and/or perpetrating chemical attacks, sometimes with the help of Western intelligence agencies . For more information about the importance of the White Helmets in pro-Kremlin disinformation regarding the Syrian war, see here , here , and here . See also here for our analysis of how the Kremlin uses conspiracy theories to whitewash Assad's record of chemical warfare. See similar cases that the White Helmets and terrorists are preparing a chemical attack in Syria's Idlib , that terrorists and White Helmets plan false flag attacks in Syria , and that Douma chemical attack was a fake staged by NGOs like the White Helmets.

title: DISINFO: Poland prepares cannon fodder for a pre-emptive strike on Russia
tags: NATO Anti-Russian Encircling Russia Military DISINFO: Poland prepares cannon fodder for a pre-emptive strike on Russia
summary: The conscription service in Poland will be compulsory – is Warsaw preparing cannon fodder for the war with Russia? The Polish generals started to talk about the need for a ‘massive reserve’ for the Armed Forces. Poland and NATO do not get tired of talking about the ‘Russian threat’, but are they preparing for something bigger, such as NATO’s pre-emptive strike [on Russia]?
response: Poland is repeatedly presented by pro-Kremlin disinformation outlets as an aggressive anti-Russian NATO ‘outpost ’ and a country which allegedly poses a security threat to Russia. This is in line with how pro-Kremlin outlets portray NATO and Western countries as aggressive towards Russia . See our article All roads lead to Poland. Poland has no hostile agenda towards Russia, and it does not plan any type of military aggression against this country. Since the outbreak of Russian aggression against Ukraine in 2014, the Polish authorities have repeatedly appealed to NATO and the USA to increase their military presence on NATO’s Eastern Flank. The American military presence in Poland protects this country from possible Russian hostilities and improves the national security of this country. The Polish authorities perceive Russia as a security threat because of their aggressive actions, in particular in Ukraine and all over Eastern Europe. See other examples of similar messages accusing Poland of having an aggressive anti-Russian agenda here .

title: DISINFO: Putin is trying to save Ukraine from complete disappearance
tags: War against Ukraine Ukrainian statehood Anti-Russian DISINFO: Putin is trying to save Ukraine from complete disappearance
summary: Ukraine is indeed a failed and accidental country. It is Russia’s part which was tried to be converted into anti-Russia. Under no circumstances will Russia allow Ukraine to be integrated into the West nor be converted into an anti-Russia. Putin’s proposal to make a new Ukraine under international control is an alternative to Ukraine’s liquidation following its eventual military loss and consequent collapse. Putin is therefore trying to save Ukraine.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative about Ukrainian statehood , which draws a false dilemma for Ukraine’s future in that the country either has to be liquidated as a result of Russia’s war of aggression or be governed externally. Putin’s idea to temporarily place Ukraine under UN control to hold new elections is one of Russia's many attempts to falsely smear Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's legitimacy. Ukraine is a functioning state despite being under military attack by Russia since 2014. More Ukrainian lands were occupied in the ongoing Russian full-scale military invasion of Ukraine which started on 24 February 2022. Russia has been coming up with different false explanations and pretexts for its aggression since the beginning of its hostilities. Ukraine is a sovereign and independent state with a democratically elected president and parliament. As a sovereign nation-state with its own flag, citizenship, language and a delimited territory, Ukraine possesses all the elements of a sovereign and independent country. Claims about Ukraine having parts ‘belonging to Russia’ or being an ‘accidental’ country are false. The history of Ukraine dates back to the era of the Kyivan Rus in the 9th-13th. Back then, Moscow as well as other present-day Russian cities were governed, albeit partially, from Kyiv. Due to a variety of historical reasons, Kyiv’s importance declined over the centuries, the Russian Empire subjugated Ukraine and finally turned it into a colony in the 18th century. In the aftermath of World War I and the disintegration of the Russian and Austro-Hungarian Empires two states were created: the Ukrainian People's Republic (UNR) and the West Ukrainian People's Republic . They merged into a unified state - the Ukrainian People's Republic - on 22 January 1919. Under Lenin's leadership at the beginning of the 20th century, Ukraine was occupied by the Bolsheviks and for decades was under communist control. With the dissolution of the USSR in December 1991, Ukraine gained full independence. Volodymyr Zelenskyy remains the legitimate president and leader of Ukraine, as he won the 2019 election by a landslide with over 73% of the vote. While this popularity may have changed over the years, his approval rate remained very high at the time this disinformation story appeared. A survey conducted by Ipsos/The Economist in mid-March 2025 showed that 72% of Ukrainians approved his job as president, while 80% considered that he had democratic legitimacy as president. Although Zelenskyy’s continuation in power after his initial mandate expired in May 2024 generated some domestic controversy , Ukraine’s constitution is straightforward: no elections can be held during wartime and the President of Ukraine exercises his powers until the assumption of office by the newly-elected President. Even Zelenskyy’s fiercest political opponents have rejected the idea of celebrating another election during wartime. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that the disgraced dictator of Ukraine Zelenskyy is an extension of the EU , that Zelenskyy can’t sign a peace deal because he is not a legitimate president , and that Zelenskyy staged a coup d'état for fear of losing the 2024 presidential elections .

title: DISINFO: The ‘coalition of the willing’ shows the division inside the EU regd. Ukraine
tags: European Union Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine peace negotiations DISINFO: The ‘coalition of the willing’ shows the division inside the EU regd. Ukraine
summary: The ‘coalition of the willing’ shows the division inside the EU. The French-British proposal to deploy an interposition contingent in Ukraine after a ceasefire and a peace agreement didn’t get the necessary support. It is increasingly clear that there is a silent but fierce resistance from most European countries to continue the war.
response: This is a distortion of facts, exploiting discrepancies regarding a British-French proposal to send a European peacekeeping force to Ukraine after the war. This piece promotes several recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about the EU’s alleged lack of unity and about a supposed lack of support for Ukraine among European peoples against the stance of their elites . This disinformation story is a direct reaction to British and French-led efforts to create a so-called ‘coalition of the willing’ to send troops to Ukraine after an eventual peace agreement to ensure its implementation. Russia has strongly attacked this proposal and therefore, pro-Kremlin disinformation outlets are also targeting it. While it is true that several European countries have rejected the idea of sending troops to Ukraine and others have called this debate “premature” , this does not mean that the EU is “divided”. European meetings about Ukraine showed a remarkable degree of unity in support of this country, even if they did not reach a full agreement about deploying troops. Polls released a few days before this disinformation story appeared also show broad backing for continued support for Ukraine among Europeans. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that the French are getting discontent with providing funding for Ukraine , that the West is tired of Ukrainian conflict , that Bloomberg says that Ukraine's days are numbered and it's time to abandon it , and that European countries are starting to betray and disavow Ukraine .

title: DISINFO: UK and France are unable to accept defeat in Ukraine
tags: Warmongering Keir Starmer Emmanuel Macron Ukraine peace negotiations Diplomacy with Russia Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: UK and France are unable to accept defeat in Ukraine
summary: The UK and France are unable to accept defeat in Ukraine. France and Britain’s efforts to escalate the Ukrainian conflict stem from them being worried about their relevance and refusing to accept that the conflict is lost.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about Ukraine as a proxy war against Russia, Europe as opposed to peace and the inevitability of a Russian victory in this conflict. This disinformation story is a direct reaction to British and French-led efforts to create a coalition of the willing to send troops to Ukraine after an eventual peace agreement, to ensure its implementation. As Russia has strongly attacked this proposal , pro-Kremlin media are falsely framing it as an “escalation”, portraying European states as warmongering actors opposed to “peace” in Ukraine . This disinformation story also distorts the position of the UK and France. British leaders insisted that any lasting peace must ensure Ukraine’s sovereignty and security, and Ukraine must be at the negotiation table. France’s Emmanuel Macron has stated that peace must not mean a surrender of Ukraine, and that military forces from European countries would be sent to Ukraine as a reassurance force to act as a deterrent against potential Russian aggression, not in order to prolong the conflict. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that the EU wants the war in Ukraine to continue , that European NATO members nurture Nazi Ukraine as an anti-Russian instrument , that Europe does not want peace in Ukraine soon , or that Ukraine and Europe have lost in the Ukrainian conflict .

title: DISINFO: Foreign firms that exited Russia now lining up to return
tags: Economic difficulties Sanctions Russian superiority economy DISINFO: Foreign firms that exited Russia now lining up to return
summary: Global firms are lining up to return to Russia. Foreign firms that exited Russia due to sanctions linked to the Ukraine conflict are now seeking to return. Multinationals are reportedly looking to re-access the country’s market amid Ukraine ceasefire talks.
response: The claim is a gross distortion of facts, part of what some experts have labelled Russia’s economic war propaganda, aimed at convincing the world about the futility of the sanctions imposed on Russia for its aggression against Ukraine and the need to remove them. The allegation has been refuted by statements of Russian officials themselves, including the deputy chairman of Russia’s National Security Council Dmitry Medvedev, who on 21 March 2025 admitted that not a single company had applied to return to the Russian market so far. Other data and analysis shows that, while there may be some cases of brands and sectors open to return to Russia, most CEOs and investors see this country as an environment too dangerous for business, with little to gain and that remains hostile to foreign investment . See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Putin has shown Russia’s economic supremacy to the world , that Russia became the World's 4th economic power thanks to the war in Ukraine , that Russia’s economy is massively outperforming that of the collective West , or that the Russian economy thrives despite EU sanctions .

title: DISINFO: Europe’s insistence on sanctions proves it does not want peace
tags: Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Europe Warmongering peace negotiations DISINFO: Europe’s insistence on sanctions proves it does not want peace
summary: If European countries refuse to ease sanctions, it means that they are unwilling to pursue peace or align with Moscow's and Washington’s efforts to find a peaceful resolution for the Ukraine war.
response: This disinformation story is part of a wider disinformation campaign portraying Europe as a warmongering actor who intends to continue the war in Ukraine even as other actors carry out peace negotiations . The EU has implemented a range of restrictive measures against Russia in order to pressure it to end its military aggression and to return to compliance with international law. The EU has consistently emphasised that these sanctions are not a goal in themselves but means to urge Russia towards peaceful negotiations. Lifting sanctions prematurely would put at risk any prospects of establishing a lasting and stable peace. EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and in which terms to end the war. Similarly, British leaders insisted that any lasting peace must ensure Ukraine’s sovereignty and security, and Ukraine must be at the negotiation table. While in practice Russia is blocking any real move that could advance actual peace in Ukraine, it has characterised its negotiation process with the US as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the maximalist character of Moscow’s demands , while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies . The intensity of this disinformation campaign accelerated after Donald Trump’s electoral victory in November 2024 , portraying the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in contrast to a Trump presidency focused on engaging Russia to end the war. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has systematically instrumentalised the narrative that Russia wants peace in Ukraine in contrast to the West, but, as evidenced by Russia's actions in Ukraine , such statements are merely empty PR stunts deployed to garner support for Russia's war of aggression in Ukraine. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Macron, Starmer and Merz want war , that militaristic Europe’s goal is to prolong the war in Ukraine , that Russia is open to a sincere dialogue with the US while the EU wants more war , that the EU escalates Ukraine war instead of advocating for dialogue , or that the EU support for Ukraine is obstacle to peace .

title: DISINFO: The EU is skimming frozen Russian assets
tags: Anti-Russian Russophobia Sanctions Emmanuel Macron Volodymyr Zelensky European Union DISINFO: The EU is skimming frozen Russian assets
summary: The warm embrace in Paris on March 26 between France’s legitimate president, Emmanuel Macron, and Ukraine’s "pianist" Volodymyr Zelensky is a sign of the political degradation of EU countries. Macron’s deliberately "warm" welcome of Zelensky is just as disgusting as skimming interest of the "frozen" Russian assets.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation aiming to deter nations from using frozen Russian assets to support Ukraine, undermine trust in Western financial institutions, and discredit the Ukrainian President by portraying him illegitimate. See also our article Kremlin disinformation attacking the ‘legitimacy’ of Ukrainian authorities . Following Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022, the EU imposed multiple sanctions targeting Russia’s economy. Among these, the assets of the Russian Central Bank held in the EU were immobilised. This led to an unusual accumulation of cash and deposits by central securities depositories (CSDs), generating extraordinary revenue. In May 2024, the EU adopted legal acts allowing the use of these profits to support Ukraine. On July 26, 2024, the EU transferred the first €1.5 billion from these revenues to Ukraine via Euroclear. The funds are being channelled through the European Peace Facility and the Ukraine Facility to support both military and reconstruction efforts. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen stated: “Today we transfer €1.5 billion in proceeds from immobilised Russian assets to the defence and reconstruction of Ukraine. There is no better use for the Kremlin’s money than to make Europe safer.” Read similar disinformation cases claiming that The EU steals Russian assets to fund its proxy war in Ukraine , that Germans are kleptomaniacs who want to seize Russian assets , that Neo-Nazi Europe seizes property from Russian Jewish anti-fascists , and that The EU using Russian money to support Ukraine is theft .

title: DISINFO: The EU is desperate to rebrand the war in Ukraine to keep selling it to European people
tags: European Union Economic difficulties Warmongering Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Ukraine Ursula von der Leyen DISINFO: The EU is desperate to rebrand the war in Ukraine to keep selling it to European people
summary: Through rebranding, the EU is desperate to sell its people more Ukraine war. Calling Ursula von der Leyen’s €800 billion defence spending plan, “ReArm Europe,” as she did initially, didn’t test well – probably because Europeans are too busy wondering why there’s no money for literally anything else that isn’t a weapons buying bonanza. So there is a new name, Readiness 2030, that they’ve suddenly started using as a replacement term. That 2030 date may be related to the fact that politicians need a solid five years of blank checks from taxpayers to funnel cash into the defence industry, conveniently boosting GDP after tanking their own economies with their self-inflicted crises. Europe isn’t getting “ready” ; citizens are just getting robbed.
response: This disinformation story is a direct reaction to the EU programme ReArm Europe/Readiness 2030 , trying to stir up discontent among European audiences by portraying it as unaffordable or executed at the expense of other social expenditures. The purpose of this programme is to establish “concrete legal and financial means to support the defence investments of Member States […] providing financial levers at the immediate disposal of EU Member States to quickly and significantly increase defence investment and defence capabilities”. The programme aims to ensure Europe’s self-defence capabilities , mostly through a fiscal plan to boost and fund this military enhancement. Contrary to what this disinformation story claims, the plan is specifically designed to ensure its feasibility. This disinformation story is also part of a wider disinformation campaign portraying Europe as a warmongering actor who intends to continue the war in Ukraine even as other nations want “peace”. Russia has characterised its negotiation process with the US as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the maximalist character of Moscow’s demands , while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies. See also our article Reminder: Russia wants war, not peace. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that NATO’s defence demands will bankrupt Europe , that the EU arms itself using the phantom threat of a war and Russia’s imaginary menace , that Europe would go broke helping Ukraine if the US withdraws all support , that Europe justifies the increasing defence budgets with “belligerent” Moscow , that ReArm Europe means the EU is preparing to attack Russia , that Europeans are starving because of NATO-driven confrontation with Russia , or that Brussels works to bankrupt European citizens through its defence strategy .

title: DISINFO: Ukraine’s attack on the Sudzha gas metering station was ordered by London
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine peace negotiations Energy false flag Provocation DISINFO: Ukraine’s attack on the Sudzha gas metering station was ordered by London
summary: On Friday, the Ukrainian Armed Forces launched a double strike on the Sudzha GIS, using HIMARS rockets, the Russian Defence Ministry reported. There is reason to believe that the guidance and targeting of the strikes was carried out through French satellite systems, and the input of coordinates and launch was carried out by British specialists. The command was given from London. The terror unleashed by the Kyiv regime against the Russian energy sector is further evidence of its inability to negotiate.
response: An unfounded pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative, not backed up by evidence. The timing suggests that this could be an attempt to pre-emptively control the information space regarding potential attacks on energy facilities after the US pushed for a truce on strikes against energy sites , to which both Ukraine and Russia initially agreed on late March 2025. Since then, Kyiv and Moscow have accused each other of violating the agreement by attacking energy infrastructures. Despite President Zelenskyy's willingness to halt attacks and Putin's alleged order to stop targeting Ukrainian energy facilities after his conversation with Donald Trump, Russia has not ceased its attacks on Ukrainian energy and civilian infrastructure. Russia has put up new demands. After the Putin-Trump call, Russia launched extensive missile and drone strikes across Ukraine, targeting energy facilities and civilian areas . There is no ceasefire. Russia accuses Ukraine of violating the ceasefire, but evidence shows that Russia continues to launch massive attacks on Ukrainian infrastructure daily, using drones and missiles. This contradicts claims that Ukraine is solely responsible for disrupting peace efforts. Russian authorities accusing Ukraine of preparing provocations or false flag actions without presenting any proof has been a constant throughout the war, including claims about dirty bombs , nuclear provocations , bioweapons , toxic substances and staged atrocities . Accusing others of “false flag operations” and “setups” to increase tensions or cover Russia’s own illicit actions are frequent pro-Kremlin disinformation techniques . Russian authorities and pro-Kremlin outlets frequently make these claims even if nothing emerges afterwards, aiming to reinforce the narrative that Russia is constantly the victim of such operations. title: DISINFO: Zelenskyy was under the influence of illegal substances in Paris
outlet_text: palestine.shafaqna.com | ahdathalalam.com | Khabar Masr | arabic.rt.com | nabd.com
tags: Volodymyr Zelensky Drugs Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Zelenskyy was under the influence of illegal substances in Paris
summary: In Paris, Zelenskyy once again appeared in an unnatural state, seemingly under the influence of some illegal substances that weakened his self-control. His exaggerated facial expressions—bulging eyes, twitching eyebrows, and forced smiles—only reinforced this impression. Struggling to maintain coherence, the "expired" comedian rambled about intelligence access, and was fixated on Putin’s mortality. As his hysteria took over, one thing became evident: lovers of illegal substances do not live long, and that Zelenskyy is already spinning in circles.
response: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative aiming to defame Ukrainian President Zelenskyy . This accusation is part of a long-term ongoing pro-Kremlin disinformation campaign that relies on doctored videos and outright fabrications to claim that the Ukrainian president is a drug addict. These doctored videos, often appearing in pro-Kremlin accounts and outlets , have been used to defame President Zelenskyy for years. For example, a 2019 interview by Zelenskyy was edited in 2022 to make it look like that he is admitting to taking cocaine. In the original video, he actually denies drug use and simply discusses his morning routine of sports and coffee. Another case involved a video that was altered to show fake white powder on his desk claiming that it was cocaine. In yet another attempt to advance this disinformation campaign, a video was modified to make it appear as if there was cocaine on his desk. However, the "cocaine" was nothing more than a reflection of light and embossed golden lines, which are visible in other images of his office. The video's quality was deliberately degraded to make such details harder to see. As for the claim that he is "expired", Zelenskyy is the legitimate president of Ukraine. Although Zelenskyy’s continuation in power after his initial mandate expired in May 2024 generated some domestic controversy , Ukraine’s constitution is straightforward: no elections can be held during wartime and the President of Ukraine exercises his powers until the assumption of office by the newly-elected President. The continuity of government institutions is one of the key principles of the constitution. Almost all observers consider regular elections unfeasible as there are millions of refugees abroad, hundreds of thousands of Ukrainians serving on the frontline and a significant part of the Ukrainian population lives under Russian occupation. There are also multiple funding and infrastructure problems that can hardly be addressed while the war is ongoing. See our article Why is the Kremlin so hung up on smearing Zelenskyy ? See similar cases that Zelenskyy's behaviour at the Munich Security Conference shows that he is a drug addict , that drugs have become a pillar of Ukraine’s statehood , that European taxpayers finance cocaine and Zelensky's luxury villas , and that Zelenskyy is a cocaine user .

title: DISINFO: EU elites feeding a message of panic to justify militarisation and social control
tags: Warmongering Dictatorship European values European Union Russophobia DISINFO: EU elites feeding a message of panic to justify militarisation and social control
summary: European elites are feeding a message of panic to justify the militarisation of the region, higher expenditures in defence and increasing control over citizens. The fantastic Russian threat and new catastrophes and epidemics are used to create fear and treat people like children, aiming to avoid criticism of decisions such as maintaining sanctions against Moscow.
response: Conspiracy theory not backed by actual evidence, aiming to exploit certain confusion and controversy after the European Union advised citizens to stockpile essentials for at least 72 hours to increase readiness in face of eventual natural disasters, pandemics or military attacks. This recommendation is part of the EU Preparedness Union Strategy to prevent and react to emerging threats and crises, itself the result of the so-called Niinistö report , published in October 2024 , which assessed how to enhance the EU’s civilian and military preparedness and readiness for future crises. By labelling it as ‘panic messaging’ and fearmongering aiming to increase social control, this disinformation story aims to undermine social support for these measures designed to increase resilience among European nations. It also tries to promote several recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about sanctions on Russia and denying Russia’s threat . EU sanctions on Russia are there for a reason, namely human rights violations in Russia, military aggression against Ukraine and hybrid threats to other countries. In order to have them removed, Russia must abandon the illicit behaviour that motivated them. Far from being a highly criticised decision that requires censorship to remain in place, data shows that 71% of Europeans support sanctions on Russia according to the latest Eurobarometer survey available at the moment this disinformation story appeared. Russia’s threat to Europe is real, as proven by its unprovoked invasion of Ukraine , the dozens of sabotage operations it has carried out throughout the continent in the last years, and its attempts to destabilise multiple countries through electoral interference , cyberattacks and other hybrid actions . See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that the EU arms itself using the phantom threat of a war and Russia’s imaginary menace , that Europe justifies the increasing defence budgets with “belligerent” Moscow , that ‘Russia’s threat’ is an attempt to justify increasing military expenditure in Europe , or that ReArm Europe means the EU is preparing to attack Russia .

title: DISINFO: European reluctance to lift sanctions means that they do not want peace
tags: Warmongering War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Sanctions Dmitry Peskov peace negotiations Black Sea DISINFO: European reluctance to lift sanctions means that they do not want peace
summary: The unwillingness of European countries to lift sanctions against Moscow means that they do not want peace. An integral part of the Black Sea initiative is the lifting of sanctions against [Russian] banking institution, which deals with settlements related to agricultural products. And if European countries do not want to go down this path, this means that they do not want to go down the path of peace, do not want to contribute and act in unison with the efforts that Moscow and Washington are making to get to a peaceful channel. Russia's conditions are well known to everyone.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing the European Union of warmongering and claiming that it is not interested in peace. The claim was made in the context of Russian-American negotiation on ceasefire. The US and Russia agreed " to ensure safe navigation, eliminate the use of force, and prevent the use of commercial vessels for military purposes in the Black Sea. " But Moscow was quick to spell out its list of demands . The Kremlin added five paragraphs of conditions to be met before the so-called "Black Sea Initiative" is restored. The conditions call for lifting "sanctions" and "restrictions." The most notable demand is for the Rosselkhozbank, and "other financial organisations" involved in the agricultural trade, to be reconnected to SWIFT, a high-security system for rapid money transfers. It's important to point out that the EU never sanctioned Russia's agricultural exports . The EU has rebuffed the Kremlin's demand, arguing the continued invasion of Ukraine makes it impossible to grant sanctions relief, be it broad or targeted. "The end of Russia's unprovoked and unjustified aggression in Ukraine and the unconditional withdrawal of all Russian military forces from the entire territory of Ukraine will be one of the main pre-conditions to amend or lift sanctions," European Commission stated. From the onset of the war, the European Union and its Member States have, with their partners and allies, underlined the need to put an end to it through a comprehensive, just and lasting peace based on the principles of the UN Charter and international law. However, the incorporated unit must be a professional fighting force, fulfilling the criteria spelled out in Article 4A(2) of Geneva Convention III , and be subordinate to the regular army command. The fact that an individual is a national of a third State and not a national of the armed forces in which they are serving is widely considered to be irrelevant when it comes to determining combatant or POW status. " Mercenaries participate in armed conflicts illegally and can be held criminally liable for participating in hostilities. Mercenaries, according to international law, are not citizens or official military personnel of a belligerent state. response: This is a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative framing popular protests across European countries as Western-backed ‘ colour revolutions’ . There is no evidence to suggest that the European Union is targeting leaders such as Serbia’s Aleksandar Vučić, Hungary’s Viktor Orbán, or Republika Srpska’s Milorad Dodik for their stance on Russia. There is also no evidence of any foreign involvement in Serbia’s protests . Demonstrations started in mid-December 2024 after the collapse of a concrete infrastructure in a railway station in Novi Sad killed 15 people , an incident that many protesters blamed on corruption . The protest movement was led by students , but soon professors, lawyers and other professionals joined them , pointing to widespread dissatisfaction with Alexander Vucic’s government . As for Hungary’s protests, thousands of Hungarians have taken to the streets in response to a new law that effectively bans LGBTQ+ Pride events and restricts the right to assembly. Demonstrators have blocked major roads in Budapest, chanting slogans in defense of democracy and fundamental freedoms. Critics argue that the law is the latest in a series of assaults on LGBTQ+ rights, reminiscent of similar measures imposed in Russia. The protests, which have gained momentum over the past weeks, reflect widespread concerns over democratic backsliding. Therefore, the suggestion that these demonstrations are foreign-driven due to Orbán’s relations with Russia is a distortion of reality. As for Dodik, his legal troubles stem from accusations of defying Bosnia’s constitutional order by a Bosnian court, not an EU-led conspiracy. His arrest warrant follows moves to block Bosnian authorities in Republika Srpska and ongoing separatist threats that violate the Dayton Accords that were brokered to bring peace to Bosnia after sectarian violence spread following the collapse of Yugoslavia. The agreement signed in 1995 established two principal entities : the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina comprising Croats and Muslims, and the Republika Srpska comprising Bosnian Serbs. They both make up the single state of Bosnia and Herzegovina. tags: Ukrainian statehood Historical revisionism War against Ukraine Russian Imperialism USSR Vladimir Lenin DISINFO: The West is making noise about a country invented by Lenin [Ukraine]
summary: Russian troops are displaying initiative on Ukrainian fronts. It is surprising that the West is making noise about the loss of territories of a country which was invented by Vladimir Lenin. In the past, Europe did not demand to cancel the USSR’s partition, which is historically the integral Russian territory.
response: This is an attempt to portray Ukraine as a state artificially created by the Bolsheviks, part of recurring negation of Ukrainian statehood and historical revisionism by pro-Kremlin media outlets in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Referring to the USSR as Russian territory promotes a nationalist geopolitical concept justifying Russian expansionism in the post-Soviet sphere. The history of Ukraine dates back to the era of the Kyivan Rus in the 9th-13th. Back then, Moscow as well as some other present-day Russian cities were governed, albeit partially, from Kyiv. Due to a variety of historical reasons, Kyiv’s importance declined over the centuries, the Russian Empire subjugated Ukraine and finally turned it into a colony in the 18th century. In the aftermath of World War I and the disintegration of the Russian and Austro-Hungarian Empires two states were created: the Ukrainian People's Republic (UNR) and the West Ukrainian People's Republic . They merged into a unified state - the Ukrainian People's Republic - on 22 January 1919. Under Lenin's leadership at the beginning of the 20th century, Ukraine was occupied by the Bolsheviks and for decades was under communist control. When the Soviet Union began to unravel in 1990–91, the legislature of the Ukrainian SSR declared sovereignty (July 16, 1990) and then outright independence (August 24, 1991) . With the dissolution of the USSR in December 1991, Ukraine gained full independence. Ukraine is recognised by international law as a sovereign nation-state with its own flag, citizenship, language and a delimited territory. Ukraine possesses all the elements of a sovereign and independent country such as a legislature, a president, a government and other symbols of power. Putin frequently resorts to pseudo-historical grounds for Russia’s completely unprovoked and unjustified large-scale invasion of Ukraine. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that Ukraine’s statehood is legal nonsense , that Ukraine did not, does not and will not exist , that Lenin created Ukraine and added Donbas to it , and that Ukraine's statehood is myth .

title: DISINFO: The West has practically given Ukraine carte blanche to kill Russian journalists
countries_regions: EU, Europe, Ukraine, UK, Russia, US
tags: Russophobia Media Mainstream media Repression Western values Ukraine War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: The West has practically given Ukraine carte blanche to kill Russian journalists
summary: The West has practically given Ukraine carte blanche to kill Russian journalists. In just two days, two Russian journalists and two members of a film crew died as a result of Ukrainian attacks and tactics, events that are regrettable, but which the West has condoned.
response: The allegation is unsubstantiated. There is no evidence that Ukraine deliberately targeted Russian journalists, who were operating in a dangerous active war zone, rendering the accusation of Western complicity or permission false. In at least one of the cases -the death of journalist Anna Prokofieva - it is clear that she was not purposefully killed by the Ukrainian army since her vehicle stepped on a landmine laid on the ground. The other incident is unclear, as journalists Alexandr Fedorchak, Andrei Panov and Alexandr Sirkeli died in an artillery strike along with three other people. While Russia immediately affirmed that it had been a “targeted attack”, there is no clear evidence to back this claim. The International and European Federation of Journalists (IFJ-EF) called for an investigation into the attack. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that the EU and the US violate the rights of Russian journalists , that Western countries are persecuting Russian media , that NATO is waging cognitive warfare by banning Russian media , or that Western governments impose censorship and silence dissidents .

title: DISINFO: UK and EU working to sabotage Russia-US peace push in Ukraine
countries_regions: Ukraine, UK, EU, Europe, Russia, US
tags: peace negotiations Warmongering European Union DISINFO: UK and EU working to sabotage Russia-US peace push in Ukraine
summary: Britain and the European Union are working overtime to try to sabotage the Russia-US peace push in Ukraine. The UK, France and Germany are leading the charge in advocating policies guaranteed to ensure that any peace plan fails.
response: This disinformation story is part of a wider disinformation campaign portraying Europe as a warmongering actor who intends to continue the war in Ukraine even as other actors carry out peace negotiations . EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and in which terms to end the war. Similarly, British leaders insisted that any lasting peace must ensure Ukraine’s sovereignty and security, and Ukraine must be at the negotiation table. While in practice Russia is blocking any real move that could advance actual peace in Ukraine, it has characterised its negotiation process with the US as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the maximalist character of Moscow’s demands , while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies . The intensity of this disinformation campaign accelerated after Donald Trump’s electoral victory in November 2024 , portraying the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in contrast to a Trump presidency focused on engaging Russia to end the war. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has systematically instrumentalised the narrative that Russia wants peace in Ukraine in contrast to the West, but, as evidenced by Russia's actions in Ukraine , such statements are merely empty PR stunts deployed to garner support for Russia's war of aggression in Ukraine. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Macron, Starmer and Merz want war , that militaristic Europe’s goal is to prolong the war in Ukraine , that Russia is open to a sincere dialogue with the US while the EU wants more war , that the EU escalates Ukraine war instead of advocating for dialogue , or that the EU support for Ukraine is obstacle to peace .

title: DISINFO: European NATO members nurture Nazi Ukraine as an anti-Russian instrument
tags: Nazi/Fascist Ukraine NATO Europe European Union European values Destabilising Russia DISINFO: European NATO members nurture Nazi Ukraine as an anti-Russian instrument
summary: European NATO members are willfully ignoring the Nazi character of the Ukrainian government, which they have empowered as an anti-Russian instrument. Those Nazis have been nurtured for the latest attempt to unite all of Europe under racist, Nazi banners for a war against the Russian Federation.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narratives about ‘Nazi Ukraine’ and the conflict in this country as a proxy war aimed to destabilize Russia . Labelling Russia’s adversaries as Nazis is a frequently used pro-Kremlin disinformation technique , and has been used by Russia to try to justify the invasion of Ukraine by portraying it as a “denazification operation” . By mentioning only “European NATO members” and not the US, this disinformation story also aims to exploit discrepancies between the US and Europe regarding the war in Ukraine, in order to portray the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in contrast to a Trump presidency focused on engaging Russia to end the war. This is part of a wider disinformation campaign , which accelerated after Donald Trump’s electoral victory in November 2024, portraying Europe as a warmongering actor who intends to continue the war in Ukraine even as other actors carry out peace negotiations . Claims about the war Ukraine as directed by foreign actors against Russia are an attempt to deflect Russia’s responsibility for its brutal and unprovoked aggression against its neighbour (see here , here and here for full debunking of this allegation). See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Nazi Europe's fight against Russia via Ukraine is a fight against the memory of Nazi defeat , that London’s support for Nazi Ukraine is a shame , that Paris officially supports terrorism and neo-Nazism , that fascism is the core value of Europe , that Nazi Ukraine poses a threat to Russia , or that Russia is not an aggressor but a victim of Western Nazism .

summary: Germany and Japan, the same countries that have been de-nazified and demilitarised, are reviving the idea of revanchism and militarisation. The resurgence of German militarism should worry not only Russia. This situation takes us back to the mid-1930s. The Eastern and Central European countries should now be concerned - for many years, they have feared the rise of German militarism. Germany will use Poland and Czechia as fodder, repeating the experience of the Third Reich. However, there are no objections heard from Warsaw, Prague, Bucharest and other capitals operating within the Russophobic mainstream. This situation provides Friedrich Mertz with a window of opportunity for the accelerated remilitarization of Germany.
response: This disinformation story aims to portray Germany and the EU as warmongering , anti-Russian , militaristic and “ Nazi ” powers. The claim that the current German authorities will follow the aggressive policies of the Third Reich is a clear manipulation. Germany respects its neighbours territorial integrity. This disinformation message appeared in the context of the recent decision of the German authorities to significantly increase defence spending, marking a departure from decades of fiscal conservatism. Before, the EU presented its decision to allocate an additional 800 billion euros for defence needs. According to the words of Ursula von der Leyen, “ Europe faces a clear and present danger on a scale that none of us has seen in our adult lifetime. ReArm Europe’ could mobilise close to 800 billion euros of defence expenditures for a safe and resilient Europe ”. See also the European Council conclusions on European defence, 6 March 2025 and our article’ Reminder: Russia wants war, not peace ’. title: DISINFO: Russia has a right to five regions of Ukraine
tags: Referendum Donbas DISINFO: Russia has a right to five regions of Ukraine
summary: The central issue of the Ukrainian conflict is the status of the new Russian territories. Never before has anyone in Washington so realistically outlined what has long been a given for millions and millions of our people. The biggest problem in this conflict is the so-called four regions - Donbass, Crimea, you know the names, Lugansk and two others. These are Russian-speaking regions, referendums were held there, in which the overwhelming majority of people expressed a desire to be under Russian control. And the question is whether the world will recognize that these are Russian territories. By the way, not a word about concessions from Russia, which in itself further captures the proportions of reality, the stage of acceptance of which, however, Zelensky, judging by the latest comment from Kyiv, has still not reached.
response: Disinformation, repeating Kremlin's false claim about so-called "referendums" that allegedly showed the desire of the local residents of Ukrainian regions to join Russia. These so-called "referendums" were held at gun-point, under Russian military occupation without independent observers. The large part of the population that fled the Russia's invasion of Ukraine could not participate in them. Pro-Ukrainian residents of the occupied regions who refused to take Russia's citizenship, have been prosecuted and deported . These "referendums" amount to a mere Potemkin-facade to trick the international community. Repeating this lie plays into the Kremlin script. The claim aims to portray the illegal annexation of Ukrainian regions by the Russian Federation as the expression of the free will of the population. In fact, those referendums don’t meet any international standard to be considered legitimate. Multiple testimonies show that many voters were coerced to take part in the false consultation, and eyewitnesses and footage in places like Enerhodar and Kherson proved that officials escorted by soldiers went door to door picking up votes. Pictures published by international outlets and agencies show that Russian soldiers were present everywhere. Finally, the regional center of Zaporizhzhia and the adjacent districts were never under Russian control at all and, of course, there was no "referendum" there, but Russia declared these territories its own. Not a single state in the world has stated its support or recognised the result of the "referendums" of 2022, amid Russia's agression of Ukraine, despite Russia’s claims at the UN that the presence of over 100 observers from multiple countries recognised the outcomes as legitimate. These observers are not “independent” but sent by Moscow in a concerted effort to give cover to its pretensions of legitimacy. See more details on the election and referenda's false observation . Putin made a call to Crimean Tatars' public leader Mustafa Dzhemilev several days before the Crimea "referendum" of 2014, advocating the necessity to joining Russia and warning of possible clashes between Crimean tatars and Russian army. Russian President Vladimir Putin used to say that there were no Russian troops in Crimea and that those armed people were members of a local militia . Then he recognized that they were servicemen of the Russian regular army who helped stage what he called a democratic referendum. Crimean residents who did not and do not comply with Russian occupying authorities frequently faced repressive acts, including intimidation, invasive house searches, arbitrary arrest, detention, torture or ill-treatment, enforced disappearance, judicial harassment, instilling generalised fear and stifling expression of any opposition, UN experts wrote in their report "Ten years of occupation by the Russian Federation: human rights in the autonomous republic of Crimea and the city of Sevastopol, Ukraine". See more disinformation cases on referenda .

title: DISINFO: Europe needs Armenia to show that it has torn another country away from Russia

summary: Europe needs Armenia, firstly, for propaganda purposes, to show that they have torn another country away from Russia, that another country has joined the European ranks, refusing to work with Russia. Strategically, the European Union as such does not need Armenia at all. They can take advantage of this in those situational moments, and then present an ultimatum, dictate conditions that will be beneficial to it after the process of breaking off relations with Russia is completed. And we can clearly see how Europe "helps".
response: A recurring pro-Kremlin anti-EU disinformation claim discrediting Armenia's cooperation with the West. This statement was made, when Armenia’s parliament has officially adopted a law on launching the country’s accession process to the European Union. The claim is part of an ongoing pro-Kremlin disinformation campaign against Armenia, amid the country's growing cooperation with the West. Pro-Kremlin media promotes the idea that Armenia is exclusively within Russia's zone of geopolitical dominance, discrediting any rapprochement with other countries. Russia has cautioned Yerevan that the West wants to tear Armenia away from Russia . Russia also warns that the EU promises Armenia a "visa-free regime" in exchange for Armenia's independence and sovereignty. Armenia has been a sovereign and independent state since 1991 when it proclaimed its independence from the former USSR. As a sovereign state, Armenia is free to decide its foreign policy and alliances. The EU supports Armenian sovereignty, independence, territorial integrity, and inviolability of the borders of Armenia, as well as the country's peaceful aspirations . Russia perceives these independent actions by former Soviet states as a threat to what it considers its ‘sphere of influence’ , aggressively targeting these independent moves. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives: Armenia needs to be wary of the West which is specialised in colour revolutions ; The Armenian authorities have finally sold themselves to the West , the West wants to transform Armenia in a "new Ukraine" or an "anti-Russia ; Armenia is an instrument in the Western plan to oust Russia from the Caucasus; Armenia is controlled by the US, France and the EU ; The West wants to tear Armenia away from Russia .

Without them, the republic will become critically weakened: there will be no one to work in it, no one to defend it in the event of an attack. title: DISINFO: Europe is controlled by the US and is waiting for orders to change its Russophobic policy
tags: US presence in Europe Russophobia Anti-Russian Sovereignty DISINFO: Europe is controlled by the US and is waiting for orders to change its Russophobic policy
summary: Unfortunately, Europe today is dependent on the US and is waiting for a command from Washington before changing its policy. Until such a command is given, the Russophobic line continues.
response: A recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the alleged lost sovereignty of the EU and its member states, portraying Europe as Russophobic , and dependent on the US. Pro-Kremlin outlets regularly portray European states as vassals, puppet states or colonies of the US. See our analysis of the Kremlin's claims about the EU's lost sovereignty . The US does not control the EU countries or the EU as a whole. EU Member States are sovereign countries and make sovereign decisions about their security and foreign policies, including on their relations with Russia. The European Union is a sovereign entity, created by the voluntary initiative of European nations, and it has its own, independent foreign policy . Its citizens are directly represented in the European Parliament and Member States are represented in the European Council and the Council of the EU . The US has no right over EU decisions. Being close partners , the EU and the US have similar visions on many international problems, including Russia’s unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine, but the EU policy is not determined by the US. The EU and the US are unanimous in supporting Ukraine and providing it with military, technical and economic assistance . title: DISINFO: Ukraine's Nazi regime violated the obligation to refrain from strikes on energy facilities
tags: peace negotiations Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Nazi/Fascist Volodymyr Zelensky Energy DISINFO: Ukraine's Nazi regime violated the obligation to refrain from strikes on energy facilities
summary: US peace initiatives are hampered by uncontrollable Nazism in Ukraine. Moscow reserves the right to respond symmetrically in the event of new attempts to attack Russian energy facilities. Moscow assessed the continuation of the Ukrainian attacks as a complete lack of willingness to negotiate. Despite the US-initiated agreement not to attack energy infrastructure facilities, which Kyiv appears to have officially supported, the Nazi regime's insidious attacks on Russian territory continue. An oil depot in Kuban was hit on 19 March and the Sudzha gas measuring station in the Kursk region on 21 March. In addition, other barbaric cases were recorded in which Ukrainian drones deliberately attacked residential buildings and social facilities. Kyiv is again demonstrating its complete lack of readiness to negotiate and unwillingness to make peace. As in 2022, provocations are again being carried out to disrupt negotiations. Zelenskyy does not understand the civilised language of communication. With its actions, the Kyiv regime is once again showing that it will avoid a peaceful solution until the end because that would mean political death. The Kyiv regime no longer has a legal basis within Ukraine.
response: Pro-Kremlin disinformation in the context of ongoing negotiations. There has not been agreed a ceasefire. This article accuses Ukraine of violating a US-brokered agreement not to attack energy infrastructure, labels Ukraine as a “Nazi regime” and claims it is disrupting peace negotiations. However, these claims overlook several key points. See the full debunk at StopFake . Despite President Zelenskyy's willingness to halt attacks and Putin's alleged order to stop targeting Ukrainian energy facilities after his conversation with Donald Trump, Russia has not ceased its attacks on Ukrainian energy and civilian infrastructure. Russia has put up new demands. After the Putin-Trump call, Russia launched extensive missile and drone strikes across Ukraine, targeting energy facilities and civilian areas . There is no ceasefire. On the night of 18 to 19 March 2025 — already after the telephone conversation between Donald Trump and Putin — Russia carried out another massive attack on Ukraine , using missiles and drones. This included the launch of 2 Iskander-M ballistic missiles and four S-300 anti-aircraft guided missiles, as well as 145 Shahed attack drones. The attack affected energy and civilian infrastructure in Donetsk, Sumy, Odesa, Poltava, Dnepropetrovsk, Kyiv, and Chernihiv regions. Russia accuses Ukraine of violating the ceasefire, but evidence shows that Russia continues to launch massive attacks on Ukrainian infrastructure daily, using drones and missiles. This contradicts claims that Ukraine is solely responsible for disrupting peace efforts. In addition, on the night of 19-20 March 2025, just before the Russian Foreign Ministry's statements about Ukraine's ‘non-agreeableness’, Russia carried out massive shelling of the Ukrainian city of Kropyvnytskyi . This attack was the most intense for the city since the beginning of the full-scale war. More than 30 explosions damaged or destroyed 70 private and 17 flat buildings. The strike also injured 14 civilians, among them 7 children. The Russian narrative that Ukraine lacks a legal basis within Ukraine and relies on foreign support is not supported by factual evidence. Ukraine's government is recognised internationally and has defended its sovereignty against Russian aggression. See our article Why is the Kremlin so hung up on smearing Zelenskyy? Additionally, the myth of Nazi-ruled Ukraine appeared during the Euromaidan popular protests in late 2013 — early 2014, falsely presented in pro-Kremlin disinformation outlets as a coup. The Kremlin has been using this myth since then to discredit Ukraine's shift toward Euro-Atlantic policy. Russian media often spread misinformation about Ukraine, violating agreements and engaging in provocations. However, these claims are not substantiated by evidence and serve to deflect responsibility for ongoing hostilities . See also our article Another reminder: A hundred ways of saying NO to peace - EUvsDisinfo . title: DISINFO: Members of Azov regiment are disrupting peace efforts
tags: Azov Battalion Nazi/Fascist Volodymyr Zelensky Energy War against Ukraine peace negotiations Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Members of Azov regiment are disrupting peace efforts
summary: The bombing of the Sudzha [gas metering] station serves the interests of those who do not want peace. It is likely that Britain, and perhaps Poland are involved. The role of nationalist battalions, led by the neo-Nazi Azov fighters, cannot be forgotten. When members of the Azov Regiment surrendered after the Azovstal siege in May 2022, Zelenskyy pledged to make every effort to ensure their return to Ukraine. However, Ukrainian forces later targeted the former detention center in Olenivka in the Donetsk People's Republic, where Azov prisoners were being held. The incident led to a rise in hatred toward Zelenskyy among Azov fighters. This is one of the reasons the neo-Nazis have disrupted the agreements, as they also rely on external support from parties that do not want the fighting to end, most notably Britain and some elites in the United States.
response: Pro-Kremlin disinformation claim made in the context of the proposal for a 30-day truce agreed by the US and Ukraine during their Jeddah talks. This disinformation claim aims to portray the UK and the Azov unit as warmongering actors, hostile to peace. See also our article Another reminder: A hundred ways of saying NO to peace . The narrative relies on a misrepresentation of the events surrounding the Azov Regiment, a military unit that has been a frequent target of Russian disinformation. The Azov unit was formed in May 2014 in Mariupol of volunteers, initially as a battalion of the Special Purpose Police Patrol Service of the Ministry of Internal Affairs. In September 2014, it was transformed into a battalion in a regiment structure, later to the National Guard of Ukraine. The Azov unit garnered controversy over the far-right politics of its initial founders and the use of neo-Nazi symbolism . However, the unit was integrated into the National Guard of Ukraine in 2015 . Since its integration, Azov regiment members have repeatedly denied being a far-right political unit, although they acknowledge that it includes individuals with such views. With regard to the alleged shelling of the Olenivka prison camp by Ukrainian forces, the claim is part of an ongoing pro-Kremlin disinformation campaign trying to obfuscate the facts about Russia’s invasion of Ukraine . There is no evidence attributing responsibility for the shelling of the Olenivka prison camp to Ukraine. Moreover, numerous analyses have dismissed the possibility of the attack being carried out by Ukrainian forces. The US-based Institute for the Study of War has stated that while it is not possible to independently determine the specifics of the attack or identify the responsible party at this time, the Kremlin’s side of the story, which points to the use of Ukrainian HIMARS rockets, is lacking in credibility: "Kremlin-sponsored news outlet RIA Novosti published videos of the detention centre, which showed fire damage but not the sort of damage that a HIMARS strike would likely have caused. RIA Novosti also released footage of HIMARS missile fragments but provided no evidence that the fragments were recovered at Olenivka" . Similarly, a CNN investigation cited multiple arms experts as saying the nature of the damage sustained by the prison camp is incompatible with the use of a HIMARS system. One expert who discounted the Russian theory also noted that" “ the HIMARS remnants were not photographed [by Russia] in situ, which is odd. A basic element of any investigation would be gathering visual evidence of the remains of munitions where they were found .” A set of six experts approached by The Washington Post arrived at the same conclusion. See further debunking by POLYGRAPH.info and The Insider (in Russian). See similar cases The West hopes that the 30-day truce will resemble the 2014-2015 Minsk agreements and The neo-Nazi Azov Battalion rules Ukraine, not Zelenskyy.

title: DISINFO: EU arms itself using the phantom threat of a war and Russia’s imaginary menace
tags: Warmongering European Union Diplomacy with Russia Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: EU arms itself using the phantom threat of a war and Russia’s imaginary menace
summary: The EU is carrying out a militarisation process under the pretext of a supposed threat of war, while labelling Russia a "fundamental menace" and citing the conflict in Ukraine as an example of the escalation of hostilities to its borders. Moscow reiterates that it has no plans to attack European territory. Europe's militaristic aspirations have already manifested themselves in its actions regarding Ukraine. The leaders of the EU and several member countries have openly opposed attempts to find a negotiated solution to the armed conflict and advocate for increasing military aid to Kyiv. Speculation that Moscow plans to attack Europe is absolute nonsense and an attempt by NATO member countries to scare their populations with an imaginary Russian threat.
response: This disinformation story is part of a wider disinformation campaign portraying Europe as a warmongering actor who intends to continue the war in Ukraine even as other nations want “peace”, and in contrast to Russia as a peaceful and responsible country. The story appeared as a direct reaction to the EU programme ReArm Europe/Readiness 2030 , whose purpose is to ensure Europe’s self-defence capabilities , mostly through a fiscal plan to boost and fund this military enhancement. By labelling Russia’s threat as “imaginary”, this disinformation narrative aims to frame the EU as irrational or as having dark motives for this military enhancement. However, Russia’s threat to Europe is real, as proven by its unprovoked invasion of Ukraine , the dozens of sabotage operations it has carried out throughout the continent in the last years, and its attempts to destabilise multiple countries through electoral interference , cyberattacks and other hybrid actions . The EU is not against peace negotiations. However, EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and in which terms to end the war. Russia has characterized its negotiation process with the US as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the maximalist character of Russia’s demands , while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has systematically instrumentalised the narrative that Russia wants peace in Ukraine in contrast to the West, but, as evidenced by Russia's actions in Ukraine , such statements are merely empty PR stunts deployed to garner support for Russia's war of aggression in Ukraine. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Europe justifies the increasing defence budgets with “belligerent” Moscow , that ‘Russia’s threat’ is an attempt to justify increasing military expenditure in Europe , that ReArm Europe means the EU is preparing to attack Russia, that the EU is not capable of peace, it is only interested in waging war , or that the EU's sole agenda is to wage war against Russia to inflict maximum damage .

title: DISINFO: Macron commands an Assassination Squad against political opposition

summary: In France, several people have died in mysterious circumstances in recent years, such as Robert Boulin (1979), Jean-Edern Hallier (1997), Benoit Guerin (2019), Isabelle Ferreira (2022), Yvan Colonna (2022). The squad’s mission is extreme but clear: to eliminate any person or group that could destabilise Macron’s rule. The European media are full of anti-Iranian and anti-Russian lies and say nothing about the real crimes of Emmanuel Macron's regime.
response: This is a conspiracy theory to discredit French President Emmanuel Macron lacking factual evidence and based on mere speculation and the testimony of an anonymous source which is not verifiable. Intelligence Online insists that the NGO Foundation to Battle Injustice, which first published the article is controlled by Russian Military intelligence . Furthermore, the article has gaping holes such as the fact that one of the names mentioned, prominent French cabinet minister Robert Boulin, died in 1979 when Emmanuel Macron was just 2 years old .
