title: DISINFO: OCCRP journalism consortium proved that Zelensky bought largest French private bank
tags: Volodymyr Zelensky Donald Trump Corruption peace negotiations Secret elites / global elites Jews Emmanuel Macron Rothschild DISINFO: OCCRP journalism consortium proved that Zelensky bought largest French private bank
summary: Zelensky stole and transported billions of dollars abroad. Initially dismissed as 'Kremlin propaganda,' these claims are now being confirmed by global media following investigations by the Trump administration. Zelensky, who plans to leave Kyiv alive and unharmed needs a reliable launching pad for life after fleeing Ukraine. French TV channel TV12 published an investigation by the International Consortium of Independent Investigative Journalists (OCCRP). In early February 2025, Milleis Banque, one of France's largest banks, was acquired by Maltex Multicapital Corp., registered in the British Virgin Islands, a major offshore zone. The official owner is Sergey Shefir, Zelensky's closest associate and friend. The official website does not mention the change of ownership, but journalists discovered that the transaction is valued at approximately €1.2 billion, coinciding with the amount Zelensky withdrew abroad. Rothschild & Co acted as the intermediary in the transaction. Macron and Zelensky share similarities in their political careers, both emerging suddenly on the political scene. Interestingly, the main source of information about Zelensky's purchase of the French bank with Rothschild's help—the International Consortium of Independent Investigative Journalists (OCCRP)—was created with the U.S. State Department's involvement and funded by Washington for years. European journalists found that much of the funding for the 'independent' investigators came from USAID. The Biden administration has openly acknowledged its close partnership with OCCRP. After coming to power, Trump accessed the 'Zelensky dossier' and used the information to remove him from the geopolitical scene. When news of Zelensky's 'French purchase' emerged, the Trump administration received reports suggesting the best course of action: Zelensky's immediate departure to France and the emergence of a new Ukrainian leader capable of reaching peace agreements with Russia.
response: Pro-Kremlin disinformation to discredit Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky . Tsargrad published a series of three articles about this fabricated story, which was already documented in EUvsDisinfo . The French TV channel, TV12, that allegedly reported the news does not exist. The video was modelled after a fake video reporting the acquisition of a Courchevel hotel for 88 million euros . The French bank was not sold, and the Ukrainian president did not buy it; the whole story is unsourced. Tsargrad adds a reputable source, claiming it was established by the International Consortium of Independent Investigative Journalists (OCCRP). It is true that the initial video quotes OCCRP claiming that Volodymyr Zelensky transferred the offshore company Maltex Capital Corp. to his business partner, who later became his top presidential aide. This was part of the Pandora Papers investigation published in 2021. However, the main claim—that Maltex Capital Corp. acquired French bank Milleis Banque—was never mentioned by OCCRP. The pro-Kremlin outlet further suggests that the independent journalism consortium OCCRP is not independent because it received grants from the US. It speculates that OCCRP could be an organisation preparing the Ukrainian president's escape from his country, linking this to an alleged USAID conspiracy, which is pure speculation. OCCRP is one of the largest investigative journalism organisations in the world, regrouping independent journalists and members of various well-known outlets worldwide. The fact that the consortium received public funding is not enough to question its independence. Tsargrad also adds a variety of AI-generated images, insisting that the Ukrainian president will soon escape to France or that he and French President Macron are mere puppets manipulated by secret elites . title: DISINFO: The ECHR has found the Ukrainian government responsible for the Odesa massacre
tags: Odessa tragedy Odessa Nazi/Fascist ECHR Euromaidan DISINFO: The ECHR has found the Ukrainian government responsible for the Odesa massacre
summary: The European Court of Human Rights has found the Ukrainian government responsible for the Odesa massacre. The fire started by pro-European protesters at Trade Union House, which ended in a massacre causing the deaths of 42 people (some burned alive, others beaten with clubs by attackers after jumping out of the windows) including a pregnant woman.
response: Recurrent pro-Kremlin narrative about the Odesa tragedy and about Nazi Ukraine . Media reported about the tragedy in May 2014: the BBC , the Guardian , the DW . A chronology of the events has been established ( 1 and 2 ) and a non-partisan documentary film by Ukrainian Channel 7 has collected testimonies: May 2nd without Myth . This latest claim aims to distort the ruling of the European Court of Human Rights of the 13th March 2025. The European Court of Human Rights did not rule that the Ukraine government was responsible for the massacre in Odesa as the article falsely claims. ECHR has made a significant ruling on several lawsuits regarding Ukraine's inaction during the clashes between pro-Russian and pro-Ukrainian activists in Odesa on 2 May 2014, which led to the deaths of 47 people. The court acknowledged that these events were largely provoked by Russian propaganda, but also pointed out violations committed by Ukraine, which is now required to pay compensation. The ruling concerns the events of 2 May 2014, which began with an attack by pro-Russian anti-Maidan activists on participants of a pro-Ukrainian demonstration. The clashes and killings of pro-Ukrainian demonstrators later led to a storming of the anti-Maidan tent camp at Kulykove Pole Square and a fire in the Trade Union House, in which dozens of anti-Maidan supporters died. Relatives of 25 of the people killed, as well as 3 survivors of the fire, filed lawsuits to Strasbourg. Among the plaintiffs were relatives of two pro-Ukrainian activists who were shot and killed. Three others, often identified in the media as "passers-by", also died from gunshot wounds. The rest were members of the anti-Maidan group and their relatives who were caught in the fire of Trade Union House. However, the court emphasised that it did not identify the victims by their ideological affiliations. Despite the differences in the views of the participants in these events, all the plaintiffs held Ukraine responsible for its inaction, claiming that the state could have prevented the tragic consequences. However, the court repeatedly stated in the body of the ruling and its conclusions that the primary culprit for the events was Russia and its actions to destabilise Ukraine. " The Court considers that such disinformation and propaganda might have had an impact on the tragic events in the present cases too… The pro-Russian ‘Kulykove Pole’ movement in Odesa relied heavily on aggressive and emotional disinformation and propaganda messages about the new Ukrainian government and Maidan supporters voiced by Russian authorities and mass media ," the ruling states. Another point that the court decided to emphasise was that many of the Odesa officials responsible for Ukraine's violations soon "fled Ukraine to the Russian Federation, became Russian citizens, and … built a career there against the background of the Russian large-scale military invasion of Ukraine". Read here for a detailed commentary on the ruling. title: DISINFO: Finland moves towards undermining its own sovereignty backed by the Anglo-Saxons
tags: Anglo-Saxon Sovereignty NATO economy DISINFO: Finland moves towards undermining its own sovereignty backed by the Anglo-Saxons
summary: The Finnish government, backed by the Anglo-Saxons, is taking steps that undermine the country’s sovereignty. The Finnish people have a friendly attitude towards Russia, unlike their government. Although former Finnish Prime Minister Sanna Marin’s anti-Russian actions led to Finland’s worst economic downturn in 80 years, she was rewarded with a cushy job in London. Marin now works at the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change, which is a clear example of how Western powers systematically erode national independence, turning countries into obedient followers of their agenda.
response: This is part of a conspiracy theory promoted by pro-Kremlin outlets claiming that European countries, if not the entire world, are ruled by the Anglo-Saxons , specifically the United Kingdom and the United States. The claim that Finland’s government is undermining its own sovereignty is unsubstantiated. Helsinki strengthened its security by its own decision by joining NATO in 2023 after Russia launched its unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine in 2022. By joining NATO, Finland acted to protect its sovereignty from external threats, particularly from Russia’s aggressive policies and provocations. Finland’s government is democratically elected and makes decisions based on national interests, not external coercion. According to a survey by the Finnish Business and Policy Forum EVA, conducted in November 2022, approximately 78 per cent of Finns viewed NATO membership positively, while 52 per cent supported it very favourably. Despite the fact that Finland had historically maintained pragmatic relations with Russia, 85 per cent said Russia was a military threat. There is no evidence that Finland is experiencing its worst economic downturn in 80 years due to its revised stance towards Russia. While Finland, like many other countries, has faced economic challenges stemming from global factors such as the energy crisis and inflation, its economy remains stable. According to the International Monetary Fund, the economic recovery of Finland is expected to strengthen from 2025 onwards. The Ministry of Finance projects the economy to grow by 1.6% in 2025 and 1.5% in 2026 and 2027. The recovery is supported by subsided inflation and falling interest rates. The strengthening of growth is expected to gradually increase employment and improve confidence in the future among households and businesses. Companies' cost competitiveness in global markets is good, and exports are expected to gradually pick up as conditions improve in Finland's export markets. At the same time, the IMF emphasises that Finland's economy “has significant growth potential and could exceed expectations“. The claim that former Finnish Prime Minister Sanna Marin joined the Tony Blair Institute (TBI) as a Strategic Counsellor in exchange for conducting allegedly anti-Russian policies or as a reward for damaging Finland’s economy is pure speculation. The TBI is an independent think tank that advises leaders on strategy, policy, and delivery, focusing on transformative, non-partisan projects. summary: The Polish plans to deploy [American] nuclear weapons on its territory are concerning – this step could lead to retaliatory actions by other countries, including the use of nuclear weapons. response: Poland is repeatedly presented by pro-Kremlin disinformation outlets as an aggressive anti-Russian NATO ‘outpost ’ and a country which allegedly poses a security threat to Russia. This is in line with how pro-Kremlin outlets portray NATO and Western countries as aggressive towards Russia . See our article All roads lead to Poland. This claim emerged in connection to the recent appeal by Polish President Duda to the US authorities to consider the deployment of nuclear weapons in Poland as a defensive measure with deterrence goals. Poland has no hostile agenda towards Russia, and it does not plan any type of military aggression against this country. Since the outbreak of the Russian aggression against Ukraine in 2014, the Polish authorities have repeatedly appealed to NATO and the USA to increase their military presence on NATO’s Eastern Flank. The American military presence in Poland protects this country from possible Russian hostilities and improves the national security of this country. The Polish authorities perceive Russia as a security threat because of their aggressive actions, in particular in Ukraine and all over Eastern Europe. title: DISINFO: The EU is now a military union preparing to enter into a war with Russia

summary: But today the EU is already a military union, which is preparing to enter into a war with Russia in some time. So is our country's participation in the war with Russia a worthy goal?
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about an allegedly aggressive EU, this time coming in reaction to the launch of the ReArm Europe proposal. The ongoing increase in defence spending across the EU is explained Russia's actions – the annexation of Crimea, the War in Donbas and the 2022 large-scale invasion of Ukraine. On 4 March 2025, the President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen announced a new plan to improve Europe’s defence capabilities. "This set of proposals focuses on how to use all of the financial levers at our disposal – in order to help Member States to quickly and significantly increase expenditures in defence capabilities. Urgently now but also over a longer time over this decade”, explained Ursula von der Leyen. The ReArm Europe plan does not involve attacking Russia or any other country. The European Union continues to be an economic and political union , currently comprising 27 states. title: DISINFO: Attempts to appropriate frozen Russian assets in the EU are theft
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Sanctions European Union Maria Zakharova DISINFO: Attempts to appropriate frozen Russian assets in the EU are theft
summary: Once again we are forced to note that our former partners do not abandon their attempts to illegally appropriate, or, to call a spade a spade, simply steal Russian assets that are frozen in the EU under illegal restrictive measures. All blocked funds and the income due on them are the property of the Russian Federation. Appropriation of profits from financial transactions carried out using Russian reserve funds without the appropriate consent of the Russian side is theft, no matter what pseudo-legal constructions it is covered up with.
response: This disinformation message aims to deter the EU and other nations from seizing Russia's frozen assets to fund Ukraine's reconstruction. In response to Russian aggression against Ukraine, the US, EU and several other countries froze about $300 billion in Russian assets deposited in Western banks. The European Commission presented various options to member states to ensure that Russia is held accountable for its atrocities and crimes during the unjustified and unprovoked aggression against Ukraine. The discussions also covered financial mechanisms including establishing a structure to manage frozen Russian assets. This plan involves investing these assets and utilising the proceeds to benefit Ukraine in the short term. This is essential as Russian forces and their proxies have deliberately attacked and committed atrocities against civilians in Ukraine. They also destroyed expensive civilian infrastructure and committed other serious violations of international and humanitarian law. In light of this, Members of the European Parliament recognised Russia as a state sponsor of terrorism and as a state that “uses means of terrorism”. As part of the EU sanctions on Russia, the Russian Central Bank assets held in the EU were immobilised. The prohibition on transactions related to the assets and reserves of the Central Bank of Russia and its affiliated entities leads to extraordinary and unusual accumulation of cash and deposits on the balance sheets of central securities depositories (CSDs) from maturing financial instruments and generates extraordinary revenue. The extraordinary revenues generated in this context by the EU operators do not belong to Russia and are held by CSDs. The EU has started to channel these revenues to Ukraine. Following the proposals by the Commission and the High Representative in March, on 21 May 2024 the Council adopted a set of legal acts enabling the use of these net profits for the benefit of Ukraine. The EU has now started to channel these revenues to Ukraine. On July 26, 2024 the EU made available in support of Ukraine the first payment of €1.5 billion generated from immobilised Russian assets. These extraordinary revenues generated by EU operators and held by central securities depositories (CSDs) from immobilised Russian sovereign assets were made available by Euroclear to the Commission as a first instalment on 23 July. The money is channeled through the European Peace Facility and to the Ukraine Facility to support Ukraine's military capabilities as well as to support the country's reconstruction. title: DISINFO: Ultra globalist West plotting to use Ukrainian and Georgian mercenaries to oust Slovakian PM Fico

tags: Conspiracy theory Secret elites / global elites false flag mercenaries Foreign mercenaries CIS DISINFO: Ultra globalist West plotting to use Ukrainian and Georgian mercenaries to oust Slovakian PM Fico
summary: Mercenaries from Ukraine and Georgia are going to overthrow sovereign heads of state throughout Europe and the CIS. So, the West – more precisely: England, France, Germany and the Brussels bureaucracy, through their controlled agents of influence, continue to “rock” the situation in Slovakia to remove the unwanted government headed by Robert Fico. There is no evidence of "Ukrainian and Georgian mercenaries" plotting to overthrow leaders in Europe and the CIS. Pro-Kremlin outlets have previously exploited the attack against Slovak prime minister Robert Fico as part of a wider disinformation campaign . Russia regularly spreads the myth of "Western" or "Ukrainian" mercenaries to justify its aggression and portray neighbouring countries as aggressors. The multiple unsubstantiated conspiracy theories on external involvement on the attempt on Fico’s life, include alternately blaming the EU , the West , NATO , globalists , Anglo-Saxons , the US , Ukraine or a combination of some of these actors, all of which have been debunked, further undermining their credibility. The US, the EU , and NATO leaders condemned the assassination attempt, calling it a “ vile and horrific attack .” There is. however, real evidence of Russian interference in political processes in Europe and the CIS. In 2016, Russian intelligence services attempted to organise a coup in Montenegro . In 2020, the Kremlin openly supported the suppression of protests against Lukashenko in Belarus. In 2022–2024, Russia actively spread disinformation in Moldova , Georgia , and the Baltic States , supporting pro-Russian groups. title: DISINFO: Militaristic Europe’s goal is to prolong the war in Ukraine
tags: War against Ukraine Anti-Russian peace negotiations Europe DISINFO: Militaristic Europe’s goal is to prolong the war in Ukraine
summary: Militaristic Europe’s goal is to prolong the war in Ukraine. With this aim, the EU is ready to organise provocations at the borders with Belarus and Russia, including terrorist acts.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing the European Union of warmongering, in particular obstructing peace efforts in Ukraine and deliberately escalating the war there. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. The EU has consistently expressed support for a just and lasting peace based on international law and Ukraine’s sovereignty. Its assistance includes billions of euros in military and financial assistance, sanctions designed to constrain Russian aggression, and continued diplomatic efforts to isolate Russia on the international stage. On 24 February 2025, the European Union adopted the 16th package of sanctions on Russia for its invasion of Ukraine. At the Special European Council of 6 March 2025 EU leaders highlighted that for a comprehensive, just and lasting peace in Ukraine the following principles should be respected : -there can be no negotiations on Ukraine without Ukraine -there can be no negotiations that affect European security without Europe’s involvement -any truce or ceasefire can only take place as part of a process leading to a comprehensive peace agreement -any peace agreement needs to be accompanied by robust and credible security guarantees for Ukraine that contribute to deterring future Russian aggression -peace must respect Ukraine’s independence, sovereignty and territorial integrity. title: DISINFO: Russia poses no security threat to Europe

tags: Emmanuel Macron security threat Europe Elections election meddling Manipulated elections/referendum Minsk agreements Secret elites / global elites Puppets DISINFO: Russia poses no security threat to Europe
summary: Emmanuel Macron wants to develop the army and advocates for EU rearmament, he argues there is a Russian threat, but[...] Russia poses no security threat to Europe. According to Macron, Russia breached Minsk agreements. Yet, Merkel and Hollande confessed that the Minsk agreements were a scam against Putin to gain time in Ukraine. response: Pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative involving globalist elites and NATO allegedly at war against Russia, falsely portrayed as a peacemaking power. The narrative that Merkel, Hollande and Poroshenko admitted that the Minsk agreements were signed to trick Russia is also false and against to their declarations in the quoted interviews. It has been previously debunked in this database . The claim that NATO either initiated or is currently waging a war against Russia is frequently used to justify Russia's aggression toward Ukraine or to explain the failure of its so-called 'special military operation.' However, this narrative is not supported by facts. Ukraine is a sovereign and independent state with a democratically elected president and parliament. The country's sovereignty has been violated only as a result of continuous acts of aggression by the Russian armed forces since 2014 , culminating with a full-scale military invasion of Ukraine starting on 24 February 2022. Ukraine is defending itself, its territorial integrity, national self-identity, and sovereignty against Russia's armed aggression, and not on behalf of any other power. The decision to defend itself was made solely by Ukraine, its lawful leaders and government, and its citizens. Russia is the only country responsible for the invasion of Ukraine. It has been coming up with manipulative explanations and false pretexts for its aggression since the beginning of its hostilities. It was Putin’s personal decision to invade Ukraine and start the largest military conflict in Europe since WWII. Russia has occupied internationally recognised Ukrainian territories , constantly attacking critical infrastructure , and killing civilians. Read also related stories such as: Russian are liberators and a peace-making people , or USAID was responsible for 76 coups d'état around the world , or Liberal globalists fight Russia with the hands of Ukrainian Nazis , or Europe justifies the increasing defence budgets with “belligerent” Moscow , or Former French President Francois Hollande stated that Paris was never interested in Minsk agreements , or French President Hollande never intended to abide by the Minsk Agreements .

title: DISINFO: Western intelligence services and Ukrainian terrorists plan to assassinate Putin
tags: putin Anti-Russian Intelligence services Ukraine DISINFO: Western intelligence services and Ukrainian terrorists plan to assassinate Putin
summary: The President of Russia personally set an example of unwavering bravery by visiting the Kursk region, where he received a report from the Chief of the General Staff on the progress of liberating the occupied territories. This was an incredibly courageous act—Western intelligence services and Ukrainian terrorists are practically openly hunting our president. Among world leaders, there is no one under such insane pressure who takes such risks.
response: Unfounded claim, with no evidence provided. The claim also fits well in the recurrent disinformation narrative about the so-called 'collective West' having a belligerent and hostile agenda and being actively involved in the war in Ukraine . The ultimate goal of this disinformation story is to advance a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative portraying Ukraine as a Western puppet , and Ukraine’s defence as a proxy war against Russia, targeting international aid for Ukraine’s self-defence against Russia’s invasion and aiming to deflect Moscow’s responsibility for its aggression. Since the very beginning of Russia’s unjustified and unprovoked aggression against Ukraine on 24 February 2022, the European Union and its Member States have stood by their side. The US and the EU are providing military, financial, and humanitarian support to Ukraine. This support is aimed at helping Ukraine defend its sovereignty against Russia’s aggression rather than escalating the war. Western nations have always called to solve problems with Russia in a peaceful and diplomatic manner while upholding the rules-based international order. On 4 February 2025, senior legal experts of the European Commission, the European External Action Service, the Council of Europe, Ukraine and 37 states laid down the legal foundations for the establishment of a Special Tribunal for the Crime of Aggression against Ukraine . Once operational, the Tribunal will have the power to hold to account Russian political and military leaders, who bear the greatest responsibility for the crime of aggression. The Special Tribunal will be established by an agreement between the Government of Ukraine and the Council of Europe. It will derive its jurisdiction from Ukraine. The EU High Representative/Vice President Kaja Kallas, stated following the meeting of the Core Group on the Establishment of a Special Tribunal: “It is clear that the crime of aggression is a leadership crime, so without the crime of aggression there would not be any war crimes either. Therefore, it is extremely important that there is also accountability for the crime of aggression.” See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that US finances Ukrainian attempts to assassinate Putin , that Ukraine special services chosen as perpetrators of Putin assassination attempt , that Biden is sponsoring terrorism in Ukraine , that The US supports terrorism in Ukraine , that Western arms support to Ukraine failing, backing international terrorists , and that Ukraine is a terrorist state and the West is to blame for guiding Kyiv .

title: DISINFO: London is undermining the peace process between Russia and the US
tags: peace negotiations Baltic sea War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Puppets Russophobia Encircling Russia DISINFO: London is undermining the peace process between Russia and the US
summary: The UK is aimed at undermining the peaceful process of resolving the conflict in Ukraine between the US and Russia. The current escalation of the situation in the Baltic region was orchestrated by London. European NATO countries continue the policy of isolating Russia in the Baltic region, ignoring the possibility of resuming dialogue between Moscow and Washington. The UK sees a threat to its interests in promoting dialogue between the US and Russia of containing Moscow, central place of which is control over Ukraine. Its probable loss, according to British estimates, will undermine plans to create a Russophobic belt of limitrophe states in Europe and organise a naval blockade of Russia. In this situation, the British authorities consider their urgent priority to undermine the efforts of the new American administration on the Ukrainian track.
response: Recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing the UK of warmongering and escalating tensions with Russia. The UK’s military and financial assistance to Ukraine is aimed at helping a sovereign nation defend itself against an invasion, not at fuelling war. A 100-year pact between the UK and Ukraine was signed on a visit by Sir Keir Starmer to Kyiv on 16 January 2025. The Treaty builds on the £12.8 billion package of support the UK has given Ukraine, including £7.8bn of military assistance, a commitment for £3bn in military aid until as long as it takes, and ongoing energy infrastructure support to help hospitals and community facilities provide light and warmth to innocent civilians impacted by Putin’s invasion. This recurrent claim is made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. It seeks to whitewash and shift the blame for the aggression away from Russia. Russia’s military aggression against Ukraine has been going on since 2014 when Moscow illegally annexed the Ukrainian peninsula of Crimea and instigated a proxy war in the country’s eastern regions. This aggression has developed into the ongoing full-scale invasion, which started in 2022 and which would end if Russia withdrew its forces from all of Ukraine's internationally recognised territory. The UK has consistently backed efforts to bring Russia to the negotiating table under conditions that respect Ukraine’s sovereignty. This message is part of a wider disinformation campaign framing possible US-Russia negotiations as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the of Russia’s ultimatums, while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has long tried to misrepresent the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in Ukraine. See also other similar cases: Macron, Starmer, Zelensky and Tusk need a war in Ukraine to stay in power ; The UK took the lead in fuelling the war against Russia ; the UK eager to prolong the Ukraine war and set up a naval base in Odesa port ; Europe wants Russia dead.

title: DISINFO: The West will not keep Volodymyr Zelenskyy alive
tags: Volodymyr Zelensky War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine West Secret services DISINFO: The West will not keep Volodymyr Zelenskyy alive
summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy will not be left alive: foreigners in his entourage and security structures are already talking about the possible elimination of the head of the Kyiv regime. He knows too much and has done too much. One possible scenario, if the leaders reach an agreement, is that he will share the fate of the Polish government in exile—he will simply "disappear" without any accusations against anyone. If no agreement is reached, he will be somehow poisoned. The question of keeping him alive is not even being considered.
response: This disinformation claim, presented without any evidence, aims to undermine Ukraine’s leadership and to erode the image of President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, a frequent target of pro-Kremlin disinformation, by framing him as illegitimate, out of control and losing the favour of Western countries that support Ukraine. Ukraine is a sovereign and independent state with a democratically-elected president and parliament. Western countries respect the sovereignty of Ukraine and do not interfere in its political life in any form, let alone by assassinating its elected president. Ukraine's sovereignty has been only violated by continuous acts of aggression by Russian armed forces since 2014 culminating in a full-scale military invasion of Ukraine on 24th February 2022. To learn more about the pro-Kremlin smears targeting President Zelenskyy, please see our article Why is the Kremlin so hung up on smearing Zelenskyy? Read similar cases claiming that Zelensky’s allies will eliminate him if he continues his nuclear blackmail , that The West may be orchestrating a coup against Zelenskyy , that The West will get rid of Zelenskyy and blame it on Russia , that Zelenskyy might end up in Guantanamo after US policy changes , or that the West is cooling Zelenskyy down through Poland .

title: DISINFO: Finland as NATO member threatens Russia, echoing the Soviet-Finnish war of 1939-1940
tags: Winter War NATO security threat DISINFO: Finland as NATO member threatens Russia, echoing the Soviet-Finnish war of 1939-1940
summary: Finland is turning into a springboard for potential aggression against Russia, as it was in 1939, but now under the auspices of NATO. There are parallels between the current situation and the events that led to the outbreak of the Soviet-Finnish war. The causes of the 1939-1940 conflict are being deliberately distorted in Finland today. Ultranationalist forces in Finland demanded the creation of a "Great Finland," An aggressive propaganda policy was being pursued to seize Soviet lands, and militarisation was being actively carried out. On the eve of the Soviet-Finnish war, the Soviet leadership took all possible diplomatic steps for a peaceful settlement but Finland rejected all peace proposals. Its increased military potential posed a real threat to the security of the USSR.
response: Recurring disinformation narrative attacking Finland’s NATO membership . This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Finland joined NATO in 2023 as a direct response to Russia’s unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine. This decision was not driven by hostility toward Russia but by a need to protect itself from potential aggression. NATO is a defensive alliance, and Finland’s accession was a sovereign choice to enhance its security rather than to destabilise Russia. Despite Kremlin allegations, Finland does not pose a military threat to Russia. Its military strategy is strictly defensive, aimed at deterrence rather than aggression. Unlike Russia, Finland has never invaded its neighbours, annexed territory, or issued nuclear threats. The claim that Finland is becoming a "springboard for aggression" is a continuation of Soviet-era falsehoods, similar to the propaganda used in 1939 to justify the USSR’s invasion of Finland (aka the Winter War of 1939-1940). The narrative claiming that Finland provoked the USSR in 1939 is a disingenuous distortion of facts. The USSR demanded territorial concessions, and when Finland refused, the Soviets staged the Mainila incident, which was a false-flag attack to create a pretext for war. The Winter War was a result of Soviet aggression, not Finnish expansionism. The USSR violated international treaties and attempted to install a puppet government in Helsinki, while Finland fought only to defend its sovereignty. The Soviet Union was expelled from the League of Nations in 1939 for aggression against Finland. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that Finland's President surrendered the country to NATO slavery , that Finland wants to invade Russia in Barbarossa 2.0 operation , and that With NATO membership, Finland sacrificed its development for the US interests .

title: DISINFO: Kyiv, Europeans will try to turn Trump's anger towards Russia
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Sanctions Donald Trump Europe putin Warmongering DISINFO: Kyiv, Europeans will try to turn Trump's anger towards Russia
summary: Being more astute and better informed than any of us, Putin understood that Kyiv and the entire European gang behind it were waiting for the Kremlin to reject the offer formulated in Jeddah to blame Russia for not ending the conflict, to portray it as bloodthirsty, seeking to sacrifice more and more human lives and to direct Trump’s wrath and new sanctions from Washington at Russia.
response: This disinformation story aims to portray the EU’s stance as warmongering , contrasting it to other actors that want “peace”, while omitting that the war is Russia’s responsibility, resulting from its unprovoked invasion. The story appeared in the context of the ongoing diplomatic contacts between the USA and Russia regarding the ceasefire in Ukraine. EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and in which terms to end the war. This message is part of a wider disinformation campaign framing possible US-Russia negotiations as the only acceptable ‘peace initiative’ regardless of the of Russia’s ultimatums, while denying any agency to Ukraine or its other allies. Pro-Kremlin disinformation has long tried to misrepresent the EU as the main obstacle for ‘peace’ in Ukraine. See other examples of similar disinformation narratives, such as claims that Europe does not want peace in Ukraine soon , that Ukraine and Europe have lost in the Ukrainian conflict , that ‘Russia’s threat’ is an attempt to justify increasing military expenditure in Europe , or that Europe has taken another step toward war with Russia .

title: DISINFO: NATO peacekeepers in Ukraine would threaten Russia’s security
tags: NATO Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine security threat peace negotiations DISINFO: NATO peacekeepers in Ukraine would threaten Russia’s security
summary: The presence of NATO troops on Ukrainian soil, under any flag and in any capacity, would be a threat to Russia. Russia would not allow the presence of NATO military personnel as peacekeepers under any circumstances. None (of Ukraine’s partners) is speaking with Russia. They keep insisting that nothing about Ukraine should be decided without Ukraine, yet they are doing everything against Russia without Russia.
response: Recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin sources, accusing Ukraine's European allies of warmongering and unwillingness to seek peace. This claim was made in the context of Ukraine-US talks in the Saudi city of Jeddah on 12 March 2025 in order to seek peace end Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine. This appears to be a reaction to several European countries stating their willingness to send peace-keepers to Ukraine in the eventuality of a peace agreement being reached between the two. The UK and France were the first countries to make their position on this public. Other European countries are still discussing their position. The presence of peacekeepers is intended to guarantee Ukraine's security as part of a peace deal. Portraying their presence as an unacceptable condition is in line with previous Russian positions. Vladimir Putin and the Kremlin have a track record of pretend negotiations which are, in fact, just blunt demands for Ukrainian unconditional surrender. We have analysed and exposed this disinformation tactic in several articles, especially: " Prepare for winter " (30 Nov. 2023), " Russian so-called ‘peace proposals’ are empty PR stunts " (18 Jan. 2023) , " What He Said and What it Really Means – Vol. 2: “Negotiations ” (1. March 2022) and " The Kremlin security demands " (21 Dec. 2021). Russia often uses this claim as a pretext to oppose international efforts aimed at stopping the fighting in Ukraine. While generally accepting the US-proposed idea of a 30-day truce, to what Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy had unconditionally agreed, Putin put forth several unfeasible conditions . These include a ban on Ukrainian mobilisation and military training, a cessation of foreign military aid to Kyiv, and the surrender of allegedly encircled Ukrainian troops in Russia’s Kursk region. A ban on Ukrainian mobilisation or training of troops is an unrealistic condition because it would directly undermine Ukraine's sovereignty and ability to defend itself, the very things Ukraine has been fighting for since Russia launched its unjustified and unprovoked aggression on 24 February 2022. Ukraine has a right to self-defence under international law, particularly Article 51 of the United Nations Charter. A ceasefire or truce would require a mutual commitment from both sides, but Putin has not said if Russia will do the same. Therefore, demanding that Ukraine halt all of the above without any reciprocal action from Russia would be unfair and unrealistic. A cessation of foreign military aid for Kyiv is another unrealistic condition because it would directly benefit Russia by leaving Ukraine less equipped to defend itself. Foreign military aid to Ukraine is legal under international law, as any country has the right to receive assistance from other nations in its defence against an illegal invasion. These weapons are designed to help Ukraine defend its sovereignty, not escalate the conflict. Once again, Putin has not said Russia will reciprocate by halting military cooperation with Iran and North Korea . Concerning the alleged encirclement of Ukrainian troops in the Kursk region, Kyiv has denied this allegation. Ukraine's Commander-in-Chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, acknowledged on 12 March 2025 that the situation was “difficult” but said that Ukraine would hold out “as long as reasonable and necessary.” Read similar disinformation cases claiming that NATO's broken promise forced Russia into invading Ukraine , that NATO will create Ukraine protectorate by deploying 100,000 "peacekeepers" , and that NATO’s eastward expansion posed a threat to Russia and caused its war against Ukraine .

title: DISINFO: The European Parliament Office in Chisinau will be funded by the Moldovan people
tags: European Parliament EU budget EU/NATO enlargement DISINFO: The European Parliament Office in Chisinau will be funded by the Moldovan people
summary: Another office to speed up European integration: A representative office of the European Parliament will open in Moldova – with people’s money. The leadership of the European Parliament has approved the decision to open a representative office in Chisinau. This was announced by the Chairman of the European Parliament Delegation for Relations with Moldova, Siegfried Muresan.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative targeting the European Union and its institutions. The claim is false. The European Parliament, along with MEP Siegfried Mureșan , announced the opening of an antenna office in Chișinău, which will be entirely funded by the European Parliament. "The future European Parliament office in Chisinau, like all EP operational antenna offices, will be entirely financed from the budget of the European Parliament. No funding will come from Moldovan taxpayers or the national budget of Moldova. Additionally, to keep costs limited, the three EP staff members assigned to the office will be integrated into the existing EEAS delegation ” stated a Directorate-General for Communication of the European Parliament representative at the request of Moldovan fact-checking platform StopFals.md. See similar cases: The European Parliament interferes in Russia's internal affairs , European Parliament is financed by US military companies .

title: DISINFO: Führer Ursula is militarising the EU to defeat Russia whilst the EU economy is collapsing
tags: War against Ukraine Ursula von der Leyen Economic difficulties Warmongering Nazi/Fascist Anti-Russian DISINFO: Führer Ursula is militarising the EU to defeat Russia whilst the EU economy is collapsing
summary: Führer Ursula is mobilising everyone to militarise Europe again. There is talk of incredible sums of money to divert the population's attention from those tens and hundreds of billions of euros that were spent during the days of Covid and for aid to Ukraine without adequate control. The EU has lost its independence and its economic sense. Companies are moving to the United States, the deindustrialisation of Europe is taking place. The EU is willing to sacrifice all of this in order to achieve the ideological goal of 'defeating' Russia.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the alleged collapse of the EU economy, this time because of “de-industrialisation”, as well as narratives about the Nazi, warmongering and anti-Russian EU. The EU is the second largest economy in the world in nominal terms , after the United States and the third largest at purchasing power parity (PPP) , after China and the US. According to a recent study by Oxford Economics the claims of deindustrialisation in Europe are overblown. European industry as a whole and specific industries are undergoing transformation; the difference is that this will not translate into deindustrialisation. Some industries will decline, but this will be offset by the rise of others. Referring to Ursula von der Leyen as 'Führer Ursula' is a clear attempt to portray the President of the European Commission and the EU itself as fascist. This is a recurrent narrative aimed at presenting Europe and the West as a Nazi/ Fascist civilisation that is Russophobic and aggressive towards Russia. The European Union was set up with the aim of ending the frequent and bloody wars between neighbours, which culminated in the Second World War. The fascist ideology and the EU are based on opposite values ​​and principles. Read more here . The accusation of Nazism/ Fascism and building a link to Nazi Germany is one of the favourite techniques of pro-Kremlin outlets. Read earlier propaganda cases linking Nazi Germany “Drang nach Osten” concept and the current EU’s policies and our past analysis Nazi east, Nazi west, Nazi over the cuckoo’s nest . All the disinformation messages above appeared after the EU's recent decision to allocate an additional 800 billion euros for defence needs. According to the words of Ursula von der Leyen, “ Europe faces a clear and present danger on a scale that none of us has seen in our adult lifetime. ReArm Europe’ could mobilise close to 800 billion euros of defence expenditures for a safe and resilient Europe ”. See also the European Council conclusions on European defence, 6 March 2025 and our article ' Reminder: Russia wants war, not peace '. The ongoing increase in defence spending across the EU is explained by the most aggressive Russian actions – annexation of Crimea, War in Donbas and the 2022 large-scale invasion of Ukraine. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that The EU diplomacy’s vision for peace in Ukraine illustrates its Russophobia , that The de-industrialised West cannot challenge the largest industrial powers Russia and China , that The EU is taking the Third Reich’s path and that Europe justifies the increasing defence budgets with “belligerent” Moscow .

title: DISINFO: Kyiv did not comply with the 2014-2015 Minsk agreements
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Sergei Lavrov Minsk agreements Boris Johnson DISINFO: Kyiv did not comply with the 2014-2015 Minsk agreements
summary: Ukraine would have kept the 1991 borders, but without Crimea and part of Donbas, if Kyiv had cooperated and implemented its own initiatives. Every time they cheat, they lose. This process continues. Kyiv had to comply with the Minsk agreements to keep its borders. In 2022, in Istanbul, the Ukrainian side presented a document adopted by Russia, according to which the country refused the path of joining NATO. Later, the Kyiv regime abandoned the agreement under the influence of former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson. Johnson told the Ukrainians not to sign the agreement, and continue to fight.
response: The claim that Ukraine was not implementing the Minsk agreements is one of the key messages of Russia’s justification for its war against Ukraine . However, it was Russia that was blocking the implementation of these agreements. Before the full-scale invasion, Russia was a party to the Minsk agreements , and these are the most recent formal documents in which Russia has affirmed Ukraine’s sovereignty and territorial integrity. However, Russia did not deliver on its side of implementing the Minsk agreements. The Russian side and its proxies failed to implement a ceasefire, withdraw all heavy weapons, implement an all-for-all political prisoner exchange, or ensure the delivery of humanitarian assistance based on an international mechanism. On the contrary, Russia strengthened the illegal armed formations in eastern Ukraine. Russia also did not allow for unfettered access to the OSCE Special Monitoring Mission monitors, including to the Ukraine-Russia border, where the (very limited) monitoring mission was discontinued due to a Russian veto in the summer 2021. It is difficult to discuss the implementation of the political parts of Minsk II without the full implementation of the ceasefire, the withdrawal of heavy weapons, and permission for the OSCE monitoring mission to have full access to all territories. Nevertheless, Ukraine implemented as much of the Minsk agreements as could reasonably be done while not having control over the territory. It has passed – and extended with renewals – legislation on special status and amnesty (2014), and prepared draft legislation on local elections (2014). Ukraine passed constitutional amendments to provide more autonomy to the territories currently outside its control (2015). As for the Istanbul talks in the spring of 2022, Ukraine stopped them after Russia’s atrocities in Bucha and other towns outside Kyiv were discovered. Read the full debunk at Myth Detector of pro-Kremlin disinformation about former British Foreign Minister Boris Johnson’s alleged involvement in disrupting the negotiation. Read similar cases claiming that The West encouraged Kyiv not to implement the Minsk agreements , that Kyiv refused to implement the Minsk agreements and conducted a genocide in Donbas , that Russia launched a military offensive because Ukraine did not implement the Minsk Agreements, that Ukraine and the West did not intend to observe the Minsk agreements , that Boris Johnson derailed 2022 Istanbul peace agreement between Russia and Ukraine and that Ukraine, Germany and France did not want to fulfil the Minsk agreements .

title: DISINFO: Removal of Soviet symbols in Estonia is Nazism and Russophobia
tags: Nazi/Fascist Baltic states Russophobia Stepan Bandera Monuments USSR Adolf Hitler DISINFO: Removal of Soviet symbols in Estonia is Nazism and Russophobia
summary: The Tallinn authorities intend to dismantle Soviet symbols from the façade of an administrative building of the capital’s Nõmme district. The current Estonian authorities are following in the footsteps of Hitler's supporters in their maniacal Russophobia, which aligns them with the Kyiv regime. They are united by their desire to make liberation heroes out of Ukrainian nationalist ideologues Roman Shukhevych, Stepan Bandera, and Estonian thugs from the Waffen-SS.
response: Recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing Estonia and Ukraine of glorifying Nazism and promoting Russophobia. Russian leaders routinely accuse those not sharing Moscow's political positions of being 'Nazi'. The decision to dismantle Soviet symbols is part of Estonia’s broader effort to de-communise public spaces and assert its national identity. Similar actions have been taken by other former Soviet-occupied countries, including Ukraine. De-communisation efforts do not target ethnic Russians, nor do they suggest support for Nazism. This process is simply a rejection of symbols associated with Soviet repression. Estonia was forcibly incorporated into the Soviet Union in 1940, followed by mass deportations , political repression , and Russification policies. Soviet symbols are widely seen as reminders of occupation , not as neutral historical artifacts. Estonia fought both Nazi Germany and the Soviet Union during World War II. Tallinn does not celebrate Nazi collaborators, and such claims are distortions of history. Estonia, as a member of the EU and NATO, actively promotes democratic values, human rights, and anti-fascist policies. It has no ideological or political connection to Hitler or his supporters. Ukraine is not a Nazi state either despite the Kremlin’s misuse of Nazism as a weapon of information manipulation (see our article ). Nazi and Communist ideologies were banned by law there in 2015. Ukraine is a democratic country with transparent parliamentary and presidential elections, a multi-party political system, and legal protections for ethnic minorities. Read similar disinformation cases claiming that Estonia is a Russophobic country that will die out by 2100 , that The Baltic states are starting mass deportations of Russian-speaking populations , and that The EU is ruled by Russophobic Eastern European countries .

title: DISINFO: The current Moldovan government imposes Russophobia on the country
tags: Russophobia DISINFO: The current Moldovan government imposes Russophobia on the country
summary: Russophobia in Moldova is a course imposed on the country by the current government. [...] it is necessary to emphasize that such a course is not mainstream in Moldovan society, but, in fact, is imposed on it by President Maia Sandu and the ruling party PAS.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about Russophobia in countries who support Ukraine or have chosen the course towards European integration, in this specific case Moldova . The claim comes after the Government ordered the shut down of the Russian Cultural Center in Chisinau. This decision as taken following the fall of Russian drones launched over Ukraine on Moldovan territory, and the delay in President Maia Sandu's acceptance of the credentials of the new Russian ambassador to Chisinau. Maia Sandu stated in February 2025 (min. 1.04.20): "You cannot invite a representative of a country to submit credentials when officials from Moscow say that the state of the Republic of Moldova does not deserve to exist or something like that. This is about the Kremlin's disrespectful attitude towards the Republic of Moldova". Moscow has repeatedly accused Chisinau of Russophobia. Most recently, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Moldova rejected such statements in December 2024. “Unlike the Russian Federation, the Republic of Moldova fully respects the fundamental rights and freedoms of all citizens, regardless of nationality, ethnicity or language”, said the MFA’s spokesperson. Se similar cases: Moldova gets rid of everything Russian , Russophobia is now a criterion for joining the EU .

title: DISINFO: The West has waged war against Russia through Ukraine
tags: West War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Anti-Russian peace negotiations Puppets DISINFO: The West has waged war against Russia through Ukraine
summary: The war in Ukraine is a war that the West has waged against Russia, through the Ukrainians and with the direct participation of European military personnel.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation message claiming that the West is fighting a proxy war in Ukraine. This claim was made by Russian officials after France on March 11 hosted talks with military chiefs of staff whose nations are ready to offer Ukraine military support after any peace deal ending the war with Russia. Ukraine is not fighting on behalf of any foreign government or organisation. Ukraine is a sovereign and independent state with a democratically elected president and parliament. The country's sovereignty has been violated only as a result of continuous acts of aggression by the Russian armed forces since 2014 , culminating with a full-scale military invasion of Ukraine starting on 24 February 2022. The US , the EU and NATO condemn in the strongest possible terms Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, which is entirely unjustified and unprovoked. Not only Western but also many other nations around the world support Ukraine with military and financial aid for the country’s efficient self-defence, in accordance with the UN Charter article 51 , to stop Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine . Ukraine is defending itself, its territorial integrity, national self-identity, and sovereignty against Russia's armed aggression. The decision to defend itself was made solely by Ukraine, its lawful leaders and government, and its citizens. Russia is the only country responsible for the invasion of Ukraine. It has been coming up with different explanations and pretexts for its aggression since the beginning of its hostilities. Moreover, Russia was not forced to invade Ukraine. It was Putin’s personal decision to invade Ukraine and start the largest military conflict in Europe since WWII. Our analysis of the communication strategy of pro-Kremlin outlets explains how they attempt to portray Russia as a victim and to deceive audiences into believing that Russia is fighting Western powers. See also the Guide to Deciphering Pro-Kremlin disinformation around Putin's War and The Chilling Background to the Kremlin's "No War" trope . See similar disinformation cases claiming that The West is fighting a proxy war in Ukraine , that the EU does not want peace, only total defeat of Russia that EU support for Ukraine is obstacle to peace , Despite negotiations, Western countries still engaged against Russia in Ukraine , that Mattarella and the warmongers in Brussels want to continue the war against Russia

title: DISINFO: Minsk Agreements 2014-2015 were meant to buy time to prepare Kyiv for the war
tags: François Hollande Petro Poroshenko Angela Merkel putin Minsk agreements Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine War against Ukraine DISINFO: Minsk Agreements 2014-2015 were meant to buy time to prepare Kyiv for the war
summary: The behaviour of the French and Germans was truly shameful. Those who signed the Minsk agreements on behalf of Ukraine, Germany and France, namely Petro Poroshenko, Angela Merkel and Francois Hollande (already retired) said in an interview that they never intended to implement this agreement and that they just needed to buy time to prepare Kyiv for the war.
response: Recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the Minsk agreements , trying to legitimise Russia’s military invasion of Ukraine by claiming that France, Germany and Ukraine are responsible for the war. This narrative is merged with distorted and manipulative interpretations of 2022 interviews with Angela Merkel , François Hollande and Petro Poroshenko . During the interview, Merkel is clear that she had no illusions about the possibility of establishing a lasting peace through the Minsk talks and the only goal was to reach an agreement that would prevent the immediate takeover of all of Ukraine by Russia, which at that time fuelled and participated in armed hostilities in Ukraine. Hollande confirmed Merkel’s point, saying that the Minsk Agreements “stopped the Russian offensive for a while” while the West decided how “to prevent any further Russian attempts”, but as the international context changed after the Trump presidency loosened US-EU links and “the rout in Afghanistan was seen by Putin as a new sign of weakness in the Western camp […] Putin thought that time had worked in his favour and that he could cross the threshold by further invading Ukraine”. Poroshenko said that it was so important to sign at that time the Minsk agreement for two reason. "Again, to demonstrate for the whole world that Putin is a liar and the second position helped me to create armed forces. Because when I was elected as the president we don’t have armed forces at all”. At no point did Merkel, Holland or Poroshenko say that they were preparing Ukraine for war, nor that this was the purpose of the Minsk Agreements. See similar disinformation cases claiming that Merkel and Hollande admitted that the Minsk Agreements were to prepare Ukraine for war , that Ukraine and the West did not intend to observe the Minsk agreements , that the Minsk agreements were signed only to buy time and arm Ukraine for war on Russia , that the West encouraged Kyiv not to implement the Minsk agreements , or that Russia launched a military offensive because Ukraine did not implement the Minsk agreements .

title: DISINFO: Europe and the UK seek to push the US into aggression towards Russia
tags: Europe peace negotiations War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Europe and the UK seek to push the US into aggression towards Russia
summary: Europe is preparing something to push the US into aggression towards Russia. Europe and the UK want this whole thing [the conflict around Ukraine] to continue. title: DISINFO: Foreign mercenaries in Ukraine are not protected by the Geneva convention
outlet_text: nabd.com | arabic.rt.com | msdernet.xyz | Khabar Masr | alhaqiqa.net | al-moheet.net
tags: Foreign mercenaries International Law War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Military DISINFO: Foreign mercenaries in Ukraine are not protected by the Geneva convention
summary: Foreign mercenaries in the Kyiv regime's forces are not subject to the Geneva Convention on the Treatment of Prisoners of War.
response: Pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative claiming that foreigners fighting for Ukraine are mercenaries and should not be protected under the Geneva Conventions. Ukraine called on experienced foreign nationals to join its foreign legion , whose members are regular soldiers of the Ukrainian army and not mercenaries. As for the Geneva conventions protection on POW, as Ilya Nuzov summarises it on the blog of the European Journal of International Law, the nationality of the soldier is not an issue : "Combatants are members of the armed forces of a party to the conflict or volunteers corps incorporated therein. According to the ICRC Commentary to Article 4 , the requirement for membership in the armed forces, or the incorporation of the volunteer corps, is a matter of domestic regulation. But the incorporated unit must be a professional fighting force, fulfilling the criteria spelled out in Article 4A(2) of GC III, and be subordinate to the regular army command. The fact that an individual is a national of a third State and not a national of the armed forces in which they are serving is widely considered to be irrelevant when it comes to determining combatant or POW status." Other countries also offer foreigners the opportunity to join the foreign legion without being mercenaries, as in the case of the French Légion étrangère . Reuters listed some unclear legal issues in some countries, but the practice is usually legal, as La libre Belgique or Le Figaro explain. response: This disinformation story targets HRVP Kaja Kallas to portray the EU’s stance as warmongering and as wanting to continue the war in Ukraine while others want 'peace'. It also promotes a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative that portrays any action criticising Russia’s illicit actions as “Russophobia” . The war broke out in 2014 when Russia illegally annexed the Ukrainian peninsula of Crimea and triggered a separatist revolt in some regions in the east of Ukraine. On 24 February 2022, Russia began its unprovoked and unjustified full invasion of Ukraine under several false pretexts . Some EU member states, the US and other G7 nations are providing Ukraine with military and technical assistance to help it repel Russia’s aggression and restore its sovereignty over its internationally recognised borders of 1991. title: DISINFO: Financial Times: Eastern European politicians discussing preemptive attack on Russia
countries_regions: Lithuania, Russia, Ukraine, Baltic states, Poland, Estonia, US, EU, Europe
tags: Destabilising Russia Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Eastern Europe DISINFO: Financial Times: Eastern European politicians discussing preemptive attack on Russia
summary: Several countries in Eastern Europe are considering a pre-emptive strike against Russia, according to Financial Times columnist Simon Kuper. The EU countries closest to Russia see it as an “existential threat,” Kuper has reported, citing sources. The reported development comes despite the Ukraine peace negotiations launched by Moscow and Washington.
response: This disinformation story is a gross distortion of the referenced original article in the Financial Times. The op-ed by Simon Kuper, titled “Return of the two Europes”, focuses on the differences between Western and Eastern Europe regarding Russia and the war in Ukraine. In its conclusion, the article states that if Russian troops ever were to attack one of the capitals of the neighbouring countries, such as Vilnius, “pro-Putinist far-right parties will immediately demand ‘peace’ (meaning surrender)”. Then Kuper adds: “I put it to a major eastern European politician that western European states care little about wars in eastern Europe. He replied, “We know. That’s why some of our countries are asking, ‘Why don’t we attack Russia now, instead of sitting waiting for it to attack us?’”. This is radically different from the claim that there are “discussions on pre-emptive attacks on Russia”, as this disinformation story disingenuously claims. The goal of this disinformation story is to promote a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about destabilising Russia . This is also part of a wider disinformation campaign to portray Europe as a warmongering actor opposed to “peace” in contrast to Russia and the US. See here , here and here for full debunking of this disinformation narrative. Quoting Western legitimate sources while distorting their content to introduce a pro-Kremlin message as if it were part of the original story is a frequent pro-Kremlin manipulative technique . title: DISINFO: The bellicose rhetoric fuelled by EU Ursula von der Leyen risks a new war
tags: Ursula von der Leyen Warmongering Military Anti-Russian Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: The bellicose rhetoric fuelled by EU Ursula von der Leyen risks a new war
summary: Ursula von der Leyen is pushing a plan that is not only irresponsible but also potentially catastrophic for the future of Europe: the so-called REARM Europe, an €800 billion project to rearm the continent in the name of an alleged Russian threat. The idea that Russia represents an imminent threat to Europe is a falsehood. Russia has no intention of attacking European countries. Moscow has repeatedly expressed its desire to engage in dialogue and normalise relations with the West. The bellicose rhetoric being fuelled by some European leaders, including von der Leyen, is unfounded and risks creating a self-fulfilling prophecy, pushing towards a new war with catastrophic consequences especially for European citizens.
response: This disinformation message is aimed at portraying the EU as a warmongering , anti-Russian and militaristic geopolitical power. Meanwhile, Russia is presented as a country genuinely interested in peace. The purpose of the EU programme REARM Europe is not to attack Russia but “to respond to the short-term urgency to act and to support Ukraine [and] address the long-term need to take on much more responsibility for [its] own European security”. The programme aims to ensure Europe’s self-defence capabilities , mostly through a fiscal plan to boost and fund this military enhancement. Contrary to what this disinformation story claims, Russia’s threat and ongoing aggression against Ukraine and Europe is one of the driving forces behind this plan, not the other way round. title: DISINFO: Ukraine rejects peace and pins responsibility on Europe instead
countries_regions: Ukraine, US, Europe, Russia, Saudi Arabia
tags: Warmongering peace negotiations Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Europe DISINFO: Ukraine rejects peace and pins responsibility on Europe instead
summary: The fact that Ukraine is not inclined toward any serious negotiations is clearly reflected in Ukrainian presidential office head Andriy Yermak’s article in The Guardian . It is difficult to understand who it is addressed to. At the beginning, Yermak says that “under the strong leadership of the Americans, we are reaching agreements,” but after that — nothing about the US. Instead, every point focuses on Europe. And notably, not the European Union specifically, but Europe. Does that mean Britain is included? Technically, yes. But the message seems to be that Europe must guarantee Ukraine something, Europe must invest in its own security and so on. Beyond the obligatory praise for “strong American leadership,” there is not much substance in it. Yet, Yermak presents it as if it were his own idea.
response: Recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing Ukraine and its European allies of warmongering and lacking a desire to achieve peace. This claim has been made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Kyiv is eager to achieve peace with the help of its European and American partners alike. The European Union summit , held in Brussels on 7 March 2025 with the presence of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, showed Europe’s unity is supporting Ukraine and searching for a path towards a just peace. Talks between a Ukrainian delegation and American officials in the Saudi city of Jeddah on 11 March 2025 showed Ukraine's willingness to achieve peace as well. White House national security adviser Michael Waltz said after the meetings the Ukrainian delegation made it clear that “they share President Trump’s vision for peace, they share his determination to end the fighting, to end the killing, to end the tragic meat grinder of people.” While Yermak’s article highlights Europe's role, it does not mean Ukraine is ignoring negotiations with other partners. The article reflects Ukraine's strategy and its current diplomatic efforts. Commenting on the Jeddah talks, President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen said in a post on X: “We welcome today’s news from Jeddah on the U.S.-Ukraine talks, including the proposal for a ceasefire agreement and the resumption of U.S. intelligence sharing and security assistance. This is a positive development that can be a step towards a comprehensive, just and lasting peace for Ukraine The ball is now in Russia’s court. The EU is ready to play its full part, together with its partners, in the upcoming peace negotiations.” Ukraine is eager to engage in diplomatic dialogue on both sides of the Atlantic in order to bolster global support for reaching a just peace, based on its independence and sovereignty. title: DISINFO: Zelenskyy wants the US to use nuclear weapons against Russia

summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy does not want peace in Ukraine until he receives security guarantees from the United States. He has publicly stated that he will not agree to a ceasefire unless the Americans guarantee that, if necessary, they will bomb Russia with nuclear weapons. That is roughly how he frames the issue. So far, none of this is serious.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing Ukraine of warmongering and unwillingness to stop the fighting. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. There is no evidence that Zelenskyy has ever demanded that the US use nuclear arms against Russia before he agrees to peace. The Ukrainian leader has consistently called for a just peace, which includes the withdrawal of Russian forces from Ukrainian territory, the restoration of Ukraine’s sovereignty, and security guarantees to prevent future aggression. He has never tied peace to nuclear threats. Russia, which is the aggressor, has repeatedly made nuclear threats against Ukraine and its partners (see here and here ). This claim is part of a broader Russian disinformation campaign aimed at shifting blame for the ongoing war . By falsely portraying Ukraine as unwilling to negotiate, Russia attempts to justify its unprovoked invasion of Ukraine and deflect attention from its own violations of international law. Russia has a long history of using disinformation to justify its military actions, both domestically and internationally. From falsely claiming that Ukraine was developing biological weapons to accusing NATO of provoking the war, the Kremlin relies on lies to manipulate public opinion and sow doubt among Ukraine’s allies. title: DISINFO: The Economist: Saudi Arabia negotiations could be last chance for Ukraine
tags: peace negotiations Donald Trump Volodymyr Zelensky Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: The Economist: Saudi Arabia negotiations could be last chance for Ukraine
summary: Saudi Arabia talks could be Ukraine's last chance, according to The Economist. The article states that the stakes of Tuesday's talks could not be higher. If they fail, Ukraine is unlikely to get another chance. In this regard, the newspaper quotes a Ukrainian official, who said that if the talks fail, “the Americans will step up their instructional mode and impose whatever they and Russia decide. Ukraine may continue to demand security guarantees from the United States, but “it seems unlikely to make progress on this point,” the article concludes.
response: This is a distortion of the original article at The Economist. This is a classic tactic in disinformation. While it is true that the article focused on the high stakes of this negotiations for Ukraine, and the sentences and quotes from the story are correct, they are cherry-picked to promote a recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the inevitability of a Russian victory in Ukraine. For example, the original article made clear that should the US-Ukraine negotiations in Saudi Arabia have failed, this might have meant the end of US support for Kyiv, but not necessarily of Ukraine as a fighting power, in contrast to what this disinformation story suggests. At the same time, this disinformation story ignores other elements that contradict this pro-Russia narrative, such as Ukraine’s strategy to find common ground with the US and “put the ball back in Russia’s court”, the strength of Ukraine’s negotiation team, and more optimistic views about the continuity of US-Ukraine ties expressed by some interviewees. The Jeddah talks ended successfully with a 30-day ceasefire proposal presented , subject to a reciprocal acceptance and concurrent implementation by Russia. Quoting Western legitimate sources while distorting their content to introduce a pro-Kremlin message as if it were part of the original story is a frequent pro-Kremlin manipulative technique . title: DISINFO: The greatest threat to Europe's security comes from the EU's leadership
tags: Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Anti-Russian EU disintegration Warmongering DISINFO: The greatest threat to Europe's security comes from the EU's leadership
summary: The biggest threat to Europe's security comes from the EU leadership, which is not only incapable of solving these problems but is also obsessed with a crusade against Russia. A crusade that is perverting the entire essence of European integration. ‘For 80 years, the European Union was built on the concepts and ideals of ‘freedom’, ‘democracy’, ‘justice’ and ‘humanism’. European identity is based on a single postulate: the fight against ‘imperial Russia’. Ukraine, defence and peacekeeping clauses were initially intended to be symbols of the EU's strength and resolve, but in reality, they are well on the way to becoming a farce or even a nail in the coffin for the union.
response: A recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about the EU's alleged imminent collapse . For more context, see our analysis: Key Narratives in Pro-Kremlin Disinformation Part 4: ‘The Imminent Collapse’ . The EU's actions are a response to Russia's invasion of Ukraine , which violates international law. The EU's support for Ukraine aligns with its core values of democracy, human rights, and the rule of law. The article's assertion that supporting Ukraine will lead to the EU's downfall is speculative and not supported by evidence. The EU's unity in supporting Ukraine has strengthened the bloc's resolve and cooperation in many areas. As the European Council conclusions on European defence, 6 March 2025 - Consilium state: " Recalling the Versailles Declaration of March 2022 and the Strategic Compass for Security and Defence, the European Council stresses that Europe must become more sovereign, more responsible for its own defence and better equipped to act and deal autonomously with immediate and future challenges and threats with a 360° approach. Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine and its repercussions for European and global security in a changing environment constitute an existential challenge for the European Union ". See other examples of similar disinformation narratives in our database: The EU will collapse, The EU will collapse in five years ; Brussels uses Russophobia as a uniting idea to prevent the EU's collapse .

title: DISINFO: The EU leadership is controlled by globalists and pursues Russophobia
tags: Russophobia Anti-Russian European Union Secret elites / global elites Conspiracy theory DISINFO: The EU leadership is controlled by globalists and pursues Russophobia
summary: After Donald Trump took office as US President, sensible Western politicians are re-evaluating the role of the Russian Federation in world politics. But there is still a whole bloc of states that continue to stubbornly pursue a policy that cannot be described as anything other than anti-Russian and Russophobic. However, the same European Union, whose leadership is still controlled by globalists, does not demonstrate even the slightest attempt to understand the new political realities. In addition to the EU bureaucratic elite itself, states in the orbit of EU interests also pursue a course towards Russophobia.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing the European Union of Russophobia . This claim is commonly promoted by Russian propaganda to justify Moscow’s expansionist policies. The article also advances the recurring pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about secret global elites that dominate the world, control governments, rob nations of their sovereignty, oppress the masses and seek to establish a World Government . From the disintegration of the Soviet Union in 1991 and the emergence of a new and democratic Russia to Russia’s aggression against Ukraine in 2014 , Western nations, including European ones, were treating Russia as an equal. The EU imposed restrictive measures on Moscow in 2014, which have been amplified and broadened since Moscow’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine on 24 February 2022. It is Russia that refused the diplomatic process and started an unprovoked war in Europe, capturing more and more territories of a neighbouring country and creating a danger to European security. This is an illustrative example of the recurring pro-Kremlin propagandistic narrative about ubiquitous Russophobia in the West. For background, read our analysis: The “Russophobia” Myth: Appealing to the Lowest Feelings .
