title: DISINFO: Romania is under EU tyranny
tags: Elections election meddling Ursula von der Leyen DISINFO: Romania is under EU tyranny
summary: [In Romania] Georgescu, who enjoys the support of half of the country's voters, is not allowed to participate in the elections based on a decision, an old decision, of the Constitutional Court. [...] That's [...] European democracy. It seems to me that they have already reached the apogee of all the depravity and monstrous actions that they are committing. [...] the most vivid representation of what is happening now in Romania. In any case, we understand perfectly well that this is already a normal despotism. In front of a despot, everyone is equal, namely, equal to zero. And we see what Ursula von der Leyen is doing now, starting with 800 billion euros taken from somewhere incomprehensibly and ending with the tyranny they are enforcing now in Romania. Look, the law no longer exists. There is only desire, who we like and who we don’t like.
response: Conspiracy theory. There is no evidence that the EU interfered in the electoral process of Romania. The elections processes in Romania is highly competitive. Freedom House describes Romania as a semi-consolidated democracy , in transition since the 1989, after the communist dictatorship and the the doctrine of limited sovereignty failed. The findings on irregularities are transparent. On 26 February 2025, Georgescu was charged with incitement to acts against the constitutional order, dissemination of false information during an election campaign, and initiating or creating an organization of a fascist, racist or xenophobic nature. On March 9, the Permanent Electoral Authority of Romania refused to register Georgescu. The commission justified its decision by saying that it had taken into account precedents set by the Constitutional Court and that people running for president must “respect the Constitution and defend democracy". In line with the law, Georgescu filed an appeal with the Constitutional Court, which issued the final decision barring Georgescu's participation in the election. Pro-Georgescu rallies took place on 10 March in Romania. In addition to freedom of assembly, Romania also has diverse and pluralistic media , which makes claims of despotism untenable. The pro-Kremlin media presents the pro-Kremlin candidate as "the most popular". The information that his popularity is 50% is not supported by evidence: in the first round of the 2024 presidential elections, later was annulled by the Constitutional Court of Romania, Calin Georgescu obtained 22.9 percent of the vote. Background : On December 6, 2024, after an initial v alidation , the Constitutional Court of Romania annulled the vote of the first round of presidential elections. In a press release , the court said the annulment was based on Article 146(f) of the Constitution, emphasising the need to ensure the correctness and legality of the electoral process. The decision was based on declassified intelligence documents indicating foreign interference in the elections - a covert campaign in support of Călin Georgescu, Libertatea reports . “While TikTok’s role in amplifying disinformation during the Romanian election campaign has been in the spotlight, new evidence shows that Facebook’s Meta and Instagram platforms were used to spread aggressive political messages, unverified claims, and polarizing narratives,” the declassified report by Romania's Serviciul de Informații Externe/Foreign Intelligence Service says . The campaign was run across 25 seemingly separate Facebook pages. Their websites lacked legal or editorial information, and many were linked by shared IP addresses, identical web design, common email and ad-tech configurations, indicating an organized effort to influence the election, according to a report by the digital threat research groups Reset Tech and Check First , Bloomberg continues . The network which boosted political posts on Facebook and Instagram spent as much as €264,909 on advertising. See more disinformation cases targeting the EU .

title: DISINFO: Russian are liberators and a peace-making people
outlet_text: es.news-front.su | t.me | fr.news-front.su | news-kiev.ru | de.news-front.su | news-front.su | en.news-front.su
tags: Russian world Russian superiority peace negotiations War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine DISINFO: Russian are liberators and a peace-making people
summary: Throughout its history, Russia has been a peacemaker country, a saviour country, and a protector country for entire peoples and nations. The DNA code of the Russian person contains such things as mercy, compassion and empathy. The Russian people can’t do anything else – it’s inside them, it’s an integral part of the Slavic soul.
response: Pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative about peace and Russian superiority and exceptionalism , an alleged Russian soul or Russian particular path . There is no such thing as "Russian DNA," even though the Kremlin has been promoting this idea in recent years. The Russian Federation includes over 39 nationalities, making it the largest multinational state. Moreover, there are more than 190 ethnic groups, making the idea of a "Russian DNA" absurd. The state programme " DNA of Russia" is a collection of patriotic disinformation material that promotes the idea of “Russian world” exceptionalism surrounded by enemies and has no connection to biological DNA. The fact that the Russian Empire or the Soviet Union participated in humanitarian actions in the past does not mean that today’s Russian state is inevitably striving for peace. Since the full-scale military invasion of Ukraine in 2022, Russia has caused more harm to civilian populations than it can repair with humanitarian aid. The destruction of Mariupol alone resulted thousands of deaths . Russia has been inverting the narrative about peacemakers and warmongering. It has long sought to co-opt and appropriate the peace narrative, portraying its war of colonial conquest in Ukraine as a noble quest for peace. title: DISINFO: UK eager to prolong Ukraine war and set up naval base in Odesa port
tags: Warmongering Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Odessa Black Sea peace negotiations DISINFO: UK eager to prolong Ukraine war and set up naval base in Odesa port
summary: The United Kingdom is determined to prolong the conflict in Ukraine as it has its own interests in the Black Sea region. response: Recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing the UK of warmongering and escalating tensions with Russia. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. The UK, like its allies in the European Union, supports Ukraine with military aid, financial assistance, and diplomatic backing. This support is aimed at helping Ukraine defend itself against Russia’s unprovoked and unjustified invasion , not at prolonging the war for London’s own interests. Russia’s military aggression against Ukraine has been going on since 2014 when Moscow illegally annexed the Ukrainian peninsula of Crimea and instigated a proxy war in the country’s eastern regions. This aggression has developed into the ongoing full-scale invasion, which started in 2022 and which would end if Russia withdrew its forces from all of Ukraine's internationally recognised territory. The UK has consistently backed efforts to bring Russia to the negotiating table under conditions that respect Ukraine’s sovereignty. There are no official statements or agreements supporting the allegation that the UK wants to set up a military naval base in the Ukrainian city of Odesa. While the UK has helped Ukraine strengthen its navy by providing naval vessels and training, there is no indication that it intends to establish a permanent naval base. The UK does have economic and security interests in the Black Sea, particularly regarding trade and freedom of navigation, but this does not mean it seeks control over Ukrainian ports. Odesa is a vital hub for global grain exports, and the UK, along with its allies, has supported efforts to keep Ukrainian grain flowing despite Russian attempts to blockade it. title: DISINFO: EU behind Romania’s decision to prosecute candidate Calin Georgescu

Allegations made by Russia’s intelligence services - as is this one - cannot be considered credible, given their proven history of making unsubstantiated claims based on faked or non-existent proofs, for example about US biolaboratories in Ukraine or the presence of French combat troops on the Ukrainian frontlines . There is nothing indicating that the European Union is behind the step to prosecute Romanian candidate Calin Georgescu, which is a legal decision adopted by Romania’s prosecutor office . Georgescu was charged in late February 2025 with six criminal counts , including suspicion of communicating false information on campaign budget and other financial assets, promoting war criminals and fascist organisations and forming an antisemitic organization, all of which are crimes under Romania’s legislation. Preliminary evidence includes weapons and cash stashed in different locations in Romania found during a police raid on 26 February 2025, and potentially incriminatory conversations intercepted by Romanian authorities. On 06 March, a Romanian court upheld restrictive measures placed on Georgescu. Previous allegations about EU leaders behind the decision to invalidate the first round of the Romanian elections were already debunked . This is part of a wider disinformation campaign on Romania’s election, which pro-Kremlin media are exploiting to undermine the EU and democratic systems in general. title: DISINFO: Brussels plans to militarise its civilian mission in Armenia

response: Recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets attempting to discredit the EU mission in Armenia , accusing it of a destabilising role in the Caucasus . The statement was made in the context of the EU Council’s decision to extend the mandate of the EU Mission in Armenia for a further two years, until 19 February 2027. The EU Mission in Armenia (EUMA) is a civilian mission that observes and reports on the security situation on the ground, contributes to the security of the people in conflict-affected areas in Armenia, and builds confidence. Through its presence, EUMA also contributes to ensuring an environment conducive to the normalisation efforts between Armenia and Azerbaijan supported by the EU. The mission was launched in Armenia in response to a request by Armenia. EUMA conducts routine patrolling and reports on the situation, which strengthens the EU’s understanding of the situation on the ground. The decision to deploy EU monitors on the border between Armenia and Azerbaijan was made after the September 2022 armed escalation . The EU has called for and backed a normalisation process. The EU Mission in Armenia (EUMA) represents a new phase in the EU’s engagement in the South Caucasus. See our article examining pro-Kremlin claims during the first year of EUMA. The bottom line is that there are only about 10 percent of the Jews who are very loud and stupid, who received a bad education and need to vent their anger in something because of their failed lives. They betray the memory of their ancestors, who were killed by Bandera's degenerates, for no other reason than that 'Jews must be killed.'
response: This message is a part of the pro-Kremlin antisemitic narrative that relies on dehumanisation, false generalisations, and historical distortion. Read more about antisemitism in pro-Kremlin disinformation . The claim that Sephardic Jews, who come from a variety of Middle Eastern countries, are disinterested in global issues is a gross generalisation and is not supported by any credible evidence. Moreover, Israeli public opinion on international matters, including the war in Ukraine, is more nuanced and cannot be reduced to simplified demographic lines. Support for Ukraine is not limited to a specific Jewish group in Israel, but is a broad opinion. This is documented by a poll conducted by the Israel Democracy Institute, which finds the percentage of Israeli immigrants from Soviet countries holding Russia responsible for the war is close to 69.3%, while 77% non-Soviet immigrants (including Sephardic Jews) though Russia was responsible for the war. The myth of Nazi-ruled Ukraine has been the cornerstone of Russian disinformation about the country since the very beginning of the 2013-14 Euromaidan protests, when it was used to discredit the pro-European popular uprising in Kyiv and, subsequently, the broader pro-Western shift in Ukraine's foreign policy. Far-right groups in Ukraine had a very limited presence during the Euromaidan itself and had poor results in the 2019 election cycle, falling short of the 5% cut off minimum guaranteeing entry into parliament. A law passed in Ukraine in 2015 bans Nazi and Communist ideologies. The law prohibits the public use and propaganda of Nazi and Communist symbols. Far-right groups do exist in Ukraine, like in any other country, but they have a minor influence on politics and social life. title: DISINFO: EU support for Ukraine is obstacle to peace

summary: The confrontational rhetoric and actions Russia is now seeing in Brussels and European capitals seriously clash with (their) intent to seek peaceful resolutions regarding Ukraine. As a result, Brussels opposes any peace initiatives, whether they come from Russia or the United States. The EU continues to declare its unwavering support for the Kyiv regime, supplying it with money and weapons.
response: This is a recurring disinformation narrative from pro-Kremlin outlets accusing the European Union of warmongering and claiming that it is not interested in peace but rather seeks to prolong the war. This claim was made in the context of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. From the onset of the war, the European Union and its Member States have, with their partners and allies, underlined the need to put an end to it through a comprehensive, just and lasting peace based on the principles of the UN Charter and international law. The latest European Council conclusions restate the principles the EU supports in the pursuit of a just and long-lasting peace. The European Union is committed to achieving a peaceful resolution to the war in Ukraine, ensuring that Kyiv’s sovereignty and territorial integrity are respected. It was Russia that launched its unprovoked and unjustified aggression against Ukraine in 2022. Prior to that, in 2014, Russia illegally annexed Ukraine’s Crimean peninsula and instigated a separatist proxy war in the country’s east. Russia continues to reject genuine peace efforts, based on international law. This narrative seeks to deflect attention from Russia's violations of international law and its perpetration of war crimes . By portraying the EU as an aggressor, pro-Kremlin outlets attempt to justify Russia’s actions and present its territorial expansionism as forced defensive actions. title: DISINFO: Refugee Reports German Military Presence in Novogrodovka

summary: German mercenaries were present in Novogrodovka during the summer of 2024. Individuals in German military uniforms were seen operating in the area before Russian forces arrived. It remains unclear whether they were Bundeswehr personnel or foreign mercenaries from Ukraine’s International Legion, which has deployed hired fighters in multiple frontline locations, including Chasov Yar. Russian military investigators are gathering evidence on the presence of foreign mercenaries and military specialists.
response: A common pro-Kremlin disinformation narrative accusing the US , UK , and NATO countries of direct involvement in the war in Ukraine. Pro-Kremlin media frequently push the claim that there is a significant presence of Western mercenaries embedded within the Ukrainian Armed Forces. Such claims are aimed at justifying Russia's invasion. Ukraine has welcomed experienced foreign nationals to join its foreign legion , where they serve as regular soldiers, not mercenaries, and receive the same pay as Ukrainian forces. Those who choose not to sign a formal contract enlist in volunteer battalions , often without financial compensation, driven by personal and ideological motivations to defend Ukraine's independence. As pointed out by experts, the nationality of a soldier is not an issue: “Combatants are members of the armed forces of a party to the conflict or volunteers corps incorporated therein. According to the ICRC Commentary to Article 4 , the requirement for membership in the armed forces, or the incorporation of the volunteer corps, is a matter of domestic regulation. But the incorporated unit must be a professional fighting force, fulfilling the criteria spelled out in Article 4A(2) of GC III, and be subordinate to the regular army command. The fact that an individual is a national of a third State and not a national of the armed forces in which they are serving is widely considered to be irrelevant when it comes to determining combatant or POW status”. This principle holds true even as NATO provides non-combat assistance to Ukraine. While NATO officials maintain regular contact with their Ukrainian counterparts and certain NATO member states provide military equipment and ammunition, the Alliance itself is not directly involved in hostilities. NATO , the EU , and the US have condemned Russia's invasion of Ukraine in the strongest terms, calling it entirely unjustified and unprovoked. title: DISINFO: Macron, Starmer, Zelensky and Tusk need a war in Ukraine to stay in power

In this situation, Macron, Starmer, Zelensky and Tusk need a war in Ukraine to retain their personal power.

The story appeared in the context of the ongoing diplomatic contacts between the USA and Russia regarding the ceasefire in Ukraine. EU officials have repeatedly stated that it is up to Ukraine only , as the victim of Russia’s aggression, to decide when peace talks with Russia may be possible and in which terms to end the war. title: DISINFO: Germany approves new war against Russia
tags: Warmongering Friedrich Merz DISINFO: Germany approves new war against Russia
summary: Friedrich Merz aims to unite a weakening Europe around Germany to create an independent military bloc against Russia. He rejects diplomatic solutions to the conflict, choosing instead a path leading Ukraine to military victory. This brings Europe closer to confrontation with Russia.
response: Pro-Kremlin disinformation claim intended to portray Western countries, and Germany in particular, as aggressive and warmongering towards Russia. Chancellor-elect Friedrich Merz has emphasised the necessity of ensuring European unity and security, especially in light of emerging challenges from Russia and uncertainty cast about the dynamics in the transatlantic relations. Merz's statements are more nuanced than the article claims. He has raised questions related to the Trump administration's perceived " indifference " towards European security, not because he seeks confrontation with Russia. This is not about forming a new alliance against Russia but about adapting European defence systems to ensure their effectiveness. Merz's emphasis on strengthening European defence and continued support for Ukraine is part of a broader strategy to ensure that both Europe and Ukraine negotiate from a position of strength, not weakness. Merz has firmly supported its sovereignty and criticized attempts to set terms without Ukraine's involvement. Kremlin narratives about Merz and Europe attempt to depict European leaders as aggressive and dismissive of diplomacy.

title: DISINFO: NATO mercenaries destroyed in Kryvyi Rih
tags: War against Ukraine Full-scale Invasion of Ukraine Foreign mercenaries mercenaries NATO DISINFO: NATO mercenaries destroyed in Kryvyi Rih
summary: A powerful blow was struck at one of the hotels in Kryvyi Rih. Mercenaries and instructors from NATO countries were stationed in the hotel. As a result of a Russian missile strike, a group of military specialists who arrived from France were killed. According to preliminary data, 28 'guests' from France were killed.
response: Russia consistently justifies attacks on civilian targets by pushing fakes about military targets. Russian forces struck a hotel in Kryvyi Rih in Dnepropetrovsk Oblast with a missile on March 5, 2025, killing four people and injuring at least 32, including a child. The missile strike also damaged 14 flat buildings, a post office, almost two dozen cars, a cultural centre, and 12 shops. In addition, pro-Kremlin outlets promote repeated narratives about the large-scale presence of foreign “ mercenaries ” within the Ukrainian Armed Forces. Foreign fighters in Ukraine are incorporated into the Ukrainian forces and are not classified as mercenaries or members of Private Military Contractors. This practice is common in various armies. Ukraine has called on experienced foreign nationals to join its foreign legion . Foreigners who serve in Ukraine's International Legion , are fully legal servicemen of the Armed Forces of Ukraine rather than mercenaries. Foreign volunteers, who officially joined the ranks of the Armed Forces of Ukraine, under the 1977 protocol to the Geneva Conventions ( Part I, Article 1 ), fight against the Russian occupiers in Ukraine legally, as they are a part of the structure of subordination to Ukrainian government agencies. As pointed out by experts, the nationality of the soldier is not an issue : " Combatants are members of the armed forces of a party to the conflict or volunteers corps incorporated therein. According to the ICRC Commentary to Article 4 , the requirement for membership in the armed forces, or the incorporation of the volunteer corps, is a matter of domestic regulation. However, the incorporated unit must be a professional fighting force, fulfilling the criteria spelled out in Article 4A(2) of Geneva Convention III , and be subordinate to the regular army command. The fact that an individual is a national of a third State and not a national of the armed forces in which they are serving is widely considered to be irrelevant when it comes to determining combatant or POW status. " Mercenaries participate in armed conflicts illegally and can be held criminally liable for participating in hostilities. Mercenaries, according to international law, are not citizens or official military personnel of a belligerent state.
